title: fix: adjust dlm time format
summary: Fixed DLM (Data Lifecycle Manager) time format configuration in CloudFormation template and deployment script. The changes address CloudFormation parameter handling in AWS CLI deployments and ensure proper time format specification for database backups.
userImpact: DLM backups are now configured with the correct time format, resolving deployment issues related to CloudFormation parameter defaults.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2583: Add pin in deep dive tag
summary: Added a new "pin" tag option to the deep dive schema definitions. This enhancement aligns with related changes in the arthur-engine and frontend to enable users to pin important items in deep dive analysis views.
userImpact: Users can now pin items in deep dive tag views for better organization and focus on critical analysis results.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2203 data plane version safety
summary: Adds exception handling to the data plane's job agent to gracefully handle job specs with unknown or unsupported types. When a job spec cannot be parsed due to a version mismatch, the job is now marked as failed with a clear error message instructing users to upgrade their data plane to support the feature.
userImpact: Users receive clear guidance when submitting jobs with features unsupported by their data plane version, rather than encountering cryptic parsing errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: pass default values to backup stacks and timescale
summary: Fixed a bug where default values were not being passed to backup stacks and TimescaleDB during database stack deployment. This ensures consistent configuration and prevents deployment failures due to missing required parameters.
userImpact: Database stack deployments now correctly apply default values to backup stacks and TimescaleDB, preventing configuration errors and deployment failures.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2406 alert rule query validation api
summary: Introduces a new API endpoint for validating alert rule queries before creation. The endpoint leverages the existing SQL parser and validation logic in the backend to check query structure and template requirements, allowing the frontend to provide real-time validation feedback to users.
userImpact: Users can now validate alert rule queries against backend validation rules without creating the alert, enabling early error detection and a better authoring experience.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: do not fail if deploy tag already exists
summary: The Create-Deployment-Tag job was failing when a deploy tag already existed, which occurs when there are no recent commits or releases to deploy. The job has been updated to handle pre-existing tags gracefully, treating this condition as expected rather than an error.
userImpact: Deployment pipelines no longer fail when the deploy tag already exists, allowing CI/CD workflows to complete successfully in scenarios with no new releases.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2553 add tags and backups support
summary: Added backup and tagging capabilities for TSDB infrastructure via a new CloudFormation stack and enhanced deployment scripts. The implementation includes automated snapshot scheduling, retention policies, and resource tagging to support disaster recovery and backup management workflows.
userImpact: Users can now configure automated backups for TimescaleDB with customizable snapshot schedules, retention counts, and backup tags via environment variables.
author: kacperek0

title: chore(deps): update dependency pytest-asyncio to v1
summary: Updated pytest-asyncio from v0.26.0 to v1.0.0. This is a major version bump that removes the deprecated event_loop fixture, improves event loop scoping efficiency, and adds Python 3.14 support. The upgrade is low-risk as it only affects test infrastructure and existing tests pass.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update dependency cachetools to v6
summary: Updated cachetools dependency from v5.5.2 to v6.0.0. This is a major version upgrade that requires Python 3.9 or later, removes MRUCache and @func.mru_cache decorator, adds optional condition parameter to @cached and @cachedmethod decorators to address cache stampede issues, and converts cachetools.func decorators to use threading.Condition.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance to address cache stampede handling and compatibility requirements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated 12 non-major dependencies across development and runtime environments, including patch updates to boto3, fsspec, gcsfs, moto, protobuf, redis, and s3fs; and minor updates to google-cloud-bigquery, mypy, pytest-xdist, and redis. This keeps the project's dependencies current with security patches and minor improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates four non-major dependencies to their latest versions: authlib (1.5.2 → 1.6.0) with OAuth2/OIDC improvements, boto3 (1.38.21 → 1.38.22) with AWS API updates, pydantic (2.11.4 → 2.11.5), and tokencost (0.1.21 → 0.1.22). These updates bring bug fixes, new features, and maintenance improvements to the project's dependency stack.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved OAuth2/OIDC support, updated AWS service integrations, and bug fixes in core validation and token cost calculation libraries.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Dependencies updated across the codebase including alembic, boto3, cfn-lint, click, duckdb, fsspec, gcsfs, and s3fs. Updates include minor version bumps for database and cloud storage libraries, and patch updates for CLI and validation tools.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2461 update query docs
summary: Updated product documentation for alert rule query requirements, including clarifications to the query-overview guide and updated screenshots. The changes reflect new query requirements and provide clearer examples for users configuring alert rules.
userImpact: Users can now reference updated documentation that accurately describes the current alert rule query requirements and configuration options.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies: authzed/spicedb (v1.43.0 → v1.44.0 minor), boto3 (1.38.18 → 1.38.19 patch), and google-cloud-bigquery (3.32.0 → 3.33.0 minor). The google-cloud-bigquery update includes new features for schema autodetection and job reservation support, plus bug fixes for AccessEntry equality and SchemaField type handling.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved BigQuery integration with new schema autodetection capabilities and job reservation support, along with stability improvements across AWS and database client libraries.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2463 link alerts to the job that created them
summary: Alerts created by the alert check job were previously stored with null job_ids. This fix ensures that the job_id is properly populated and linked when alerts are created, establishing the connection between alerts and their originating job.
userImpact: Alerts are now correctly linked to the job that created them, enabling proper tracing and auditing of alert origins.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated eight non-major dependencies to their latest patch and pre-release versions, including boto3, numpy, and various OpenTelemetry instrumentation libraries. These updates include bug fixes and improvements from upstream projects such as boto3's AWS API changes and OpenTelemetry's observability enhancements.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes, security patches, and API improvements in AWS SDK and observability tooling without breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2462 alert rule refactor pt. 2 - update alert check job to be interval aware
summary: Updates the alert rule check job in the data plane to implement interval-based alert check logic, ensuring alerts only run within the context of metrics uploads. Removes automatic alert job creation on job failures to prevent false positives from running alert checks outside scheduled metrics upload intervals.
userImpact: Alert rule checks now respect configured intervals and only execute during metrics upload jobs, eliminating spurious alerts from out-of-sequence job executions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2522 update keycloak to enable multi-domain IDPs
summary: Upgraded Keycloak to include a fix for multi-domain IDP support. This resolves an upstream Keycloak issue that prevented proper authentication configuration for environments using multiple identity provider domains. Bootstrap authentication configuration was updated to work with the new Keycloak version.
userImpact: Multi-domain identity provider configurations now work correctly in Keycloak-based authentication.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2402 Alert rule improvements feature branch
summary: Added an "interval" field to alert rules to prevent false positives by making alert checks aware of configurable check intervals. The feature includes database migrations, updated API models, improved alert rule validation, and comprehensive test coverage for the new interval-aware alert checking behavior.
userImpact: Users can now configure alert check intervals on alert rules to reduce false positives and control how frequently alerts are evaluated.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add more sql funcs
summary: Added more SQL functions to the query modifier to expand the available functions for constructing alert rule queries. This enables users to write more expressive and flexible alert rules with additional SQL capabilities.
userImpact: Users can now use additional SQL functions when writing alert rule queries.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates all non-major dependencies across the project, including patch updates for Flask, SQLAlchemy, boto3, psycopg, and multiple type stub packages, as well as minor version updates for Faker, redis, and timescaledb. These updates bring in the latest bug fixes, security patches, and minor feature improvements from upstream dependencies.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream bug fixes, security patches, and minor improvements in key dependencies like Flask, SQLAlchemy, boto3, redis, and timescaledb.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2402 alert rule refactor pt. 1
summary: This MR introduces a new "interval" field to alert rules and alerts with corresponding database schema and API model updates, along with a migration to populate the field for existing records. The query modifier logic has been refactored to require `'{{interval}}'`, `'{{dateStart}}'`, and `'{{dateEnd}}'` templates and to support variable substitution anywhere in the query, enabling time-bucketing functions in SELECT statements.
userImpact: Users can now configure time intervals on alert rules, which are captured on individual alerts to maintain a record of the settings that were active when the alert was triggered, enabling more sophisticated time-based alert query patterns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates two non-major dependencies to their latest compatible versions: boto3 from 1.38.13 to 1.38.14 (patch update) and google-cloud-bigquery from 3.31.0 to 3.32.0 (minor update). The boto3 patch includes AWS service API updates across multiple services, while the google-cloud-bigquery minor release adds new dataset access policy attributes, incremental results preview support, and additional enum values.
userImpact: Users gain access to the latest AWS service API updates, dataset access control features, and BigQuery query capabilities without breaking changes to existing functionality.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2345: alert check job fault tolerance
summary: Improved fault tolerance in the alert check job executor to better handle failures and edge cases during alert evaluation. This prevents cascading failures and ensures alert jobs remain operational even when encountering transient issues.
userImpact: Alert check jobs now continue to run reliably even when encountering transient errors or edge cases, reducing alert evaluation failures.
author: alex380125

title: [NO-REF] Default alert rule bugfix
summary: A newly created default alert rule was missing SpiceDB authorization relationships, causing 404 errors when attempting to fetch it. This migration adds the missing relationships to the SpiceDB database so the default alert rule can be properly retrieved.
userImpact: Users can now successfully retrieve the default alert rule without encountering a 404 error.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2446: Create alert check job on put state endpoint
summary: Implemented automatic alert check job creation when a job reaches a permanently failed state (after exhausting all retries). The alert check job is now triggered on the put state endpoint when job runs are marked as failed, ensuring that users are notified only when meaningful action is required, reducing alert noise from self-resolving failures.
userImpact: Users now receive alerts only for job failures that require attention, as alert check jobs are created exclusively when jobs fail permanently after all retry attempts are exhausted.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates all non-major dependencies including boto3, click, exceptiongroup, greenlet, opentelemetry packages, psycopg, and tokencost to their latest patch and minor versions. The updates bring in bug fixes, minor improvements, and API documentation updates across the dependency stack.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2434: Create default job failure alert rule
summary: A default job failure alert rule is now automatically created when new models are initialized. This rule notifies users when job execution fails, improving observability of model training and inference pipelines without requiring manual alert configuration.
userImpact: Users now automatically receive alerts when jobs fail for their models, eliminating the need for manual alert rule setup.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2433: Create system metrics table
summary: A new system metrics table has been created in the TimescaleDB database to track system-level metrics across jobs and operations. The metrics repository, API models, and job operators have been updated to support reading from and writing to this new table, enabling better observability of system performance and job execution patterns.
userImpact: Users can now access system-level metrics that provide insights into job performance and system health across the platform.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: boto3 (1.38.11 → 1.38.12), cfn-lint (1.35.0 → 1.35.1), and postgres (16.8 → 16.9). These are routine patch and minor version updates that include AWS API documentation updates and bug fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2456 add link task job and API
summary: Added a new API endpoint to create a scope GenAI model from an existing task in the GenAI engine. The implementation includes a new job type for linking tasks, updated API models, database migrations, and authorization rules via SpiceDB schema updates to support this workflow.
userImpact: Users can now create GenAI models directly from existing tasks through a new API endpoint, streamlining the model creation workflow.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.38.10 to 1.38.11 and cfn-lint from 1.34.2 to 1.35.0. These updates include new AWS API support for EC2 Path Component Exclusion, ImageBuilder SSM Parameters, MediaLive Anywhere Settings, and CloudFormation linting improvements.
userImpact: Users benefit from latest AWS service API updates including EC2 Reachability Analyzer enhancements, MediaLive channel updates, and improved CloudFormation template linting.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2452 fix duplicate user creation API response
summary: Fixed a TypeError in the duplicate user creation error handling for AWS deployments with real Keycloak. The error occurred when comparing a bytes-like object to a string, preventing proper detection of duplicate user email responses from the Keycloak API.
userImpact: Duplicate user creation API responses are now correctly handled in AWS environments, preventing crashes and allowing the API to properly reject duplicate email registrations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2447: Handle non-iterable in filters gracefully
summary: Fixed handling of non-iterable values in connector read filters to prevent crashes when unexpected data types are encountered. The connector filter logic now gracefully handles cases where filter values are not iterable, improving robustness during data plane operations.
userImpact: Connector read operations no longer fail when non-iterable values are passed to filters, enabling more resilient data retrieval from S3 and other connectors.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2445 create raw data reader role
summary: Adds a new "raw data reader" role that can be bound to multiple resource types (workspace and project), allowing organizations like Expel to grant users both Workspace Read All and raw data viewing permissions through separate roles. The role is defined in SpiceDB schema with comprehensive unit tests covering multi-resource-type binding and cross-project permission isolation.
userImpact: Users with the Workspace Read All role can now be granted an additional raw data reader role to view raw data, enabling fine-grained permission management for data access.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2436 fix redis connections in AWS
summary: Redis-py v6 changed the default value of `ssl_check_hostname` from `False` to `True`, causing Redis connections in AWS to fail with a certificate verification error. This fix adjusts the Redis client configuration to work with the new default while maintaining secure connections in AWS environments.
userImpact: Redis connections in AWS deployments now work correctly with Redis-py v6.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency boto3 to v1.38.10
summary: Updated boto3 from v1.38.9 to v1.38.10. This patch release includes botocore API changes for EC2 (EBS Provisioned Rate for Volume Initialization), ServiceCatalog (InvalidParametersException handling), and Timestream services (dualstack endpoints support).
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency update maintains compatibility while providing access to latest AWS API improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2383 fix ownership of upsolve execute patches directory in ECS
summary: Fixed a permission error that prevented the upsolve user from accessing the db-patches directory in ECS deployments. The directory ownership was not being properly set when running in ECS (only worked locally in Docker Compose due to volume mounting), causing patch file execution to fail with an EACCES permission denied error.
userImpact: Upsolve patch execution now works correctly in ECS environments without permission errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2383 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded upsolve to include backend and theme fixes, along with new read-only support. This update addresses issues in the upsolve service and introduces capability for read-only access patterns.
userImpact: Users now have access to backend bug fixes and theme improvements, plus the ability to use upsolve in read-only mode where applicable.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2423: Handle failure to load datasets in metrics calculation jobs
summary: Fixed metrics calculation jobs to gracefully handle dataset loading failures. Previously, when a dataset failed to load, metrics calculations would fail with a cryptic `KeyError`. Now, the system clearly reports which datasets failed to load and skips metrics calculation for those datasets, providing clearer error messaging to users.
userImpact: Users now see clear, actionable error messages when dataset loading fails in metrics jobs, instead of confusing `KeyError` exceptions, and metrics calculations proceed for successfully loaded datasets.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2425: Add datetime information to task connection info
summary: This change adds datetime information to task connection info by introducing a new database migration that populates time-related fields in the connection info table. The enhancement updates related API models, repositories, and job executors to handle the new temporal metadata, with corresponding test updates to validate the functionality.
userImpact: Users can now access datetime information about task connections, providing better visibility into when connections were established or modified.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2427 refresh keycloak token during user invite call
summary: Fixed a bug where the custom user invite call would fail in rare cases when the Keycloak token didn't exist or had expired on server startup. The fix adds token refresh logic to the invite call to ensure a valid token is available before attempting the operation.
userImpact: User invites now succeed reliably even when called immediately after server startup, eliminating rare failures due to missing or expired authentication tokens.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2426 dashboard edit rbac
summary: Added a new RBAC permission `model_edit_dashboard` to control access to dashboard editing functionality. The permission is integrated into the SpiceDB schema and managed roles, with corresponding tests to ensure proper validation and enforcement.
userImpact: Dashboard editing access can now be controlled through role-based access control permissions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2424: Fix regeneration failures
summary: Fixed regeneration failures in task connection secrets management that were introduced when adding support for deleting connection info in the control plane. The issue involved scheduled secret deletion behavior in AWS Secrets Manager where deletion scheduling was interfering with secret regeneration.
userImpact: Task connection secret regeneration now completes successfully without failures related to scheduled deletions.
author: alex380125

title: Update dependency redis to v6
summary: Updated the redis-py dependency from v5.2.1 to v6.0.0, which includes new hash commands with expiration support, improved security defaults (ssl_check_hostname now True by default), and experimental VectorSet command support. This is a major version bump that includes breaking changes to retry configurations and removal of deprecated APIs.
userImpact: Users gain access to new hash commands (HGETDEL, HGETEX, HSETEX) and improved security defaults, but must update cluster and standalone client configurations due to mandatory retry object handling and changed default retry strategies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project including boto3 (1.38.5 → 1.38.9), psycopg (3.2.6 → 3.2.7), spicedb (v1.42.1 → v1.43.0), and redis (5.2.1 → 5.3.0). These updates bring patches and minor version improvements to core infrastructure and AWS SDK libraries, ensuring the system benefits from the latest stability fixes and feature enhancements.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved AWS service support (datazone, devicefarm, ec2, ecs, mediaconvert), enhanced database compatibility, and updated caching infrastructure through the latest minor and patch releases of key dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2399: Tech design for failing metrics calculation jobs
summary: Added technical design documentation for implementing failure monitoring on metrics calculation jobs. The design outlines the approach for creating automatic alert rules that notify users when metrics calculation jobs fail, including job type tracking in alert dimensions and alert check job failure handling considerations. This establishes the technical architecture for a new out-of-the-box alert system to help users monitor critical background job execution.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: UP-2402 alert fixes
summary: Added technical design documentation for stabilizing false positive alerts as part of the 2025 alerting refactor initiative. The documentation outlines the proposed approach to address alert reliability issues in the Arthur Scope platform.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.38.3 to 1.38.5 and pydantic from 2.11.3 to 2.11.4. These are patch-level dependency updates that include minor API enhancements for AWS services and internal improvements to pydantic.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2420: Delete validation key on task deletion
summary: When a task is deleted, its associated validation key is now removed from the control plane to maintain consistency between the control plane and Shield. The endpoint is used within the delete job and follows the same pattern as other control plane state updates, ensuring the API key state remains consistent even if the model deletion process fails partway through.
userImpact: Validation keys are now properly cleaned up when tasks are deleted, preventing orphaned API keys in the system.
author: alex380125

title: NO-REF: Fix bug with data plane config for raw data retrieval
summary: Fixed a configuration bug in the data plane that was causing e2e test failures during raw data retrieval jobs. The issue was in the config handling logic, which has now been corrected to ensure proper initialization and behavior.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval jobs now complete successfully without configuration-related failures.
author: alex380125

title: Up-2383 fix upsolve migration
summary: Fixed a migration issue in version 0.2.40 that caused failures when applied to dev databases. Version 0.2.41 includes the corrective fix by updating the upsolve Dockerfile configuration.
userImpact: Database migrations for upsolve now execute successfully without errors on dev environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.38.3
summary: Updated boto3 from v1.38.2 to v1.38.3. This patch release includes API improvements for bedrock-runtime (S3 integration for images and documents), documentation updates for ECS, and minor fixes for AWS Marketplace Deployment Service.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance update with upstream improvements to AWS SDK capabilities.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] rename engine internal env vars
summary: Renamed internal environment variables used by the engine for consistency and clarity. Changes affect configuration files, connectors, and documentation across the data plane and local development setup.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2385: Validation key regeneration job
summary: Implemented a validation key regeneration job that allows users to securely rotate their API validation keys. The job creates a new key in the Shield backend, removes the old key, and safely updates the control plane with the new key to prevent orphaned credentials and ensure deleted keys are fully removed from the system.
userImpact: Users can now regenerate their API validation keys through a dedicated job, providing a secure way to rotate credentials and manage key lifecycle.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2386: Upload validation key in task creation job
summary: Added functionality to upload validation keys to Shield during task creation job execution. The system now generates and manages task-specific API keys through the task creation process, storing them securely via the secret manager and ensuring they are available for Shield operations.
userImpact: Users can now create model tasks with automatic validation key generation and upload to Shield, enabling secure task-specific API authentication without manual key management.
author: alex380125

title: Bump auth version to 1.0.47 in scope-version-manifest.json
summary: The auth service version was bumped to 1.0.47 in the scope-version-manifest.json file. This update ensures the manifest reflects the latest available auth service version for dependency tracking.
userImpact: The system will now use auth service version 1.0.47, incorporating any updates and fixes included in that release.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [NO-REF]: Update invite user docs
summary: Updated documentation for the invite user endpoint to include the correct status code in API docs. This ensures the API documentation accurately reflects the expected response status rather than showing the default 200.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] add expel scripts to add schema
summary: Added helper scripts for Expel to manage their dataset schema during onboarding. Two utility scripts were added: one to add columns to their schema and another to remove columns. These scripts streamline the customer onboarding process by enabling self-service schema modifications.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Statful services ansible playbook
summary: Added Ansible playbook for provisioning stateful services (PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB containers) on on-premise RHEL VMs using Podman. The playbook handles container setup, volume mounting with proper permissions, certificate generation for testing, and multi-registry image authentication.
userImpact: Operations teams can now automate the deployment of stateful database services on on-premise RHEL infrastructure using the provided Ansible playbook.
author: kacperek0

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.38.0 → 1.38.2), cfn-lint (1.34.1 → 1.34.2), and h11 (0.14.0 → 0.16.0). These updates include AWS API improvements for App Runner, AppSync, DynamoDB, and other services, plus improvements to the h11 HTTP/1.1 library.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility while pulling in upstream bug fixes and API enhancements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2834: Add task connection APIs
summary: Added new task connection APIs to enable management of external connections and validation keys for AI model tasks. This includes database schema migrations, secret management infrastructure for task connection credentials, permission role definitions, and router endpoints to support the full lifecycle of task connections alongside models.
userImpact: Users can now create, retrieve, update, and manage task connections with secure credential storage and role-based access control, enabling integration of external services with AI model tasks.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2398 add capabilities to engine resources
summary: Added a typed Capabilities object to engine resources with an initial `gen_ai_enabled` property to track Shield installation status. The API now supports setting capabilities via POST and PATCH endpoints, and filtering data plane resources by `gen_ai_enabled` status across workspace and project scopes.
userImpact: Users can now track and filter data planes by GenAI capability status, enabling better visibility and management of Shield-enabled installations across workspaces and projects.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.37.38 to 1.38.0 and greenlet from 3.2.0 to 3.2.1. The boto3 upgrade includes minor API changes across multiple AWS services (Account, Cognito, EC2, Entity Resolution, MQ, Redshift Serverless) and marks end of support for Python 3.8.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance with potential AWS service API enhancements available through boto3 updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] fix shield e2e test
summary: Fixed a flaky Shield e2e test that was failing because the target task was not appearing on the first page of results. The demo instance contains ~40 tasks, so pagination needed to be handled to reliably locate the test task.
userImpact: Shield e2e tests now run reliably without intermittent failures due to pagination issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: allow timescale stack to create backup role
summary: The timescale stack deployment script now includes permissions to create a backup role. This fixes an issue where the stack lacked the necessary role creation capability for backup operations.
userImpact: Timescale database backups can now be created successfully with the proper backup role in place.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2379 create internal connectors on data plane project association
summary: When a data plane is associated with a project, an internal connector is now automatically created for that data plane. The system now prevents removal of data planes from projects if user-facing connectors or internal connectors with dependent models exist, and automatically cleans up internal connectors and datasets during deletion if no models depend on them.
userImpact: Users can now leverage internal connectors automatically provisioned when adding data planes to projects, with safeguards preventing accidental removal of data planes that have active model dependencies.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2219: Add engine name validation
summary: Added engine name validation to the backend API, implementing checks in the data planes and users operators to ensure engine names conform to required standards. This prevents invalid engine names from being created or updated, improving data integrity and reducing client-side errors.
userImpact: Engine names are now validated server-side, preventing creation or updates with invalid engine names and returning clear validation errors.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2378 & UP-2380 hide engine internal connectors and datasets
summary: Updated the connectors and datasets APIs to hide engine internal connector resources and datasets by default. This prevents internal system connectors and their associated datasets from appearing in user-facing API responses, reducing clutter and clarifying which resources are available for direct user management.
userImpact: Users no longer see internal engine connectors and datasets in the API, simplifying the interface to show only user-manageable resources.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix email settings passing to bootstrap CFT
summary: Email configuration settings were not being passed to the bootstrap CloudFormation template, causing warnings during bootstrap job execution. This fix adds the missing email settings to the CFT and deploys scripts to ensure authentication email reply-to fields are properly configured.
userImpact: Bootstrap jobs now correctly pass all email configuration settings, eliminating authentication email warnings and ensuring proper email reply-to field setup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: add timescale daily backups for ec2 images/amis
summary: Added daily automated backups for TimeScale by creating EC2 AMI snapshots. PostgreSQL data on the root volume is now backed up daily, providing a backup and restore option for the database.
userImpact: TimeScale database now has daily automated AMI backups, enabling easier recovery and data protection.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2377 internal connector
summary: Added a new `engine_internal` connector type that functions identically to the Shield connector but sources credentials from the data plane's environment variables instead of the platform. This connector is intentionally hidden from the API response for connector types to prevent it from appearing in the frontend.
userImpact: Users can now configure Shield data sources using credentials from the data plane's environment, enabling Shield API key management workflows to populate code templates for Shield instance integration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2290: Add API to retrieve feature flags
summary: Added a new API endpoint to retrieve all feature flags as a single JSON object containing feature flag names mapped to their values. This enables the frontend to consume all global environment feature flags in a single call without requiring backend changes when new flags are added.
userImpact: Users can now query a unified API endpoint to retrieve all feature flags for the environment, allowing dynamic feature flag consumption in the frontend.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2315: Update org bootstrap to use immutable org identifier
summary: Updated organization bootstrap logic to use an immutable organization identifier instead of recreating default organizations on each bootstrap run. This prevents unintended recreation of default organizations when bootstrap configuration is updated, while maintaining backward compatibility with existing default organization IDs.
userImpact: Default organizations are no longer recreated during bootstrap runs when configuration changes, ensuring stability and preventing duplicate organization creation.
author: alex380125

title: XP-1556: Add variable to control fetching raw data
summary: Added an environment variable to control whether raw data fetching is enabled in the data plane. The feature is configurable via the new FETCH_RAW_DATA env var across CloudFormation, Docker Compose, and all deployment environments, with corresponding configuration logic and validation tests.
userImpact: Users can now enable or disable raw data fetching at the engine level by setting the FETCH_RAW_DATA environment variable, giving administrators control over data access permissions.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: fix: timescale port log in deployment script
summary: The deployment script was incorrectly logging the timescale hostname instead of the timescale port in a log line. This fix corrects the log output to display the actual port value, improving visibility into deployment configuration during testing.
userImpact: Deployment logs now correctly display the timescale port instead of the hostname, making it easier to debug deployment issues.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2291: Create new user via email
summary: Implemented user invitation via email, allowing admins to invite new users and assign them to groups or roles without requiring password setup. This feature adds a new endpoint for creating user invites with associated permissions, includes database schema updates to track group assignments in invites, and introduces comprehensive authorization validation across the system.
userImpact: Users can now invite new team members via email and assign them to groups or roles directly during the invitation process, with the invited users setting their own password upon signup.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2383 update upsolve and new export
summary: Upgraded Upsolve to the latest release, including a new multiclass model dashboard and text description chart feature. These changes enable better visualization and documentation of multiclass classification workflows that were previously missing dashboard support.
userImpact: Users can now visualize and manage multiclass classification models with a dedicated dashboard, and add text descriptions to charts for better documentation.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2066 tech design for task API key management
summary: Added technical design documentation for managing Shield API connection information in the platform and supporting API key regeneration for Shield tasks. This design proposal outlines the backend, job, and key management approach for Shield integration, addressing UP-2066.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Temporarily support user claims with or without org ID included
summary: Updated the token validator to support user claims with or without organization ID included, enabling a temporary backwards compatibility mode during the transition to org ID-based claims. This allows the system to handle tokens in both formats while migrations to the new claim structure are rolled out across environments.
userImpact: Tokens with or without organization IDs in user claims are now correctly validated, preventing authentication failures during the staged rollout of organization ID support.
author: alex380125

title: Hotfix/byo timescale
summary: This MR adds support for Bring-Your-Own (BYO) Timescale parameters across the Scope infrastructure stacks. It removes cross-stack references to the managed Timescale stack and updates the Upsolve, Bootstrap, and App Plane stacks to accept configurable Timescale host and instance ID parameters, while also updating the deployment logic to pass these parameters during stack creation.
userImpact: Users can now configure Scope deployments to use external Timescale instances by providing BYO_TIMESCALE_HOST and BYO_TIMESCALE_INSTANCE_ID parameters, enabling more flexible infrastructure architecture.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2242 Fix Query Slowness by removing old metric versions
summary: Metrics versions are now automatically cleaned up when a new version is uploaded, preventing accumulation of old versions that were causing severe query slowness. Previously, hundreds of thousands of old metric versions would pile up over time (e.g., Axios had thousands in a 2-week window), causing dashboards to take over a minute to load. This change implements automatic deletion of superseded versions to keep the metrics tables lean and performant.
userImpact: Users will experience significantly faster dashboard load times as old, unused metric versions are automatically cleaned up, preventing the query performance degradation that occurred when versions accumulated.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Add organization user-friendly display name
summary: Added user-friendly display names for organizations by including the organization ID in user authentication claims and refactoring organization identity management to use immutable org IDs instead of mutable names for keycloak API operations. The keycloak organization display name is now synced with the database name field for clarity in email invitations, while the org ID serves as the stable unique identifier across systems.
userImpact: Users can now see organization display names in email invitations and other communications, providing clearer context about which organization they're joining while maintaining secure and stable identity management.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] update docs
summary: Minor updates to product documentation including corrections to metrics query overview and simplification of SDK authentication code examples. These are non-functional documentation improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; documentation remains consistent with existing functionality.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency types-croniter to v6
summary: Updated types-croniter dependency from v5.0.1 to v6.0.0 across app_plane and data_plane requirement files. This is a dependency maintenance update that ensures the project uses the latest type stubs for croniter.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including patch and minor version bumps for key libraries like boto3, numpy, uvicorn, pydantic_core, opentelemetry packages, greenlet, and others. This keeps the dependency tree current with the latest stable releases while avoiding breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2328 add allowed functions for new PSI query
summary: Added missing allowed functions to the PSI query modifier to support Expel's continuous alert rule. The query modifier now permits a broader set of functions required by the PSI continuous query implementation.
userImpact: Expel's PSI continuous alert rule now functions correctly with the previously unsupported query functions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies to patch versions: boto3 (1.37.30 → 1.37.31) with AWS API updates, cfn-lint (1.33.0 → 1.33.1), and pytest-httpserver (1.1.2 → 1.1.3) with daemon thread fix. These are routine maintenance updates to keep dependencies current.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain compatibility and include upstream bug fixes and API documentation improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2317 fix keycloak email from
summary: Added configuration for setting the From Display Name field for emails sent by Keycloak across environments. The settings were previously manually configured on dev and prod, but are now codified in configuration files to ensure consistency and ease future environment setup. Tested locally to verify the configurations are applied correctly during bootstrap.
userImpact: Users will now see the proper From display name when receiving Keycloak emails, improving email authenticity and brand consistency across all environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2183: Support email on password update
summary: Added support for sending email notifications when users update their password by configuring Keycloak's email event listener. The change updates the Keycloak authentication configuration to include the JBossLoggingEventListenerProvider, which logs user events including password updates and error conditions, matching Keycloak's default behavior.
userImpact: Users will now receive email notifications when they update their password, enabling better account security awareness and activity tracking.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] turn on sign up in prod
summary: The sign-up feature flag has been enabled in production, making the sign-up functionality available to users in the production environment. This deployment follows completion of sign-up development and testing.
userImpact: Users can now sign up for new accounts in production.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix invalid escape sequence string
summary: Fixed an invalid escape sequence warning in converters.py that was being triggered by a string literal containing `\.`. The fix corrects the string escaping to properly handle the backslash character without triggering Python's SyntaxWarning.
userImpact: The SyntaxWarning for invalid escape sequence is no longer emitted during application startup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2303 add multiclass use case for expel
summary: Added support for multiclass classification as a new model problem type in Expel. This includes new metrics for multiclass inference count by class and confusion matrix calculation, along with comprehensive documentation and test coverage including a new vehicles classification dataset.
userImpact: Users can now monitor and analyze multiclass classification models with inference count by class and confusion matrix metrics.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2304: Fix deletion of sub-resources
summary: Fixed deletion of sub-resources (datasets, models, connectors) to properly handle cascading deletions and dependent resource cleanup. The fix updates the base operator's deletion logic and includes more informative error messages that reference the specific resource names when deletion fails due to dependent resources still in use.
userImpact: Users can now successfully delete workspaces, projects, datasets, and connectors when appropriate, with clear error messages indicating which dependent resources are blocking deletion.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including boto3 (1.37.28→1.37.30), cfn-lint (1.32.4→1.33.0), duckdb (1.2.1→1.2.2), pydantic (2.11.2→2.11.3), python-keycloak (5.3.1→5.5.0), and timescaledb (2.19.1→2.19.2). These are routine patch and minor version updates that maintain compatibility while incorporating bug fixes and minor enhancements from upstream projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2233 remove model metrics configs for unlinked datasets
summary: When a dataset is unlinked from a model, any metrics configurations that reference the unlinked dataset are now automatically removed. Previously, these stale metrics would persist even after the dataset was unlinked, causing inconsistency in model configuration.
userImpact: Users no longer need to manually clean up metrics configurations that reference datasets removed from a model—they are now automatically removed when the dataset is unlinked.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2238 add filter to get /project/id/datasets API to allow limiting results by data plane ID
summary: Added a filter parameter to the GET /project/{id}/datasets API endpoint to allow limiting dataset results by data plane ID. This enables the frontend to efficiently retrieve only datasets associated with a specific data plane, improving query precision and performance.
userImpact: Users can now filter datasets by data plane ID when querying the GET /project/{id}/datasets API, allowing them to retrieve only relevant datasets for a specific data plane.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2237 prevent models and joined datasets from multiple engines
summary: Added validation to prevent models and joined datasets from being backed by different engines in multi-engine workspaces. Also denormalized data_plane_id to datasets, available_datasets, and models resources to enable frontend filtering of dataset choices based on engine compatibility.
userImpact: Users can no longer accidentally create models or joined datasets that reference resources from different engines, preventing invalid cross-engine configurations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.37.27 → 1.37.28), cfn-lint (1.32.3 → 1.32.4), and moto (5.1.2 → 5.1.3). These patch-level updates include bug fixes and enhancements to AWS mocking and CloudFormation linting tools used by the project.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2223: Enforce 1:1 model to shield dataset
summary: The model-to-shield dataset relationship is now enforced to be 1:1, preventing a single model from being associated with multiple shield datasets. This change ensures proper data isolation and prevents configuration conflicts that could arise from duplicate model associations.
userImpact: Users can no longer associate a single model with multiple shield datasets; models are now restricted to a 1:1 relationship with their corresponding shield dataset.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2220 final task apis
summary: Implements task state cache storage in the database to complete the final API needed for GenAI model management in the UI. Adds database migration for task storage cache, new repository methods, and API endpoints to retrieve cached task state from the backend. Includes comprehensive integration tests and cleanup tooling for local development.
userImpact: Frontend can now retrieve cached task state from the backend, completing the GenAI model management API surface.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2282: Set up assigning permissions to first user
summary: Implemented invitation acceptance workflow that assigns permissions to the first user of an organization. When a user accepts an invite via the `accept-invite` endpoint, they are redirected to the create workspace flow on the Arthur home page. This sets up proper permission initialization for newly invited users joining organizations.
userImpact: Users who accept email invitations can now be properly onboarded to their organization with appropriate permissions automatically assigned.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated patch versions of boto3 (1.37.26→1.37.27), pydantic (2.11.1→2.11.2), and typing_extensions (4.13.0→4.13.1). These are routine dependency updates that bring in bug fixes and minor improvements to core libraries used by the system.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including authlib 1.5.1→1.5.2, boto3 1.37.25→1.37.26, pydantic_core 2.33.0→2.33.1, cfn-lint 1.32.1→1.32.3, and upgraded Python base image from 3.8-3.12 to 3.13 Alpine. These are routine patch and minor version updates for improved compatibility and bug fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2221 Part 2 implement task jobs
summary: Implements task management job types and executors for the shield connector, enabling end-to-end task creation, fetching, and lifecycle management. Refactored shield type handling to use API models instead of generated client types for JSON parsing, resolving union type discrimination issues with auto-generated clients. Includes database migrations, job execution logic, and comprehensive type converters for seamless integration between control plane and data plane APIs.
userImpact: Users can now create, manage, and execute shield task management jobs through the control plane API with proper rule configuration support (KeywordRule, RegexRule, PIIDataRule).
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates non-major dependencies across the codebase, including boto3 (1.37.24→1.37.25), timescaledb (2.19.0→2.19.1), spicedb (1.41.0→1.42.0), and types-PyYAML type stubs. All updates are patch or minor version bumps that maintain compatibility and include routine maintenance improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2221 Part 1 Create separate job types for API-facing jobs vs internal jobs
summary: Introduces separate `PostJobKind` and `PostJobSpecType` types to distinguish between API-facing jobs and internal jobs like `fetch_data`. Internal jobs are now only submittable through internal data retrieval endpoints, not the public POST jobs API. This lays the foundation for additional internal jobs to support task management via dedicated task management APIs.
userImpact: The public POST jobs API no longer exposes the `fetch_data` job type, ensuring internal-only operations cannot be triggered directly by external callers; the GET jobs API continues to list all job types for reference.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2222 part 2 add task management to shield connector
summary: Adds task management functions to the shield connector as a follow-up to the initial shield connector implementation. This includes support for creating, updating, deleting, and retrieving tasks through the Shield API client, with comprehensive unit and integration test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now manage tasks (create, read, update, delete) directly through the shield connector integration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Feat/ssm onprem replacement
summary: Replaced on-premise Secret Store Manager with PostgreSQL-backed implementation and integrated AWS Secrets Manager for cloud environments. This eliminates the in-memory secret store and provides a unified, persistent secret management solution across both on-premise and AWS deployments.
userImpact: Users can now securely store and retrieve secrets using a persistent PostgreSQL backend for on-premise deployments and AWS Secrets Manager for cloud environments, improving secret durability and compliance.
author: kacperek0

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated patch-level dependencies across boto3, fsspec, gcsfs, s3fs, and types-psutil to their latest versions. These are routine maintenance updates that improve compatibility with AWS services, cloud storage integrations, and type checking tools.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2222 part 1, convert shield to use generated API client
summary: Converted Shield connector to use an auto-generated API client based on OpenAPI specifications. The generated client code is now checked in under `shield_api_client_gen/clients/python`, replacing manual HTTP handling with a typed, maintainable client library. This enables easier maintenance and sets the foundation for future auto-generation from CI/CD pipelines.
userImpact: Shield connector functionality remains unchanged from the user perspective; the underlying implementation now uses a generated API client for better maintainability and type safety.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2220 add onboarding_identifier to model resource and APIs
summary: Adds `onboarding_identifier` field to the model resource and POST/GET model APIs, enabling the frontend to label models during the GenAI onboarding flow for easy lookup after model creation completes. The change includes database migration, API model updates, repository changes, and comprehensive test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now assign an onboarding identifier when creating models, allowing the frontend to track and retrieve models created during the GenAI onboarding process.
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat: push data plane images to dockerhub
summary: This MR adds CI/CD automation to push Data Plane images to Docker Hub when releases are created. Previously, images were only available in internal registries (ECR/Nexus). The implementation includes safeguards to push the "latest" tag only on production releases, and uses release version manifests as container tags to maintain consistency with API deployments.
userImpact: Users can now pull Data Plane images directly from Docker Hub at release time, ensuring version alignment with SaaS API deployments and eliminating the need for internal registry access.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies including alembic, boto3, fsspec, gcsfs, moto, pydantic, s3fs, and tokencost to their latest patch versions. These updates include bug fixes and minor improvements across AWS SDK, database migration, file system, and validation libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependencies updated to receive latest patches and bug fixes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2073 GenAI connector lifecycle tech design
summary: Technical design documentation added for supporting workspace-level connectors to enable GenAI engine usage across all projects in a workspace. The design covers connector lifecycle management, authorization considerations, and integration with existing project-level connectors, establishing the foundation for future workspace connector implementation.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is technical design documentation to guide future implementation of workspace-level connector support.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project: SQLAlchemy (2.0.39→2.0.40), boto3 (1.37.21→1.37.22), pydantic (2.10.6→2.11.0), and types-requests. These updates include patch releases with bug fixes and a minor pydantic release focused on build-time performance improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency updates ensure compatibility with latest library versions and performance improvements from pydantic v2.11.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2220 initial task management API facade
summary: Introduces an initial API facade for task management in Shield via the control plane. The implementation provides hardcoded endpoint responses for task CRUD operations through new routers and API models, enabling frontend development to proceed with typed clients while backend job execution and resource creation are implemented in follow-up work.
userImpact: Users can now call task management API endpoints to create and manage tasks in Shield projects, though endpoints currently return hardcoded responses and do not yet persist actual task resources or job executions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2258 make metric names more user friendly
summary: Metric names now display user-friendly display names instead of internal class names. Developers can set explicit `display_name` properties on metric definitions, making metrics more readable in the UI and easier to search when adding new ones to a model. No database migration required since metrics are loaded from aggregation definitions at runtime.
userImpact: Users now see clear, human-readable metric names in the UI instead of technical class names, and developers can more easily discover and manage metrics when configuring models.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for boto3 (patch: 1.37.20 → 1.37.21) and pydantic_core (minor: 2.32.0 → 2.33.0). boto3 adds API enhancements for AWS services including Direct Connect tagging and WAF v2 Amplify integration. pydantic_core adds extra keys validation for model fields and reverts a by_alias default value change.
userImpact: Users gain access to latest AWS API improvements and Pydantic validation enhancements without any breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2245 ship data plane latest tag
summary: Updated the CI/CD pipeline to automatically tag the latest data plane deployment to SaaS production with the "latest" tag. This simplifies version management by ensuring the most recent production deployment is always clearly marked.
userImpact: Users and downstream systems can now reliably reference the latest production data plane version using the "latest" tag instead of tracking specific version numbers.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2196: Make schema inspection work with no recent data
summary: Schema inference now works reliably when there is no recent data by progressively expanding the time interval backwards to find sufficient data for analysis. The job now prioritizes the most recent data first and falls back to older intervals if needed, ensuring schema inference succeeds even when data is sparse in recent time periods. Data is returned in descending order by default to match expected monitoring platform behavior.
userImpact: Schema inspection jobs no longer fail when recent data is unavailable; the system automatically searches further back in time to find a sufficient sample for accurate schema inference.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2249: Fix org name validation
summary: Fixed organization name validation by replacing the broken alphanumeric-only pattern with Keycloak's URL-friendly regex standard from RFC 3986. This allows organization names to include dashes, non-English characters, and numbers at the start, matching Keycloak's actual requirements and enabling better support for international users and SaaS deployments.
userImpact: Users can now create organizations with names containing dashes, non-English characters, and leading numbers, bringing backend validation in line with Keycloak's actual requirements.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies including boto3, cfn-lint, google-cloud-bigquery, PyYAML types, pytz types, and typing_extensions. These patch and minor version updates include API enhancements to AWS services (bedrock-agent, eks, sagemaker, etc.) and improvements to type stubs.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2241 latest upsolve export
summary: Updated the latest Upsolve export to resolve issues with the regression dashboard's link filters pop-up displaying incorrectly. A corrupted filter state was fixed by Upsolve, and pulling the latest export resolves the problem.
userImpact: Users can now properly access and display link filters in the regression dashboard without encountering display issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2177: User permissions tech design
summary: This merge request adds technical design documentation for user permissions and the sign-up flow, including sequence diagrams to clarify the interactions between the Arthur frontend, Keycloak, and backend APIs. The documentation outlines how user state is created across Arthur DB and Keycloak, and how users are redirected and granted permissions after registration and invite acceptance.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: [chore] Update SpiceDB 1.41.0
summary: Updated SpiceDB dependency from the previous version to 1.41.0 across all deployment environments (dev, prod, and STS platforms). The image has been pushed to ECR and verified with the update script.
userImpact: Users benefit from SpiceDB v1.41.0 improvements, bug fixes, and security patches as the authorization service is upgraded across all platform deployments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including Faker (37.0.2 → 37.1.0), boto3 (1.37.18 → 1.37.19), pytest-asyncio (0.25.3 → 0.26.0), spicedb (v1.40.1 → v1.41.0), and TimescaleDB (2.18.2 → 2.19.0). These updates bring improved test framework capabilities, enhanced AWS API support, and new localization features.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved async test support in pytest-asyncio, updated AWS service APIs in boto3, and enhanced database performance with TimescaleDB 2.19.0.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2229 and UP-2217 add JSON data type
summary: Adds support for DuckDB's JSON data type to handle complex nested structures that cannot be mapped to single-type lists or structures (such as multi-typed arrays). This enables successful onboarding of datasets with messy, heterogeneous data like Expel's new model dataset. Also includes CICD refactoring to ensure app and data plane tests run correctly when scope_common is modified.
userImpact: Users can now successfully load and onboard datasets containing complex JSON structures and multi-typed lists that previously failed during schema inference.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2224 update alert rule query guides
summary: Updated alert rule query guides and documentation that were missed in a previous merge request. The changes include fixes to alert rule query examples in the onboarding demo and refreshed documentation with an updated screenshot and revised query overview text.
userImpact: Users now have accurate and up-to-date alert rule query documentation and examples in the onboarding guide.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2201: Include jobs referencing datasets using models in model activity log
summary: Model activity logs now include jobs that reference datasets using the model, providing a more complete view of model usage and dependencies. This enhancement extends the activity tracking to capture indirect model relationships through dataset references, improving visibility into model impact and usage patterns.
userImpact: Users can now see all jobs that use a model either directly or indirectly through dataset references in the model's activity log.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2167: Update CFTs with email parameters
summary: Updated CloudFormation Templates across multiple deployment environments to include email configuration parameters. Configuration files for AWS dev, prod, Axios, and Expel STS platforms now define email-related environment variables, enabling email functionality to be controlled through deployment configurations rather than hardcoded values.
userImpact: Users can now control email parameters across different deployment environments through configuration files, allowing for environment-specific email settings during application deployment.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2224 convert alert rule required fields
summary: Alert rule validation now expects `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` columns instead of `timestamp` and `value`, resolving naming conflicts with table columns that were causing bugs. This change includes updates to validators, tests, demo onboarding scripts, and query execution logic, with corresponding frontend changes in a separate MR.
userImpact: Users can now create alert rules without encountering naming conflicts between required columns and table columns, and must use `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` as the required column names for new alert rules.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Deploy multi tenancy feature branch
summary: This merge request deploys the multi-tenancy feature branch, implementing organization-scoped authentication and Keycloak org management. Changes include refactoring user authentication to use organization names instead of realms, automatically creating Keycloak organizations with default scopes for new clients, and updating authorization schemas to support org-level role management.
userImpact: Users can now authenticate and operate within organization-scoped contexts, with automatic Keycloak organization provisioning and org-level authorization controls enabled.

title: UP-2225 fix hallucination chart
summary: Fixed the hallucination chart query by properly wrapping the time range filter in brackets, allowing the conditional date filtering to apply correctly. This resolves an issue where the chart was not respecting the selected date range.
userImpact: Users can now correctly filter the hallucination count metrics by date range in the chart.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] prevent data plane local dev script from overwriting client secret if already set
summary: The data plane local development startup script was unconditionally overwriting the client secret environment variable, preventing developers from running the script against a dev environment with an already-configured secret. This change adds a conditional check to preserve any existing client secret value.
userImpact: Developers can now run the data plane local dev script without losing their existing client secret configuration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2172: Create sign up endpoint
summary: A new sign-up endpoint has been created to allow users to register and create an organization. The endpoint accepts sign-up requests, sends verification emails, and redirects users to the frontend home page upon acceptance with the org member role assigned.
userImpact: Users can now sign up and create an organization through the new registration endpoint, receiving email verification and automatic role assignment.

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: UP-2209 make numeric sketch metrics use continuous tag
summary: Numeric sketch metrics now respect a "continuous" tag to determine whether to apply sketch-based drift detection or categorical counting. Previously, the system auto-applied numeric metrics to all INT and FLOAT columns regardless of their actual data type, causing PSI bucket allocation to fail for categorical integer columns with fewer than 10 unique values. This change allows users to tag columns appropriately so the correct metrics are auto-assigned during model creation.
userImpact: Users can now tag numeric columns as "continuous" or categorical, and the platform will automatically assign sketch aggregation for continuous columns and categorical count for categorical columns, enabling proper data drift detection for mixed-type numeric data.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2073 tech design for shield state sync
summary: Added technical documentation for Shield state synchronization with the control plane, detailing implementation options discussed at the March 2025 offsite. The document covers API design decisions, task state management, and integration patterns for syncing Shield tasks with the Arthur control plane, including API contract examples and endpoint specifications.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] increase auth replicas to two
summary: Increased the auth service replica count from 1 to 2 across all ECS environments to eliminate zero-replica alerts. This ensures at least one replica remains available during maintenance windows without service disruption.
userImpact: Auth service is now more resilient and will remain available during maintenance operations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2167, UP-2169: add config for enabling emails and forgot password flow
summary: Added configuration options to enable email functionality and the forgot password flow in Keycloak. This includes new CloudFormation parameters for SMTP credentials (username, password, host, port) and toggles for SSL/StartTLS, along with bootstrap updates to configure Keycloak realms with these email settings. Manual Keycloak realm updates are required for email setup as automated realm configuration is not yet supported.
userImpact: Administrators can now configure email sending through SMTP/AWS SES and enable the forgot password flow in Keycloak by setting the new configuration parameters and manually updating the Keycloak realm.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1698 add axios ground truth dataset

title: Onprem functionality with OpenShift
summary: Added comprehensive Helm charts to deploy Scope in on-premise OpenShift environments. The charts include control plane components (frontend, app plane, Keycloak, Redis, SpiceDB, Upsolve) and a data plane component with support for configurable deployments, service discovery, and horizontal pod autoscaling.
userImpact: Users can now deploy and manage Scope in on-premise OpenShift clusters using industry-standard Helm charts with customizable configuration through values.yaml and secrets files.

userImpact: Token cost calculations no longer fail when processing inferences from legacy Shield instances that lack token data.

summary: Implemented alert deduplication logic to prevent duplicate alerts from firing when the same data point remains out of threshold across metric recalculations. The system now deduplicates alerts based on timestamp and alert rule, ensuring that users only receive alerts when new or changed data points actually trigger threshold violations, rather than re-triggering on unchanged historical data.
userImpact: Users will no longer receive duplicate alert notifications for the same out-of-threshold data point across multiple metric calculation runs.

title: UP-1986: Proposal for query API updates for data drift
summary: Added proposal and technical documentation for Query API updates to support data drift detection. The update introduces a new `variables` section in the API schema that allows queries to use templated time variables (e.g., `{{dateStart}}`) aligned with upsolve-style SQL patterns, simplifying migration from charts to alert rules. This is a breaking change requiring alert rules to use standardized time variables for consistent query execution.
userImpact: Users can now define data drift detection queries using familiar upsolve-style templated variables, making it easier to copy SQL patterns from charts directly into alert rules.

title: UP-1976: data drift chart implementation
summary: Implemented data drift chart functionality for monitoring categorical and numerical feature distributions over time using Population Stability Index (PSI) calculations. title: UP-1993: fix avail datasets pagination
summary: Fixed a pagination bug in available datasets queries where an outer join on `DBDatasetLocatorField` caused datasets with multiple locator fields to appear as duplicates in the result set. Duplicates were removed from the final response but not before pagination was calculated, resulting in inaccurate page counts and totals. Pagination now correctly reflects the actual number of unique available datasets.
userImpact: Users querying available datasets will now see correct pagination counts and proper distribution of results across pages, with no duplicate entries inflating the total result count.

title: Consolidate alb stacks
summary: Consolidated load balancer CloudFormation stacks by changing the Hosted Zone parameter type from a reference to a String. This allows a dummy value to be passed when Route 53 records are not being created, working around CloudFormation template validation limitations where conditional parameters are still validated even when unused. The changes include removing the separate no-DNS stack variant and improving logging to show the load balancer DNS name for manual record creation.
userImpact: Users can now deploy a single consolidated load balancer stack in both DNS-enabled and DNS-disabled configurations without needing separate stack templates.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve BE 0.2.16
summary: Upgraded Upsolve backend to version 0.2.16 to fix bugs with custom dashboards. Updated Upsolve API key tenant configuration to use unique display names instead of the deprecated `key` field, and removed Grafana from the local development stack.
userImpact: Custom dashboards now function correctly, and Upsolve tenant identifiers are properly configured to prevent issues with dashboard filters and tenant management.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: add deployment configuration for auth to safely update
summary: Added ECS deployment configuration to the Auth service with minimum 0 and maximum 100 task instances, ensuring all existing tasks are terminated and replaced in a single batch during updates. This configuration enables graceful Auth service upgrades without partial deployments.
userImpact: Auth service deployments now complete safely and consistently, preventing partial or failed upgrade scenarios.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1723: Add ordering prio to schema fields
summary: Added ordering priority field to schema fields, enabling control over field presentation order in connector schemas. A database migration was added to support the new field, and connector schema definitions across AWS, BigQuery, GCS, Google, S3, and Shield connectors were updated with priority values to establish initial field ordering.
userImpact: Connector schema fields are now presented in a consistent, ordered manner as specified by priority values, improving the user interface presentation of available connector fields.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1935: remove local_fs constant
summary: Removed the unused local_fs constant from the ConnectorType enumeration after the logic for deleting local_fs connectors was migrated to use direct SQL operations. This cleanup is now safe to deploy since the migration no longer relies on API parsing of the local_fs connector type.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal cleanup of unused connector type constant.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project, including authlib (1.3.2 → 1.4.0), boto3 (1.35.84 → 1.35.85), fsspec (2024.10.0 → 2024.12.0), gcsfs (2024.10.0 → 2024.12.0), psutil (6.1.0 → 6.1.1), and s3fs (2024.10.0 → 2024.12.0).
