title: UP-1645: Add dataset and connector metadata to models
summary: Added dataset and connector metadata fields to the models API response and repository layer. This prepares the groundwork for future support of multiple datasets per model, currently stored as a single-item list that will be expanded when related frontend changes are merged.
userImpact: Models API responses now include dataset and connector metadata, enabling clients to understand which datasets and connectors a model is associated with.

title: UP-1674: auto-masking for column conflicts
summary: Implemented auto-masking for column conflicts when creating joined datasets. The system now automatically handles schema conflicts by applying alias masks to manage overlapping column names across datasets being joined. This prevents naming collisions and enables seamless dataset combination without manual conflict resolution.
userImpact: Users can now join datasets with conflicting column names, which are automatically masked and resolved through an alias mask mechanism without requiring manual schema editing.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] enable vercel domains in CORS
summary: Updated CORS configuration to support Vercel branch deployment domains, which use unique random subdomains that previously broke CORS. This enables frontend deployments on Vercel preview environments to communicate with the backend without cross-origin errors.
userImpact: Users can now access frontend applications deployed on Vercel preview branches without encountering CORS errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: check if source branch is origin/main
summary: Fixed tag-driven deployment jobs that were failing to deploy AWS dev because the commit branch was not set. The fix checks if the source branch is origin/main to properly trigger AWS dev deployments.
userImpact: AWS dev deployments now trigger correctly for tag-driven jobs without requiring the commit branch to be explicitly set.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.83 → 1.35.84), moto (5.0.23 → 5.0.24), protobuf (5.29.1 → 5.29.2), pydantic (2.10.3 → 2.10.4), and pydantic_core (2.27.1 → 2.27.2). These are patch-level updates that include minor bug fixes and AWS service API enhancements.

title: chore: allow releases to be triggered on a schedule
summary: Updated GitLab CI/CD pipeline rules to allow the Create-Release-Tag job to be triggered by scheduled pipelines. Previously, scheduled release pipelines would fail to create release tags due to restrictive job rules. This enables automated scheduled releases and deployments to aws-dev-platform.

title: chore(deps): update dependency croniter to v6
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v5.0.1 to v6.0.0. This major version includes timezone handling improvements, public API additions for datetime conversion methods, EPOCH calculation fixes for non-UTC and 32-bit systems, and code quality enhancements.

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.83

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: UP-1697 & UP-1875 axios regression dashboard
summary: Adds three new use-case dashboards: Regression (with inference count, MAE, MSE, RMSE, category/numeric distribution, and null count charts), Custom (with category counts, numeric distribution, and null counts), and Enhanced Binary Classification. Introduces reusable Categorical Column Counts and Numeric Column Distribution charts. Includes a new onboarding script for the Axios demo dataset that pre-populates these dashboards with sample metrics.
userImpact: Users can now view regression model performance metrics on a dedicated dashboard and leverage standardized data quality charts across multiple use cases, with the Axios demo dataset automatically configured with these metrics.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1734: Rip out local fs connector
summary: Removed the local filesystem connector implementation from the codebase, including the connector schema, bootstrap configurations, and the local file connector module. A database migration safely deletes any existing local_fs connectors and their secrets. The frontend still hard-codes LOCAL_FS in one place but will be updated in a follow-up ticket after the backend removal deploys; integration tests have been updated to use BigQuery datasets instead of local filesystem data.
userImpact: Users can no longer create or use local filesystem connectors; existing local_fs connectors will be automatically deleted during the database migration.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.81→1.35.82), cfn-lint (1.22.1→1.22.2), and pglast (6.11→6.13). These are routine patch and minor version updates that include AWS API updates and bug fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1888 BAM demo data
summary: Added a Mergers and Acquisitions demo dataset for BAM customers with a complete onboarding script. Enhanced the data generation script to support varying the number of inferences per day, enabling more realistic and diverse demo scenarios. The dataset includes metric configurations for predicted column statistics and categorical counts.
userImpact: BAM customers can now onboard with a pre-built M&A demo dataset that generates inference variation, allowing them to explore ArthurAI's capabilities with realistic data patterns.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Remove dbops part1
summary: Removed the legacy dbops endpoint exposure and transitioned database bootstrap operations to use init containers in both Docker Compose and ECS deployments. This change simplifies the bootstrap process by running the `execute-patches` command in an init container before the main application starts, eliminating the need for a separate bootstrap host.
userImpact: Database bootstrapping is now handled automatically via init containers at startup, removing the need to manually manage the separate dbops endpoint.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1791: Introduce minimal org member role
summary: Introduces a minimal org member role that is automatically granted to all new users upon creation via the `post_organization_users` endpoint. The role includes limited permissions to read organization members and role bindings, allowing new users to see their own profile and role information without confusion or 404 errors. This role serves as a sensible default for any user joining an organization.
userImpact: New users automatically receive a minimal org member role with permissions to view their own information and organization members, eliminating 404 errors and providing better onboarding clarity.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1689 - update model APIs to accept & return a list of dataset IDs
summary: Model APIs now accept and return a list of dataset IDs instead of a single dataset ID. This change enables models to be associated with multiple datasets, with a new database join table managing the model-dataset relationships. On model onboarding, the aggregation matcher runs across all associated datasets and unions the results.
userImpact: Users can now create and update models with multiple associated datasets, allowing a single model to analyze data across multiple data sources.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.80 → 1.35.81), moto (5.0.22 → 5.0.23), and uvicorn (0.32.1 → 0.34.0). These are routine patch and minor version updates to keep dependencies current with upstream improvements and bug fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact—these are dependency maintenance updates that improve underlying library performance and compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1893 fix schema inference and metrics calculation on cols with spaces
summary: Schema inference and metrics calculation failed when column names contained spaces because identifiers were not properly quoted in SQL queries. This fix adds proper SQL identifier escaping throughout schema inference and all aggregation functions to handle column names like "MA History" correctly.
userImpact: Users can now perform schema inference and metrics calculations on datasets with spaces in column names without errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1890 fix raw data retrieval with nans
summary: Fixed raw data retrieval for Expel by encoding NaN values as strings instead of JSON-incompatible NaN literals. The frontend cannot parse NaN in JSON, so this change ensures data with NaN values can be properly serialized and deserialized without errors.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval now works correctly for datasets containing NaN values, eliminating failures that occurred when fetching data from Expel.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1798: Support updating users
summary: Split the user `name` field into separate `first_name` and `last_name` fields to match Keycloak's data model and support PATCH operations. Added new Keycloak scopes to include both fields in user tokens, and added support for the `picture` attribute. This change enables reliable user profile updates without edge cases around name splitting.
userImpact: Users can now update individual user profile fields (first name, last name, picture) via PATCH requests, and the API now returns first_name and last_name as separate fields instead of a combined name field.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1581 upsolve custom chart docs
summary: Added comprehensive user-facing documentation for configuring custom charts in dashboards. The guide includes step-by-step instructions, visual examples, and SQL configuration details to help users create and customize chart visualizations.
userImpact: Users can now reference detailed documentation to learn how to configure custom charts, including proper SQL query patterns and dashboard integration steps.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1896 fix binary classification dashboard
summary: The binary classification dashboard was omitting days with no data, causing gaps in metric graphs that confused the visual timeline. This MR updates the dashboard queries to use the time_bucket_gapfill function to properly fill missing data points with zeros, and fixes styling issues that caused graph overflow on certain curves.
userImpact: Users now see complete timeline continuity in binary classification dashboard graphs, with accurate zero-filled gaps for days with no inferences, and improved visual rendering without overflow.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3, cfn-lint, pytest-asyncio, and authzed/spicedb. These are routine patch and minor version updates to maintain dependency freshness and access bug fixes and minor improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] move data plane health check to port 7492 to prevent conflict with auth on 8080
summary: The data plane health check port has been moved from 8080 to 7492 to eliminate conflicts with Keycloak authentication services running on port 8080 in local development environments. This change simplifies local development setup and reduces port collision issues.
userImpact: Developers can now run Keycloak and the data plane health check simultaneously on localhost without port conflicts during local development.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1891 upgrade datasketches 5.1.1 to use prebuilt wheels for python 3.13
summary: Upgraded datasketches from a custom-built wheel to the official prebuilt wheel for Python 3.13, now available from the Apache datasketches project. This eliminates the need for custom build dependencies in Docker, allowing the project to rely exclusively on prebuilt wheels for all major dependencies (duckdb, pyarrow, and datasketches).
userImpact: Users benefit from faster builds, reduced build complexity, and official Apache-supported wheels for Python 3.13.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1892 fix bigquery connector
summary: Fixed BigQuery connector initialization that was incorrectly passing the full tuple returned by `load_credentials_from_dict` instead of just the credentials object. This was causing authentication failures when initializing the BigQuery client.
userImpact: BigQuery connector now correctly authenticates with Google Cloud credentials, resolving ValueError exceptions that prevented successful connections.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.78→1.35.79), opentelemetry-exporter-otlp (1.28.2→1.29.0), and opentelemetry-sdk (1.28.2→1.29.0). This routine maintenance update ensures the application benefits from the latest patches and minor versions of its dependencies.
userImpact: No direct user impact. These are standard dependency maintenance updates handled automatically.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1794 fix orphaned scheduled jobs when models are deleted
summary: Fixed orphaned scheduled jobs that persisted after model deletion. The model deletion logic now properly cleans up associated scheduled jobs, and the job schedule foreign key was moved to the job_schedules table to ensure every schedule maintains a valid link to its parent model.
userImpact: Users can now safely delete models without leaving behind orphaned scheduled jobs that would cause errors or consume unnecessary resources.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1799: Support reseting user credentials
summary: Added support for resetting user credentials in the authentication system. The implementation includes new API endpoints and logic to rotate user secrets across Keycloak and static configuration authentication backends, with corresponding authorization rules and data model updates.
userImpact: Users can now reset their credentials through the API, generating new authentication secrets that immediately invalidate previous ones.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1792: Lazily delete users
summary: Implemented lazy deletion for users to maintain data integrity while marking accounts as deleted. When a user is deleted, their tokens are immediately invalidated through JWK cache invalidation, preventing further API access even if deletion is still in progress. A new database table tracks deleted users, and authentication flows now check this table to deny access for soft-deleted accounts.
userImpact: Deleted user accounts are immediately prevented from making API requests, even if background deletion processes are still running.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.77 to 1.35.78 and cfn-lint from 1.21.0 to 1.22.0. These are routine dependency updates with no breaking changes—boto3 includes AWS service API documentation updates and cfn-lint includes minor enhancements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: automatically create aws deployment tag and also a success tag
summary: Added automatic AWS deployment pipeline that creates a deployment tag when a new release is created from main, triggering an AWS dev deployment. Upon successful completion, a success tag is automatically created to track the last successful version, which can be used to determine the baseline for future scheduled AWS Prod deployments.
userImpact: Releases to AWS dev are now automatically triggered and tracked with success tags, enabling better visibility and automation for subsequent production deployments.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: automatically bump the patch version if no release version is specified
summary: Added automatic patch version bumping in the deployment pipeline when no explicit release version is specified. This simplifies the release process by eliminating the need to manually specify version increments for patch releases.
userImpact: Users can now release patch versions without manually specifying a version number; the system automatically increments the patch version.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename expel_sts to expel-sts-platform
summary: Renamed the `expel_sts` environment to `expel-sts-platform` to establish consistent naming conventions across deployment commands and environment definitions. This affects deployment folder names and pipeline configuration, while the underlying stack names remain unchanged and no infrastructure recreation occurs.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal deployment naming consistency improved for development teams.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore: rename aws dev platform_v4 to aws-dev-platform to match aws-prod-platform
summary: Renamed the AWS development platform from `platform_v4` to `aws-dev-platform` to establish consistent naming conventions across deployment environments. This aligns the development platform name with the existing `aws-prod-platform` pattern, making deployment scripts and configuration easier to understand and maintain.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal deployment naming standardized for consistency.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1792: Fix db manager deletion bug
summary: Fixed a bug where user deletion flow failed to delete relationships in SpiceDB due to SQLAlchemy's object reference management. The issue occurred because `db_manager.flush()` moves objects from memory to the transaction buffer, causing the SpiceDB delete queue to be unexpectedly emptied even though deletions hadn't been committed. The fix enqueues copies of deleted objects instead of the objects themselves, ensuring deletions succeed even when `flush()` is called.
userImpact: Users can now be successfully deleted from groups, with their relationships properly removed from SpiceDB.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.76 → 1.35.77), cfn-lint (1.20.2 → 1.21.0), and authzed/spicedb (1.38.1 → 1.39.0). These are routine dependency updates to incorporate bug fixes and minor feature additions from upstream projects.

title: UP-1881 fix runaway scheduled jobs
summary: Fixed runaway scheduled jobs that were retrying indefinitely when their associated models were deleted or had invalid configurations. The system now detects non-retriable errors and stops job retries by capping the max_attempts to the current attempt number. A new PATCH endpoint was added to allow manual updates to a job's max retries for administrative control.
userImpact: Users can now prevent scheduled jobs from consuming excessive resources and crashing the API due to unrecoverable errors, while maintaining job history for audit purposes.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 fix app plane health check

author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1880 data plane health check
summary: Added a health check endpoint to the data plane service that allows ECS to detect deadlock conditions and trigger restarts. The fix also wraps API calls in exception handlers to prevent crashes from propagating and marks JobRunner threads as daemon processes to ensure clean shutdown when the main thread fails.
userImpact: Data plane service now automatically restarts when it enters an unresponsive state, improving system reliability and preventing indefinite hangs.

title: UP-1589 - Lint housekeeping
summary: This PR addresses multiple linting and type safety issues across the codebase. Changes include making the `temporary` field on PostConnectorSpec default to False, adding discriminators to JobSpec types for proper deserialization (requiring an openapi-generator version upgrade), making AlertCheckJobSpec start/end times non-optional, and fixing unsafe access patterns in AlertsResponse and various import issues across executors. Connectors and data fetching now properly validate and throw exceptions when encountering virtual datasets without locators.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal code quality and type safety improvements that strengthen error handling and prevent runtime issues.

title: UP-1680 - Make GET jobs endpoint faster
summary: Fixed an N+1 query problem in the GET jobs endpoint where each polymorphic job spec was fetched with a separate database query. Implemented proper SQLAlchemy selectin_polymorphic loading to fetch all columns for derived types in a single query, reducing endpoint response time from 500-1000ms to near-instant.
userImpact: The project activity logs now load significantly faster, eliminating the 500-1000ms slowdown users experienced when retrieving jobs.
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Further reduce role binding api endpoint latencies
summary: Optimized role binding API endpoint performance by applying efficient join and query patterns to all GET operations on role bindings, not just list endpoints. This reduces latency from ~8 seconds to ~4 seconds on dev infrastructure, with production improvements expected to bring sub-300ms response times when accounting for lower network latencies.
userImpact: Users will experience significantly faster response times when retrieving role bindings, particularly for organizations with large group sizes and user counts.

title: NO-REF - Update expel data to be less skewed
summary: Updated the Expel test data generation to incorporate hourly variation in addition to daily variation, reducing skew in the synthetic prediction ranges. Previously, the data generated a narrow range [X, X + 0.1] per day, which resulted in entire days being classified into a single class after thresholding. The fix incorporates hour-level granularity to create more realistic and balanced class distributions in test data.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this improves the quality and balance of test data used for integration testing.

title: UP-1566 Expel Onboarding scripts
summary: Refactored SDK authentication to support both client credentials and device-based user authentication via browser, with automatic token refresh and thread-safe implementations. Added onboarding scripts for Expel models with schema inference and a fix for Keycloak CLI client mapper configuration in newer environments.
userImpact: Users and external partners like Expel can now authenticate with the SDK using either client credentials or browser-based device flow, and onboarding scripts enable streamlined model setup with automatic schema detection.

title: UP-1819 Fix schema inference for timestamp types, and add auto tagging for primary timestamp
summary: Fixed schema inference for DuckDB timestamp columns by adding proper type mapping to Arthur's schema types. Additionally implemented automatic tagging of singular timestamp columns as PRIMARY_TIMESTAMP to reduce manual configuration overhead during data onboarding.
userImpact: Users can now successfully ingest datasets with timestamp columns, and single timestamp columns are automatically tagged as PRIMARY_TIMESTAMP during schema inference.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1695 - Add MAE aggregation function
summary: Added Mean Absolute Error (MAE) regression metric to the aggregation functions library. For each 5-minute interval, the function sums absolute errors and counts data points, reporting both as separate metrics with aligned timestamps. At query time, daily/hourly/etc buckets can be constructed using SUM(absolute_error) / SUM(count).
userImpact: Users can now compute and aggregate Mean Absolute Error metrics for regression model evaluation across configurable time intervals.
author: peter496

title: UP-1819 fix bucket based schema inspection
summary: Fixed bucket-based connectors (S3, GCS) to allow schema inspection and data reading when a primary timestamp tag is not present, provided pagination options are configured. The logic now only warns if the timestamp tag is missing during data reads, and only fails if both the tag is absent and pagination is not set, aligning behavior with the BigQuery connector.
userImpact: Users can now successfully read datasets from bucket-based connectors without requiring a primary timestamp tag, as long as pagination options are set.

title: UP-1702 - Refactor aggregations to take a dataset reference as argument rather...
summary: Refactored aggregations to accept a DatasetReference object instead of an encoded table name string. The metrics_calculation_executor now converts dataset IDs into DatasetReference objects at calculation time, which contain the dataset name, ID, and table name. An Alembic migration updates existing metric configurations to use the new reference-based approach.
userImpact: Users' existing metric configurations are automatically migrated to the new dataset reference format, with improved clarity in aggregation parameter definitions.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to v33
summary: Updated the Faker dependency from v32.1.0 to v33.0.0. This major version update reverts a previous change that made pytest fixtures session-scoped, restoring the default fixture scope behavior.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.60 to 1.35.62 (patch) and PostgreSQL from 16.4 to 16.5 (minor). These are routine dependency updates that include minor AWS API enhancements and PostgreSQL maintenance improvements with no breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF - Reuse threadpool per day
summary: Modified the bucket-based connector to reuse the threadpool across daily time increments instead of reinitializing it on each increment. This optimization reduces unnecessary threadpool creation overhead and improves performance when processing data across multiple days.
userImpact: Users will experience faster data processing when the bucket-based connector handles multiple days of data, as threadpool initialization is now cached per day rather than per increment.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates two non-major dependencies: boto3 from 1.35.59 to 1.35.60 and python-keycloak from 4.7.0 to 4.7.1. These are routine patch updates that include AWS SDK API improvements and bug fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1754 - Break connector pagination options off from filters
summary: Pagination parameters (page, page_size, limit) are now separated from data filters in connector read operations. This eliminates the need for connectors to repeatedly parse pagination from filter arguments and clarifies when default limits apply. Pagination is now required on fetch data jobs, and the new ConnectorPaginationOptions model enforces limits: user-defined page sizes capped at 500 and default limit increased to 25 (from 15).
userImpact: Connectors now have cleaner, more predictable pagination behavior with enforced limits and a standardized interface that accepts pagination parameters separately from data filters.
author: peter496

title: UP-1755 - Add inference counts by class aggregation functions
summary: Adds two new aggregation functions for binary classifier inference count analysis. BinaryClassifierCountByClassAggregationFunction processes binary classifier predictions (int/bool/str) and outputs count by prediction value, while BinaryClassifierCountThresholdAggregationFunction creates pseudo-predictions from float columns using configurable true/false labels for the same aggregation.
userImpact: Users can now aggregate and analyze inference counts grouped by predicted class for binary classification models, with support for both direct prediction columns and threshold-based prediction generation from float scores.
author: peter496

title: feat: sts expel
summary: Added STS Expel deployment configuration and infrastructure as code for a new Expel deployment environment. This includes CloudFormation stack modifications for IAM, VPC, and load balancing, along with deployment scripts and CI/CD pipeline updates to support the new expel_sts environment alongside the existing platform_v4 infrastructure.
userImpact: Users can now deploy Scope infrastructure to the Expel STS environment using the new deployment configuration and pipeline automation.
author: jdb-arthur

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to v32
summary: Updated the Faker test dependency from v30.8.2 to v32.1.0, including pytest fixture improvements and expanded locale support. This is a routine dependency update with no breaking changes to the application.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.35.58 → 1.35.59), cfn-lint (1.18.4 → 1.19.0), fastapi (0.115.4 → 0.115.5), and pydantic_core (2.26.0 → 2.27.0). These updates bring the latest AWS API changes, CloudFormation linting improvements, and Pydantic compatibility fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1680 - Add support for aws log collecting
summary: Added AWS CloudWatch log collection support to the Arthur Scope platform by integrating OpenTelemetry (OTEL) collector for traces and metrics. The implementation includes telemetry sidecar deployment alongside the app-plane service, CloudFormation infrastructure updates for both old and new stack templates, and configuration management for multiple environments including Expel STS and Platform V4.
userImpact: Users can now collect and export application telemetry data to AWS CloudWatch, enabling better observability and monitoring of the Arthur Scope application plane in production and development environments.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF - Fix integration tests, scheduled job code blocking manual metric runs
summary: Fixed a critical bug where metric schedule validation code was running even when no schedule was defined, blocking manual metric runs. Also corrected integration tests that were attempting to apply unsupported filter operations on table columns.
userImpact: Users can now run metrics calculations manually without being blocked by schedule validation errors, and integration tests execute successfully.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.57 to 1.35.58 and google-cloud-bigquery from 3.26.0 to 3.27.0. The boto3 patch includes minor API updates to CloudFront, Inspector2, Lambda (Python 3.13 support), OpenSearch, and Outposts services. The google-cloud-bigquery minor version adds support for setting max_stream_count.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance to keep packages current with latest improvements and API enhancements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated patch-level dependencies including boto3, OpenTelemetry instrumentation packages, and OpenTelemetry SDK. This routine maintenance release incorporates upstream bug fixes and minor improvements across AWS SDK and observability libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1704 - Convert get_jobs state filter to states to allow IN filter
summary: The `get_jobs` state filter parameter has been converted from a single-value filter to a multi-value `states` parameter that supports IN-style filtering. Users can now query jobs with multiple states in a single request (e.g., `state IN (QUEUED, RUNNING)`) instead of being limited to exact state matches. This is a breaking change for API clients that currently use the `state` parameter.
userImpact: Users can now filter jobs by multiple states simultaneously using the new `states` parameter with IN filter syntax, but must update clients currently using the `state` parameter.
author: peter496

title: UP-1680 - Optimize role bindings respository functions
summary: Optimized role bindings repository queries by adding explicit `selectinload` statements to reduce N+1 query problems and adding limit optimization to frequently-called functions. The original query was fetching excessive related data across multiple joins, causing performance degradation in role binding retrieval operations.
userImpact: Role binding queries now execute more efficiently with reduced database load and faster response times for operations that retrieve user role assignments.
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.56
summary: Bumped boto3 dependency from v1.35.55 to v1.35.56. This patch release includes upstream API improvements to autoscaling, bedrock, cleanrooms, quicksight, resource-explorer-2, and synthetics services via botocore updates.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1560: Raw data retrieval for bucket based connectors
summary: Implemented raw data retrieval for bucket-based connectors, refactoring the data retrieval pipeline to support direct access to raw data stored in S3 or equivalent bucket systems. This simplifies data access for bucket-based connectors by removing unnecessary abstraction layers and improving efficiency.
userImpact: Users can now retrieve raw data directly from bucket-based connectors without intermediate processing steps.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1431 - Add PUT job state permissions to frontend users for job cancellations
summary: Frontend users now have access to the put_job_state operation, enabling the addition of a cancel job button in the UI. This permission grant allows users to cancel jobs directly from the frontend interface.
userImpact: Users can now cancel jobs using a dedicated cancel button in the frontend application.
author: peter496

title: UP-1560: support pagination for bucket-based connectors
summary: Added pagination support for bucket-based connectors (such as S3), allowing incremental retrieval of inference data in batches rather than loading entire datasets at once. This implementation performs post-filtering on read files to balance performance with memory efficiency.
userImpact: Users can now paginate through large inference datasets from bucket-based connectors, enabling more efficient querying and reduced memory consumption for large-scale data retrieval.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.35.55
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.35.54 to v1.35.55. This patch release includes AWS API updates for CodeBuild, GuardDuty, Lake Formation, Q Apps, S3 Control, and Verified Permissions services, along with protocol enhancements in botocore.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a routine dependency update that may enable access to new AWS service APIs and features when boto3 is used.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1585: Support BigQuery Connector
summary: This MR adds support for BigQuery as a new connector type in the Arthur Scope system. It includes the complete implementation across both app-plane and data-plane components, with database migrations, connector schemas, and comprehensive test coverage. A workaround was implemented to address build compatibility issues with the previous approach for Python 3.13 on amd64.
userImpact: Users can now connect BigQuery datasets to Arthur Scope for data monitoring and analysis.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve enable editing
summary: Upsolve backend service upgraded to the latest version with editing functionality enabled. Changes include updates to database connection configurations and Dockerfile to support the new Upsolve backend capabilities.
userImpact: Users can now edit data in Upsolve with the upgraded backend service.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: authzed (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) and opentelemetry-sdk (1.27.0 → 1.28.0). These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep the project current with minor versions.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Routine dependency updates maintain compatibility and receive upstream improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1705 - Add friendly names, descriptions to metrics parameters
summary: Added friendly names and descriptions to aggregation function parameters across the metrics system. This metadata will enable the UI to display more user-friendly parameter names and provide helpful explanations, improving the experience when configuring metrics.
userImpact: Users can now see human-readable names and descriptions for metrics aggregation parameters when using the UI.
author: peter496

title: UP-1545 - Add flag to connector schema indicating if list datasets is supported
summary: Added a new flag to the connector schema to indicate whether list datasets is supported by the connector. This flag allows the UI to determine whether to run the list datasets job and display available datasets, or instead prompt the user to manually create datasets. The change includes a database migration, updated API models, and bootstrapped values for existing connector schemas.
userImpact: Users can now create datasets through either automatic dataset listing (when supported) or manual dataset creation, depending on the connector's capabilities.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates five non-major dependencies to their latest versions, including alembic (1.13.3→1.14.0), cfn-lint (1.18.3→1.18.4), duckdb (1.1.2→1.1.3), moto (5.0.19→5.0.20), and pydantic_core (2.25.1→2.26.0). The updates are primarily patch versions with one minor version bump for alembic, bringing security patches and bug fixes from upstream projects.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream bug fixes and improvements, including duckdb's enhanced CSV scanning and adaptive sniffer for single-row files.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] fix dataset locator migration
summary: The dataset locator schema migration was incorrectly depending on connector instances in the `connector_specs` table instead of connector types in `connector_spec_schemas`. This caused the migration to skip populating `dataset_locator_spec_schemas` for connector types without existing connector instances. The fix now populates the table for all connector types that exist.
userImpact: Dataset locator schema configurations are now properly migrated for all connector types, regardless of whether connector instances currently exist.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1534 - Upsolve Binary Classifier Dashboard
summary: Added integration test and test data for the Upsolve Binary Classifier Dashboard, which provides visualization of inference counts, prediction distributions by class, and confusion matrix metrics over time. The dashboard includes optimized queries using time_bucket_gapfill for cleaner timeline visualization and line charts for better readability of model performance trends.
userImpact: Users can now view and monitor binary classifier model performance through a comprehensive dashboard displaying inference trends, class distributions, and confusion matrix rates with proper time-series visualization.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Routine dependency updates across the project including minor version bumps for spicedb and python-keycloak, plus patch updates for boto3, moto, numpy, and types-psutil. These updates maintain compatibility while incorporating upstream improvements, bug fixes, and security patches from the respective projects.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1556 - Add to test dataset, add integration test, fix tests
summary: Added more realistic test data to the networking dataset with increased pattern variation, nulls, and reduced uniformity. Added integration test to verify binary classifier metrics loading to Timescale, and fixed related integration test failures caused by the dataset changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1533: Support GCS connector
summary: Added support for Google Cloud Storage (GCS) as a connector option alongside existing S3 support. The implementation includes GCS connector schemas in both the app plane and data plane, with unit and integration tests, plus refactored shared test helpers to reduce code duplication.
userImpact: Users can now configure and use Google Cloud Storage as a data connector source, with the same bucket-based connector workflow as S3.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates three non-major dependencies: Faker (patch), boto3 (patch), and pglast (minor). The updates include bug fixes in Faker, new AWS API features in boto3, and improvements in pglast. These are routine dependency maintenance updates with no breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependencies are updated to their latest non-major versions for improved stability and new AWS service features.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1579 - Update nested gitlab ci to use new variable name
summary: Updated variable references in the nested GitLab CI configuration to use the new variable name. This fixes previously missed references that were using the old variable name convention.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: [no-ref] update keycloak
summary: Keycloak dependency has been upgraded to incorporate bug fixes and improvements from arthur-auth upstream. This update pulls in fixes that address authentication and user experience issues tracked in UP-1717 and XP-1161.
userImpact: Users benefit from bug fixes and improvements in Keycloak authentication functionality.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1579 - Python 3.13 upgrade
summary: Upgraded Python runtime from 3.12 to 3.13 across app_plane and data_plane services. Updated Dockerfiles, local development setup scripts, and CI configuration to use Python 3.13, with all unit tests, integration tests, and client generation verified as working.
userImpact: Users running local development or Docker deployments now use Python 3.13, gaining access to latest Python language features and performance improvements.
author: peter496

title: [no-ref] Upgrade pglast 6.9
summary: Upgraded pglast from version 6.8 to 6.9, which includes a fix for the breaking format change introduced in the previous version. This upgrade allows us to revert the test workarounds that were temporarily added to handle the format inconsistency.
userImpact: Users benefit from pglast's improved SQL parsing stability and can rely on consistent query formatting behavior.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.35.51 to 1.35.52 and cfn-lint from 1.18.2 to 1.18.3. These are patch-level dependency updates that bring AWS API support enhancements (AppSync Event APIs, DataSync Enhanced mode, Amazon Location services, and others) and cfn-lint improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency maintenance updates for improved AWS service support and linting capabilities.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1530: S3 connector support
summary: Added comprehensive support for S3 and GCS bucket-based connectors, enabling users to connect to cloud storage buckets and read data directly from them. The implementation includes connector schema definitions, dataset locator validation, and optimized multi-worker file reading that reduces job execution time from 45 minutes to 6 minutes. This foundational work enables the platform to support cloud-native data sources alongside existing connectors.
userImpact: Users can now configure and connect to S3 and GCS bucket connectors, with dramatically faster data reading performance through parallel file processing.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1657 - Add log emitted by job agent for reporting job failure
summary: The job agent now logs an error message to the job when it fails the job during shutdown. This provides visibility into why a job was terminated, particularly in cases of hardware preemption events. The change includes updates to the job agent logic and corresponding unit test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now see detailed error logs in their failed jobs explaining the reason for termination, such as hardware preemption events.
author: peter496

title: UP-1599 - Handle SpiceDB writes in batch fashion
summary: SpiceDB write operations (creates, deletes, etc.) are now performed in batch fashion rather than individually. This improves performance by reducing the number of round trips to the authorization system. The batching logic has been centralized as a function call within the DBManager class.
userImpact: SpiceDB authorization operations complete faster and more efficiently through batched writes.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies: pglast (6.2→6.8), protobuf (5.28.2→5.28.3), and pydantic_core (2.25.0→2.25.1). Adjusted test assertions to match formatting changes introduced in pglast 6.7's SQL parser output.
userImpact: Users benefit from latest bug fixes and improvements in pglast, protobuf, and pydantic_core dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency types-croniter to v4
summary: Updated the types-croniter type stub package from v3.0.3.20240731 to v4.0.0.20241030, bringing the latest type annotations for the croniter library. This dependency update ensures compatibility with current type checking standards and includes any improvements or changes in the croniter type stubs.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved type checking accuracy and compatibility with the latest croniter type definitions when using type checkers like mypy.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency croniter to v5
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v4.0.0 to v5.0.1. This major version update reintroduces support for 7 as a valid DayOfWeek value in cron expressions, deviating from standard cron behavior in response to community requests.
userImpact: Cron expressions can now use 7 to represent Sunday (in addition to the standard 0), providing greater compatibility with common cron syntax variants.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated 24+ non-major dependencies across all project modules to their latest patch versions. This includes critical libraries like SQLAlchemy (2.0.29→2.0.36), FastAPI (0.115.0→0.115.4), boto3 (1.35.0→1.35.51), and pandas (2.2.2→2.2.3), along with security and stability patches for templating engines, YAML parsing, authentication, and cloud integration libraries.
userImpact: Users benefit from security patches, bug fixes, and stability improvements across database drivers, API frameworks, cloud SDKs, and data processing libraries.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1718 upgrade auth stack to keycloak 25
summary: Upgraded the authentication stack to Keycloak 25 and python-keycloak 4.0, addressing breaking changes in the new client library and API. The upgrade includes fixes for Keycloak 25's PUT semantics (instead of PATCH) for user updates, addition of a user profile schema to manage the `user_type` attribute in the admin UI, and refactored initialization logic to account for the new client's lazy connection behavior.
userImpact: Users can now operate with the latest Keycloak 25 version, and the `user_type` attribute is now properly managed and visible in the Keycloak admin interface.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1554 - Add POST available_dataset endpoint
summary: Added a new POST endpoint to manually onboard available datasets for connectors where bulk repopulation is not applicable. This provides users with granular control over dataset onboarding. Additionally, removed an unused ID parameter from the PutAvailableDataset model that the API never honored.
userImpact: Users can now manually onboard individual available datasets per connector without needing to wipe and repopulate all datasets.
author: peter496

title: UP-465 - Add true/false flag to available datasets, datasets, models to...
summary: Added an `arthur_shield_dataset` boolean flag to available datasets, datasets, and models to indicate whether a dataset is an Arthur Shield dataset. The Shield connector now filters to only output available datasets with this flag set to true, and at model creation time, the flag is derived from the dataset and set permanently. This enables the UI to properly identify and distinguish Shield-based models and datasets.
userImpact: Users can now see which datasets and models are Arthur Shield datasets through the `arthur_shield_dataset` indicator, allowing them to better understand and manage their Shield-based configurations.
author: peter496

title: Revert "Bumped version for upsolve to 8035 in scope-version-manifest.json"
summary: Reverted the version bump for upsolve to 8035 because the previous upgrade was missing required migrations in the upsolve image. This ensures the service maintains a stable version until migrations are properly included.
userImpact: Users will continue using a stable upsolve version that includes all necessary migrations, preventing potential service failures from incomplete upgrades.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1713 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded the upsolve service to the latest backend release. This brings in the most recent improvements and updates to the upsolve component used by the system.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest upsolve service improvements and bug fixes included in the newest backend release.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] lock dependencies to prevent ambiguity now that we have renovate bot
summary: Pinned all dependency versions explicitly across requirements.txt and setup.py files to prevent version mismatches between CI/CD and local environments. This ensures consistency and eliminates ambiguity now that Renovate bot manages dependency updates automatically.
userImpact: Users and CI/CD now use consistent, locked dependency versions, preventing subtle bugs from version drift.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] add docs for docker login in readme setup
summary: Added documentation to the README files instructing developers how to authenticate their Docker daemons with the Arthur Docker 1Password user. This is necessary now that the Upsolve container is a project dependency.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency croniter to v4
summary: Updated croniter dependency from v3.0.3 to v4.0.0. This major version release removes the DayOfWeek alias that mapped 7 to 0 to align with standard cron behavior, and includes a fix for DOW range calculations when the last day falls on Sunday.
userImpact: Cron expression evaluation now adheres strictly to standard cron conventions, particularly for day-of-week calculations.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated pytest-httpx from 0.32.0 to 0.33.0. This release adds Python 3.13 support, introduces a `should_mock` callable option for conditional mocking, adds `match_files`, `match_data`, and `match_extensions` parameters for more flexible request matching, and replaces the deprecated `non_mocked_hosts` option.
userImpact: Users can now use Python 3.13 with pytest-httpx and have access to more flexible request matching options via `should_mock`, `match_files`, `match_data`, and `match_extensions` parameters.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1538 - Expel Binary Classification Performance Metrics
summary: This MR introduces confusion matrix aggregation functions to calculate binary classification performance metrics (TP/TN/FP/FN counts). Three variants are implemented to handle different input types: bool/int predictions with bool/int ground truth, string labels with manual true/false value parameters, and float predictions with a configurable threshold applied against bool/int ground truth. A new networking security test dataset was added and a bug in ParameterSchema's optional field derivation was fixed.
userImpact: Users can now compute binary classification confusion matrix metrics directly within aggregation queries across multiple input data types without manual preprocessing.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated redis-py from 5.1.1 to 5.2.0, adding support for the scorer argument in AggregateRequest. This minor version upgrade includes new features for Redis search operations and maintenance improvements.
userImpact: Users can now use the scorer argument when creating AggregateRequest objects in redis-py for more flexible Redis search scoring.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] correct dataset onboarding flow
summary: The dataset onboarding flow was missing a critical step to fetch raw data for manually created datasets, preventing users from confirming their configuration was correct. This fix restores the missing data fetch step in the onboarding workflow.
userImpact: Users manually creating datasets can now see their raw data fetched and displayed during onboarding to verify their dataset is configured correctly.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1593: move data retrieval to dataset level
summary: Data retrieval operations and fetch data job specs have been migrated to operate at the dataset level instead of the model level. This includes SpiceDB authorization updates to migrate old model-level data retrieval permissions to new dataset-level permissions. Additional endpoints for available dataset fetch data jobs and data retrieval operations are planned in follow-up work.
userImpact: Data retrieval operations and fetch data jobs now operate at the dataset level rather than the model level; frontends will need to be updated to reflect this structural change in how data retrieval is accessed and managed.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1679 - Properly convey exit code from dataplane processes
summary: JobExecutor was catching all job exceptions and returning a completed state regardless of outcome, causing the process to always exit with code 0. The executor is now wrapped to properly propagate exit codes when jobs fail, ensuring the dataplane process reflects the actual job status.
userImpact: Dataplane processes now exit with the correct status code based on job success or failure, enabling proper error detection and handling in downstream systems.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Revert FE image to pre-localization bug
summary: Reverted the frontend image to a version prior to a localization bug that was impacting the demo. This change restores the previous stable version of the frontend component.
userImpact: Users will no longer experience the localization-related issues that were present in the affected frontend version.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated mypy from 1.12.0 to 1.13.0 via automated dependency management. This is a minor version update to the type checker that the development team relies on for code quality assurance.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1412 full onboarding diagram
summary: Added a comprehensive diagram documenting the complete resource onboarding workflow, including the ability to reuse existing resources and modular step design. The diagram captures new onboarding capabilities needed for tabular MVP: non-listable data sources, joined/multiple datasets, dataset schema editing, and metrics config editing.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1538 - Change aggregation function contract to emit more than one metric
summary: Changed the aggregation function contract so that each function can now emit multiple metrics instead of being limited to a single series output. Aggregation functions now return a set of metrics for each calculation, enabling multi-metric aggregations like confusion matrices that can report multiple statistical measures (TP, FP, TN, FN, etc.) from a single function invocation.
userImpact: Users can now use aggregation functions that emit multiple related metrics in a single calculation, laying the groundwork for more sophisticated multi-output metrics like confusion matrices.
author: peter496

title: Revert FE image to yesterday morning's build
summary: Reverted the frontend image to a previous build from yesterday morning to address an issue discovered before a demo. This is a temporary operational change to the scope-version-manifest.json.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies to their latest minor versions: Faker (~=30.8.0), cfn-lint (~=1.18.1), and types-psutil (~=6.1.0.20241022). These updates include bug fixes and new locale support from upstream projects.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream improvements including updated city names for Ukrainian locale in Faker and new Gujarati locale support.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1650 - Reduce fetch data memory requirements for improved latency
summary: Reduced memory requirements for data retrieval jobs to fit within thread execution thresholds instead of requiring new process spinup. This eliminates 4-6 seconds of latency overhead by avoiding expensive process initialization for these quick, latency-sensitive operations.
userImpact: Data retrieval jobs now execute with significantly lower latency, improving overall application responsiveness.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF - Bump memory too
summary: Adjusted Fargate memory allocation to match CPU resources (4 CPU + 8GB memory) and comply with AWS Fargate pod configuration requirements. A previous update increased CPU without corresponding memory, causing a resource ratio mismatch.
userImpact: Fargate pods now run with the correct CPU-to-memory ratio, ensuring compliance with AWS requirements and stable pod execution.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Temporarily up dataplane resources
summary: Increased dataplane CPU resources in the development environment to investigate and potentially reduce job latency. This is a temporary measure to gather performance data before deciding on a permanent resource allocation strategy.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 alerts improvements
summary: Added comprehensive technical documentation for metrics preaggregation and alerting system design, including visual diagrams to illustrate current drawbacks and proposed improvements. The documentation explores challenges around handling multiple metric values across different versions within the same time bucket and discusses aggregation strategies used by similar systems.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This change provides internal technical documentation to guide future improvements to the alerting and metrics systems.
author: ianmcgraw

title: XP-1145 - Update Shield connector configs to use query optimization results
summary: Updated Shield connector configurations to use query optimization results from the upstream Shield API. Now uses `include_count=False` to avoid expensive count queries and increases the default page size from 900 to 1500 to take advantage of Shield's improved performance. This reduces job execution time by approximately 44% (from 731 seconds to 407 seconds).
userImpact: Shield data loading jobs now complete significantly faster, improving overall system responsiveness and throughput.
author: peter496

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated fsspec dependency from ~=2024.9.0 to ~=2024.10.0 as part of routine non-major dependency maintenance. This is a minor version bump of the filesystem_spec library.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add 5 minute validation to lookback period
summary: Added validation to enforce that the lookback period is aligned to 5-minute boundaries, consistent with the cron frequency alignment established in a previous MR. This validation ensures the system reads data from properly aligned start times, preventing potential data consistency issues even though the UI currently only accepts hourly input.
userImpact: Invalid lookback periods that are not aligned to 5-minute intervals will now be rejected, ensuring data integrity in scheduled model monitoring.
author: peter496

title: UP-1590 - Small refactors to scheduled jobs, metrics calculation executors, add some tests
summary: Refactored scheduled jobs and metrics calculation executor classes to improve testability and clarity. Added comprehensive unit tests for both executors and validators, plus fixes to job scheduling logic to correctly calculate start timestamps from lookback periods.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1513 fix grafana query for toxicity latency
summary: The Grafana query for the Toxicity Rule Median latency metric has been corrected to properly merge sketches before calculating the median value. This ensures accurate latency reporting for toxicity detection rules in the Shield dashboard.
userImpact: Users will now see accurate median latency values for toxicity detection rules in the Grafana dashboard, fixing previously incorrect metric calculations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1617: Update job create logic to have queued time match ready time for future jobs
summary: Updated job creation logic to set the queued timestamp to match the ready time for scheduled (future) jobs instead of using the creation time. This fixes a frontend display issue where scheduled jobs were incorrectly sorted below jobs with earlier queued times, even when scheduled to run at the same time.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs now display in the correct chronological order on the frontend, grouped with other jobs running at the same time rather than appearing buried under earlier jobs.
author: peter496

title: UP-1538 - Refactor aggregation parameter parsing, definition, api models
summary: Refactored aggregation parameter parsing to split the monolithic ScopeMetricParameter object into separate type-specific definitions for datasets, column references, literals, and group-by dimensions. This simplifies the API model schema and reduces complexity for frontend integration. No aggregation function names or values were changed, and no database migration is required.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is an internal refactor that improves code maintainability and API clarity for future feature development.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed dashboard issues in Upsolve by updating the dashboard configuration and creating a new export to address toxicity and latency graph problems. This resolves display and data accuracy issues in the Upsolve dashboard monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now view accurate toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard without display or data issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1609 fix upsolve dash
summary: Fixed toxicity and latency graphs in the Upsolve dashboard by applying a new database patch. The patch addresses issues affecting data visualization and system performance in the dashboard.
userImpact: Users can now see accurate toxicity and latency data visualization in the Upsolve dashboard without graph display issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Change cron validation logic
summary: Fixed cron expression validation logic to correctly verify that job intervals are aligned to 5-minute boundaries and occur at least 5 minutes apart. Previously, expressions like `*/7` were incorrectly validated as passing the 5-minute threshold even though they created unaligned intervals (e.g., 9:56 and 10:00 as separate 7th-minute occurrences). The new logic parses cron expressions and validates that all subsequent intervals are evenly divisible by 5 minutes and properly aligned.
userImpact: Cron-scheduled jobs now correctly reject improperly aligned or insufficiently-spaced intervals, preventing misconfigured schedules from being accepted by the system.
author: peter496

title: UP-1609 bump upsolve image
summary: Updated the upsolve Docker image to the latest version. This is a routine dependency update to ensure the service uses the most current base image.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat: new stack deployments
summary: Adds automated deployment pipeline and CI jobs to enable new stack deployments and on-premises installations for customers. Includes CloudFormation stacks for core infrastructure, databases (RDS, TimescaleDB, Redis), and application services (Auth, SpiceDB, Upsolve, App Plane, Data Plane, Frontend, Grafana), along with deployment scripts and environment configuration for orchestrated provisioning.
userImpact: Customers can now deploy and manage complete Scope stacks on-premises or in new AWS environments using automated scripts and configuration files, reducing manual deployment effort.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated three non-major dependencies: Faker to v30.6.0 (adding passport provider for ru_RU locale), cfn-lint to v1.17.1 (minor improvements), and psutil to v6.1.0 (significantly improving process_iter() performance on Windows and adding pytest support). These updates bring performance improvements and extended locale support to the project's dependencies.
userImpact: Users benefit from faster process iteration on Windows systems and expanded support for Russian passport data generation through updated dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Add back dataplane stack
summary: Restored the dataplane stack configuration in the CloudFormation root scope template. This change modifies the infrastructure-as-code definition to reinstate the dataplane stack that had been previously removed.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Recreate Dataplane stack
summary: The Dataplane stack was stuck referencing a non-existent autoscaling policy, preventing deployment. This fix removes the problematic Dataplane stack definition and temporarily removes its outputs from the CloudFormation configuration, allowing CloudFormation to cleanly delete the stuck resources and prepare for a fresh redeployment.
userImpact: Resolves deployment failures caused by the Dataplane stack referencing missing autoscaling policies, enabling the infrastructure to be redeployed cleanly.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Up autoscaling thresholds
summary: Autoscaling thresholds have been increased from 10% to 25% CPU utilization to better accommodate parallelized job workloads and reduce unnecessary scaling churn. With lower thresholds, rapid bursts from scheduled job batches were triggering cascading scale-up events before scale-down periods could complete, causing worker counts to exceed configured limits.
userImpact: The dataplane now scales more efficiently and reliably under recurring job loads, preventing worker count overages and better utilizing available CPU resources.
author: peter496

title: Revert "UP-1609 update timescale connection type"
summary: Reverted a previous update to the Timescale connection type configuration in response to guidance from the team. The change restores the connection type to its previous state in the connections configuration file.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1609 update timescale connection type
summary: Updated the Timescale database connection type to use the correct connection type identifier. This ensures proper configuration and connectivity for Timescale database integrations.
userImpact: Timescale database connections will now use the correct connection type for proper integration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated Faker to v30.4.0 and uvicorn to v0.32.0. These are non-major dependency updates that improve locale support for Hindi names and add Python 3.13 support and request limit warnings in uvicorn.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1518 - Add graceful termination for ECS tasks
summary: Implements graceful termination handling for ECS tasks during shutdown. When an ECS task receives a SIGTERM, the job agent now stops accepting new jobs, allows running jobs a configurable grace period (default 15 seconds) to complete, then cleanly shuts down any remaining jobs and threads. The job agent now reports job completion/failure status to the app plane rather than relying on individual processes to self-report, improving reliability during graceful shutdowns.
userImpact: Jobs in flight during ECS task termination are now handled gracefully, with a grace period for completion and proper status reporting, preventing orphaned jobs and race conditions during deployment or scaling events.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1609] upgrade upsolve image
summary: Upgraded the upsolve Docker image to a newer version and fixed a timescale connection issue. The changes include updating the Dockerfile and adjusting connection configuration in the database patches.
userImpact: Users benefit from improvements and bug fixes included in the updated upsolve image, including corrected timescale database connectivity.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix local dev upsolve set up
summary: Fixed a 401 Unauthorized error that occurred when attempting to retrieve the Upsolve token during local development. The issue was in the docker-compose setup configuration for the Upsolve service integration.
userImpact: Local development environments can now successfully authenticate with the Upsolve service and retrieve tenant tokens without encountering authorization errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updates non-major dependencies including authzed/spicedb from v1.37.0 to v1.37.1 and mypy from ~=1.11.1 to ~=1.12.0. These are routine dependency updates to keep the project current with security patches and minor improvements.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1592: Add dataset locator validations and unit tests
summary: Added comprehensive validations for dataset locators to ensure created, available, and patched datasets conform to schema requirements. This includes new validation logic in a dedicated datasets operator, unit tests covering various validation scenarios, and schema updates across dataset and connector APIs to enforce data integrity constraints.
userImpact: Users can now reliably create and update datasets with confidence that validation errors will clearly identify schema mismatches before datasets are persisted.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1601 - Add correction for 0 indexing shield connector pages
summary: Fixed page indexing mismatch between Shield connector and Scope, where Shield uses 0-based indexing while Scope expects 1-based indexing. The Shield connector now applies a correction to convert Scope's 1-based page numbers to Shield's 0-based format.
userImpact: Shield connector pagination now works correctly with Scope's 1-based indexing standard, eliminating off-by-one errors in page retrieval.
author: peter496

title: UP-1600 - UP-1654 - UP-1430 - Move SQLAlchemy code to 2.0 style from legacy style, remove issue of ambiguous sorting
summary: Migrated all SQLAlchemy queries in the repositories layer from legacy 1.4 style to 2.0 style syntax, a prerequisite for enabling async support in the future. Additionally, implemented automatic default ordering by `id DESC` across all queries to centralize sorting logic and eliminate ambiguous sorting issues that previously required manual double-sort handling in infinite scroll use cases.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this refactor improves code maintainability and prepares the codebase for async paradigm adoption while reducing the need for manual sorting workarounds.
author: peter496

title: UP-1592: Expand dataset locator concept
summary: Expanded the dataset locator concept to include a structured schema definition that enumerates required fields for datasets based on their connector type. Moved the `data_file_type` field into the dataset locator structure so it's only present when needed by the connector. This change includes a database migration, updated validation logic, and comprehensive test coverage for the new locator validation system.
userImpact: Frontend contract is broken; datasets now require a properly structured locator with connector-specific required fields defined in the connector's dataset_locator_schema, enabling more flexible and connector-aware dataset configuration.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1590 - Break up job_executor.py to smaller pieces
summary: Refactored monolithic job_executor.py into smaller, focused modules (alert_check_executor, connector_test_executor, fetch_data_executor, list_datasets_executor, metrics_calculation_executor, schedule_jobs_executor, and schema_inference_executor). This restructuring improves code maintainability and testability by separating concerns into their own files with no functional changes to behavior.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: peter496

title: UP-1597 webhook alert routing
summary: Implemented webhook notification routing so that alerts are now delivered only to the webhooks linked to their corresponding alert rule. Also fixed a validation bug where alerts for a model could reference any alert rule ID in the system without verifying it belonged to that specific model.
userImpact: Users can now set up targeted alert routing by linking specific webhooks to individual alert rules, and alerts are correctly routed only to the intended webhook destinations.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1597 alert rule webhook routing
summary: Added API endpoints and state management to link alert rules to webhooks for notification routing. This is part 1 of 2, establishing the data model and API layer; the next MR will update alert notification routing to use these links. Changes include a new database migration, updated alert rule API models and validators, and comprehensive test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now associate webhooks with alert rules through the API to control where alert notifications are sent.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1041 - Add parallelism to dataplane execution
summary: Adds parallel execution support to the dataplane job agent, allowing multiple jobs to run concurrently in separate threads or processes. Each job declares its memory requirements, and the agent allocates resources if available, tracks running jobs on each loop iteration, and reclaims memory when jobs complete. Also makes MemorySecretsClient thread-safe to support parallel integration testing.
userImpact: Dataplane jobs can now execute in parallel, improving throughput and resource utilization when multiple jobs are queued, while latency-sensitive jobs can opt for threaded execution.
author: peter496

title: chore: upsolve update to 0.1.2
summary: Updated upsolve base image to version 0.1.2 and refreshed the associated patch file. This maintenance update ensures the upsolve component is running the latest available version.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1435 job queue management design tweaks
summary: Updated job queue management design documentation to include a self-loop transition for the "move to front of queue" action and corrected a missing state in the acceptance criteria. These changes clarify the complete state machine behavior for job queue operations.
userImpact: Job queue management state transitions are now fully documented, ensuring accurate understanding of queue operation capabilities including the ability to move jobs to the front of the queue.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Use new file for tracking current in memory mock Keycloak client
summary: Fixed race condition in local development where multiple processes competed for the same JSON file tracking the in-memory mock Keycloak client. Changed implementation to use a separate temporary file for each process, eliminating unnecessary file sharing and resolving the contention issue.
userImpact: Developers working in local-dev environments will no longer experience race condition errors when running multiple processes that interact with the mock Keycloak client.
author: peter496

title: Add unify-app hosted in vercel origin to CORS
summary: Added https://unify-frontend-app.vercel.app to the CORS allowed origins list to support Vercel deployments of the frontend application. This enables the Vercel-hosted frontend to communicate with the backend API without CORS restrictions.
userImpact: Users can now access the application via the Vercel-hosted frontend at unify-frontend-app.vercel.app without encountering CORS errors.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-1041 - Initial changes to set up multithreaded job runs
summary: Added job priority and memory requirement fields to the jobs database schema and data models to support multithreaded job execution. Updated the job dequeuing logic to filter jobs based on agent memory capacity constraints, and refactored JobsRepository.submit_job to use a struct-based argument pattern with sensible defaults for internal fields like retry count and timestamps.
userImpact: Jobs can now be dequeued with consideration for agent memory limits, and job submission uses a cleaner API that prevents callers from accidentally overriding internal state fields.
author: peter496

title: UP-1435 job queue management tech design
summary: This merge request adds a technical design document for job queue management, establishing how the system should support job cancellation, retries, and state transitions. The document aligns the team on queue priority levels, job cloning detection, and operational patterns for managing job lifecycles through a Kubernetes-inspired paradigm with priority tiers (USER_FACING, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW).
userImpact: Users can now reference a clear technical specification for how job queue management will work, enabling informed decisions about job prioritization and queue operations like moving jobs to the front of the queue or cancelling running jobs.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1582 | Remove task_name from allowed shield filters
summary: Removed the `task_name` filter from allowed filtering fields in the shield connector to prevent potential data access issues. The Shield API applies AND logic to combined filters, so exposing task_name alongside task_id could allow unintended cross-task data access.
userImpact: Users can no longer filter shield connector data by task_name, preventing potential unauthorized access to data from other tasks.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1493: Scripts to generate expel sample tabular dataset in s3
summary: Added scripts and utilities to generate synthetic tabular datasets for the Expel use case and upload them to S3. The generator creates configurable sample data with time-based predictions and can output to either local files or S3, supporting both testing and dashboard population workflows.
