title: Fix alarms
summary: Fixed alarm creation by correctly resolving and passing target group and load balancer full names to the CloudFormation alarm configuration. The alarms were not triggering properly due to missing or incorrect resource name lookups, which are now resolved through updated metric search logic in the deployment script.
userImpact: Application plane 5xx error alarms now correctly trigger when error thresholds are exceeded, restoring visibility into application health issues.
author: jdb-arthur

title: add aggregation timeout config to scope
summary: Adds configurable timeout support for custom and default aggregations in arthur-scope, aligning with the ML engine implementation. The timeout parameter is now exposed as an environment variable that can be configured across deployment environments via CloudFormation and configuration files.
userImpact: Users can now configure aggregation timeout values for both custom and default aggregations in arthur-scope deployments.
author: talerez

title: UP-815 idp group management implementation
summary: Implements IDP (Identity Provider) group management, allowing group membership to be managed through user claims in authentication tokens instead of directly in Arthur. When a group has `idp_group_mapping_name` set, membership is now managed via SpiceDB using claims from the token. The implementation includes SpiceDB schema updates to use explicit group relations, enhanced token parsing to extract IDP group claims, and Keycloak bootstrap configuration for new environments.
userImpact: Users can now configure groups to be managed by their identity provider, with group membership automatically determined by claims in their authentication tokens rather than requiring manual management in Arthur.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Always use Postgres SSL connection for prod grade deployments
summary: PostgreSQL connections for production-grade deployments now enforce SSL/TLS encryption. This change updates both the CloudFormation stack for ECS-based authentication and the Keycloak Helm deployment template to require secure database connections in production environments.
userImpact: Production deployments now automatically enforce encrypted PostgreSQL connections, improving security posture and preventing unencrypted database traffic in production-grade environments.
author: nori2

title: Postgres ssl cert folder permission fix
summary: Fixed PostgreSQL SSL certificate folder permission issues in the Docker configurations for both spicedb and upsolve services. The fix ensures proper access to SSL certificates during Postgres connections by updating folder permissions in the Dockerfiles and bumping the arthur-auth dependency.
userImpact: PostgreSQL SSL connections now work correctly without permission-related failures.
author: nori2

title: UP-815 tech design for IDP managed authz
summary: Added comprehensive technical design documentation for implementing IDP-managed authorization, including detailed instructions for configuring OIDC group mapping with customer IDPs like Keycloak. The design outlines how group membership can be managed at the IDP level while permissions are controlled within Arthur, establishing a clear path for future implementation while supporting manual strategies initially.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is design documentation establishing the technical foundation for future IDP-managed group authorization features.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-3022: Update ML engine volumes for non-root user
summary: Updated ML engine volume mounts in docker-compose.yaml to be compatible with non-root user execution. This aligns with changes to expected source code paths in the arthur-engine service.
userImpact: ML engine can now run as a non-root user with correctly configured volume mounts.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project including boto3, mypy, numpy, opentelemetry, pydantic, authzed, and arthur-common to their latest patch and minor versions. This keeps the codebase current with upstream improvements and security patches while maintaining API compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from upstream bug fixes, performance improvements, and security updates in key dependencies like boto3, numpy, and opentelemetry without breaking changes.

title: Add the missing curl install in the final image
summary: The final Docker image was missing the curl utility, which is required for various health checks and operations. This change adds the curl installation to the Dockerfile to ensure the dependency is present in production builds.
userImpact: Users can now use curl commands within the Docker container without encountering "command not found" errors.
author: nori2

title: add unit tests for validate_aggregation_specs
summary: Added unit tests for validate_aggregation_specs to verify that the function correctly handles validation of custom aggregations, including soft-deleted ones. The investigation confirmed that check_if_custom_aggregation_exists_in_workspace already calls _get_custom_aggregations_with_count with include_deleted_aggs=False by default, eliminating the need for additional deletion checks.
userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Enable SSL connection for Postgres on the app plane
summary: PostgreSQL connections on the app plane are now configured to use SSL encryption. This change updates the CloudFormation stack templates for database and app plane configuration to enable secure communication with the database, protecting credentials and data in transit.
userImpact: PostgreSQL connections from the app plane to the database are now encrypted using SSL, improving security for database communications.
author: nori2

title: RDS cert bundle file on Scope container image
summary: The RDS certificate bundle is now baked into the Scope container image, enabling secure SSL connections to AWS RDS databases. A new entrypoint script ensures the certificate is properly configured at container startup, and the Docker environment has been updated to support this change.
userImpact: Users can now establish SSL-encrypted connections to AWS RDS Postgres databases without manual certificate configuration.
author: nori2

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.40.24→1.40.26), moto (5.1.11→5.1.12), psycopg (3.2.9→3.2.10), and arthur-common (2.1.60→2.1.61). These patch and minor version updates include API improvements and bug fixes from upstream projects.

title: UP-3006: Docs update with data-plane deprecation and common dev workflows

This enhancement coordinates with ml-engine changes to properly handle stale aggregation references and prevent silent failures in metric calculations.

title: chore: switch all environments to ml-engine with a fixed version

title: Add Snowflake connector schema support
summary: This MR introduces a dedicated Snowflake connector type with its own schema definition, creating clean separation from the generic ODBC connector. A new `ConnectorType.SNOWFLAKE` enum value was added along with dedicated schema files (`snowflake_connector.py` and `snowflake_connector_spec.py`) containing Snowflake-specific fields like warehouse, role, authenticator, and key-based authentication support. The bootstrap system was updated to register and load the Snowflake connector schema properly.
userImpact: Users can now configure Snowflake connectors with a dedicated, Snowflake-specific interface that includes native support for warehouse selection, roles, and key-based authentication without mixing concerns with generic ODBC connectors.

title: Use ml engine
summary: This MR switches the AWS Dev Platform from data-plane to ml-engine for deployment. The change includes updates to the AWS DEV configuration, the Cloudformation deployment script to support ml-engine with a fixed version via docker.io registry, and the release pipeline to capture the ml-engine version for future production release locking.
userImpact: The AWS Dev Platform now uses ml-engine instead of data-plane, enabling testing and validation of ml-engine functionality in the development environment.

title: Custom Aggregations refactor
summary: Refactored the custom aggregations data model and database schema to improve structure and maintainability. The changes include updates to the internal model, API models, repository layer, and database schema migration, with comprehensive test coverage to ensure backwards compatibility with existing custom aggregation data.
userImpact: Users can continue using custom aggregations as before; this refactor improves the internal data model structure without changing user-facing functionality.

title: UP-2840: Integration test for custom aggregation execution
summary: Added integration test for custom aggregation execution to verify the functionality works end-to-end with BigQuery. The test suite includes updates to test fixtures and new test cases to ensure custom aggregations execute correctly.

title: [NO-REF] Fix Flaky Integration Test
summary: Fixed a race condition in the multi_dataset_model integration test that caused occasional failures. The alert check job timing could miss detecting the previous metrics calculation job failure it was meant to validate, leading to flaky test behavior. The fix ensures proper time range coverage between these sequential test operations.
userImpact: Integration tests now run reliably without intermittent failures due to timing issues.

title: Add segmentation overview to product docs
summary: Added segmentation overview to product documentation to help users understand how to configure and use segmentation for metrics. This includes guidance on setting up model versioning support, which allows users to segment metrics by model version to track and compare performance across different model iterations.
userImpact: Users can now reference clear documentation on how to segment metrics by custom dimensions like model version, enabling better tracking and comparison of model performance over time.

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: Match aggregations with no column parameters
summary: Fixed the aggregation matcher to correctly handle aggregations with no column parameters. The matcher now properly identifies and processes these edge cases, improving the robustness of aggregation matching logic across the system.

title: Update OpenAPI Spec to 2.1.59

title: UP-2839: Implement PUT method for custom aggregations

title: UP-2844: Validate if model and custom aggregation are in the same workspace
summary: Added validation to ensure that custom aggregations used as metrics in models must reside in the same workspace as the model. This prevents cross-workspace metric configuration errors and ensures proper data isolation. The validation is applied when creating or updating models with custom aggregation metrics.
userImpact: Users can now safely add custom aggregations as metrics to models with automatic validation that prevents misconfigurations from mixing aggregations across different workspaces.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: [NO-REF]: Bump arthur-common to 2.1.55

summary: Increased the maximum data retrieval size limit in Scope from its previous value to 25MB and made it configurable via environment variable. The max retrieval size is now defined in config and defaults to 25MB when not explicitly set via env var.

title: UP-2912: Grant custom_aggregation_read to engines
summary: Engines are now granted the `custom_aggregation_read` permission to enable them to access custom aggregation data. This change includes updates to authorization roles, SpiceDB schema, and a data migration to grant the permission to existing engines in both fresh and existing environments.

The shield connector now retrieves traces from the SpansApi, and metrics calculation is performed on agentic models before data aggregation, with trace metrics added directly to tasks rather than through rules.

title: UP-2829: Improve handling of scheduled job race condition
summary: Improved handling of scheduled job race conditions by adding validation to prevent submission of jobs with existing nonce values and stopping job retries. Previously, duplicate job submissions with the same nonce would result in 500 errors; now they are elegantly rejected at submission time, preventing server errors and unnecessary retries.
userImpact: Scheduled jobs no longer fail with 500 errors when a race condition causes duplicate submissions with the same nonce value.
author: alex380125

title: Add SSL connection support for Postgres for Upsolve
summary: Added SSL connection support for Postgres in Upsolve, enabling encrypted database connections in production environments. The implementation includes configuration updates to the CloudFormation stack, Docker setup, and connection entrypoint scripts to establish secure SSL/TLS connections to the Postgres database.
userImpact: Upsolve can now establish encrypted SSL/TLS connections to Postgres databases, improving security for data in transit.
author: nori2

title: Dockerfile fix - use Alpine as the build image for SpiceDB
summary: Updated the SpiceDB Dockerfile to use Alpine as the build image instead of a heavier base image. This reduces the final image size and build time while maintaining functionality. The Dockerfile was also corrected for proper CI/CD integration.
userImpact: Users can now pull smaller, faster-building SpiceDB container images, reducing deployment time and resource consumption.
author: nori2

title: fix: copy script into/from installer stage
summary: Fixed the startup script handling in the SpiceDB Dockerfile by ensuring the script is properly copied into the installer stage and then propagated to the final stage. This addresses a missing script issue that prevented proper initialization in production builds.
userImpact: The SpiceDB container now correctly includes and executes the startup script during initialization.

title: fix: use timescaledb for the gcp artifact registry
summary: Fixed GCP artifact registry configuration to correctly use timescaledb repository. The change updates the GitLab CI pipeline to reference the proper GCP artifact registry for timescaledb dependencies, ensuring correct artifact retrieval during builds.
userImpact: GCP artifact registry now correctly pulls timescaledb artifacts, preventing build failures due to misconfigured repository references.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: make spicedb image version dynamic for dev
summary: The SpiceDB image version in dev deployments is now dynamically configured instead of being hardcoded. This ensures the dev environment can automatically pick up SpiceDB version updates without manual config changes.
userImpact: Dev deployments will now use the latest SpiceDB image version without requiring manual updates to configuration files.

title: Spicedb ssl
summary: SpiceDB now uses SSL to connect to Postgres, improving security for database communications. Support for Postgres SSL certificates has been added to the SpiceDB configuration, with enforcement of SSL connections in the CloudFormation stack.

summary: Adds SSL connection support for SpiceDB Postgres backend through a custom Docker container that imports SSL certificates. title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: This change enforces lowercase normalization for all email addresses across the system through database constraints and API validation. A database migration adds check constraints to ensure emails are stored in lowercase, while API models and repositories are updated to normalize emails on input. Comprehensive test coverage validates the normalization behavior across user registration and lifecycle operations.
userImpact: Users can now only register and manage accounts with lowercase email addresses; any uppercase characters in emails are automatically normalized to lowercase for consistency and to prevent duplicate accounts.
author: alex380125

title: Adding dev-sts env and a new convenience deploy script for running the deploy outside the CICD

userImpact: Developers can now deploy to the dev-sts environment manually using the provided deploy script without relying on CI/CD automation.

title: Revert "UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails"
summary: This MR reverts the lowercase email enforcement changes (UP-2756) due to failures in production. The migration file is retained but emptied to allow safe deployment in all environments (development with prior migration runs, and production/expel/axios where the migration never ran).

author: alex380125

title: UP-2756: Enforce lowercase emails
summary: Fixed a bug where pending invitations were not being granted to users with uppercase letters in their email addresses. The issue occurred because Keycloak normalizes all emails to lowercase, but the pending invitation system did not enforce this same constraint, causing email equality checks to fail. The fix enforces lowercase email normalization across all user objects to match Keycloak's behavior and ensure consistency in the platform's storage layer.
userImpact: Users with uppercase letters in their email addresses can now successfully accept pending invitations and have their roles properly granted.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2752: Aggregation time segmentation performance protections
summary: Aggregations now validate segmentation arguments at metric calculation time to prevent performance issues. Arguments tagged with `possible_segmentation` are checked to ensure they have ≤3 columns, appropriate data types, and don't exceed a configurable limit of unique values. This validation occurs during aggregation argument processing rather than in aggregations themselves, ensuring automatic protection for future custom metrics with segmentation support.
userImpact: Aggregation jobs will now fail early with clear validation errors if segmentation arguments don't meet performance requirements (column count, data type, or unique value limits), preventing expensive runtime failures during metric calculation.
author: alex380125

title: chore: bump arthur-common to 2.1.49

The change includes updates to environment variables, configuration files, and references across the app_plane package, along with removal of legacy Python client setup files that are no longer needed.
userImpact: Users and developers will see consistent "Arthur Client" naming across documentation, configuration, and code, reducing confusion around internal component naming.

title: feat: Add ODBC connector support
summary: Comprehensive ODBC connector support has been added to Arthur Scope, enabling connectivity to any ODBC-compatible database (SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.) through a unified interface. The implementation includes API model updates, connector schema definitions, complete data plane connector logic with pagination and filtering, integration with the connector factory, and integration test coverage with PostgreSQL.
userImpact: Users can now connect to any ODBC-compatible database through a single connector interface, with support for time-based filtering, pagination, dataset listing, and schema inspection.

title: UP-2733: ignore optional params in aggregation matcher
summary: The aggregation matcher now ignores optional parameters when validating dataset configurations, enabling support for optional metric parameters like segmentation. Previously, the matcher required datasets to have columns matching all configured parameters, even optional ones. This change allows metrics to be configured with optional parameters without requiring those columns to exist in every dataset.
userImpact: Users can now configure metrics with optional segmentation parameters without needing those columns present in every dataset that uses the metric.
author: alex380125

title: unit test fix: don't use external http server for webhooks
summary: Replaced unreliable external httpstat.us server with a local HTTP server for webhook unit tests, eliminating test flakiness. Also added arthur-common to test requirements to fix failing integration tests on main.
userImpact: Unit tests are now more reliable and no longer fail due to external service unavailability.

title: UP-2598: Add Slack notification for user and model register
summary: Added Slack webhook integration to send notifications when users register and models are onboarded. The feature includes a new Slack messaging module, integration with user registration and model onboarding endpoints, and environment configuration for Slack webhook and channel settings across all deployment environments.
userImpact: User and model registration events now trigger Slack notifications to configured channels, enabling better visibility and monitoring of onboarding activities across the platform.

title: UP-2273: Add validation for invalid file suffix regexes
summary: Added validation for invalid file suffix regexes in the file locator schema, preventing malformed regex patterns from being accepted when configuring connectors. This prevents confusing error messages where schema inspection failures were actually caused by invalid regex patterns in the dataset posting phase, improving the user experience by catching errors earlier in the workflow.
userImpact: Users will now receive immediate, clear validation errors when attempting to use invalid regex patterns for file suffix configuration, rather than encountering cryptic failures during schema inspection.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2526 resolve internet dependency with Upsolve
summary: Resolved an internet connectivity dependency issue with Upsolve when running on OpenShift. The fix removes unnecessary internet connectivity requirements from the Upsolve deployment configuration, streamlining the Helm deployment template.

title: fix: adjust dlm time format
summary: Fixed DLM time format configuration in CloudFormation template and deployment script. The fix ensures proper parameter handling during AWS CLI CloudFormation stack deployments by hardcoding default values that are consistent across environments.
userImpact: Database backup stack deployments now execute successfully with correct time format configuration.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2583: Add pin in deep dive tag
summary: Added support for a "pin" option in the deep dive tag within the schema definitions model. This enhancement enables users to pin important items in deep dive analysis, improving visibility and prioritization of key findings. The change is coordinated with corresponding updates in arthur-engine and the frontend application.
userImpact: Users can now pin items within deep dive tags to highlight and prioritize critical analysis results.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2203 data plane version safety
summary: Added exception handling in the data plane's job agent to gracefully handle job specs with unknown types that are not recognized by the Scope Client. When an unrecognized job spec is encountered, the job is marked as failed with a user-friendly log message instructing them to upgrade the data plane to support the feature.
userImpact: Users will now receive clear guidance when attempting to use features that require a newer data plane version, rather than encountering undefined behavior.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: pass default values to backup stacks and timescale
summary: Fixed database deployment script to properly pass default values to backup stacks and timescale configurations. This ensures backup and timescale components are correctly initialized with expected default parameters during stack deployment.
userImpact: Database backup stacks and timescale configurations now initialize correctly with proper default values during deployment.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2406 alert rule query validation api
summary: Adds a new API endpoint for validating alert rule SQL queries before creation, leveraging existing backend SQL parser and validation logic. The endpoint uses the same permissions as the create alert rule endpoint and returns validation results including checks for required columns (metric_timestamp, metric_value) and template variables.
userImpact: Frontend can now validate alert rule queries in real-time, providing immediate feedback to users about query correctness before attempting to create the alert rule.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: do not fail if deploy tag already exists
summary: The Create-Deployment-Tag job no longer fails when a deploy tag already exists. This condition occurs when there are no new commits or releases to deploy and is not an error condition, improving pipeline reliability.
userImpact: Deployment pipelines will no longer fail due to pre-existing deploy tags, reducing false failures in CI/CD workflows.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2553 add tags and backups support
summary: Added backup capability for TimescaleDB with CloudFormation support for automated snapshots and retention policies. The implementation introduces a new backup stack configuration and deployment scripts that enable database backups with configurable tags, snapshot timing, and retention counts.
userImpact: Users can now configure automated backups for their TimescaleDB instances with customizable backup tags, snapshot schedules, and retention policies.

title: UP-2402 Alert rule improvements feature branch
summary: Added "interval" configuration to alert rules to prevent false positives by enabling time-aware alert checking. The feature includes database schema updates, refactored alert checking logic, and improvements to alert-to-job linkage to ensure alerts are properly tracked and validated at the configured intervals.
userImpact: Users can now configure an interval on alert rules to control the frequency of alert checks and reduce spurious alerts caused by temporary metric fluctuations.

title: UP-2402 alert rule refactor pt. 1
summary: This PR introduces an "interval" field to alert rules and alerts, with new database models and API schemas to track the time interval used during alert evaluation. The query modifier logic now enforces required template variables (`{{interval}}`, `{{dateStart}}`, `{{dateEnd}}`) and supports placing these templates anywhere in queries, enabling advanced time-bucketing functions like `time_bucket_gapfill()` in SELECT statements. A database migration populates the new interval field for existing alert rules and alerts.
userImpact: Users can now define custom time intervals for alert rule evaluations and queries can use time-bucketing functions with gapfill to handle sparse time-series data more effectively.

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.38.13 to 1.38.14 and google-cloud-bigquery from 3.31.0 to 3.32.0. These updates include new AWS service API capabilities for Deadline Cloud, EC2, IAM, MediaLive, and other services, plus new BigQuery features for dataset access policy versioning and incremental results support.
userImpact: Users gain access to new AWS API capabilities and BigQuery features including dataset access policy version attributes and preview support for incremental results.

title: UP-2345: alert check job fault tolerance
summary: Improved fault tolerance for the alert check job executor by adding error handling and recovery logic. The executor now gracefully handles failures during alert checks and continues processing, with corresponding unit tests added to validate the new behavior.
userImpact: Alert check jobs are now more resilient and will continue operating even when individual alert checks encounter errors.
author: alex380125

title: [NO-REF] Default alert rule bugfix
summary: Fixed an issue where default alert rules created via alembic migrations were missing SpiceDB authorization relationships, causing 404 errors when attempting to fetch them. This migration manually reconstructs the missing relationships for the default alert rule so it can be properly accessed.
userImpact: Users can now successfully retrieve and interact with default alert rules that were previously inaccessible due to missing authorization relationships.

title: UP-2446: Create alert check job on put state endpoint
summary: Implemented automatic alert check job creation when job state transitions to a final failed state via the PUT state endpoint. The system now logs job failures and triggers alert checks only when jobs are permanently failed (after all retries are exhausted), preventing noise from transient failures that self-resolve on retry.
userImpact: Users now receive alerts only for job failures that require action, eliminating false alarms from jobs that succeed after retries.
author: alex380125

author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2456 add link task job and API
summary: Added a new API endpoint for creating a scope GenAI model from an existing task in the GenAI engine. The implementation includes database migrations, job execution logic, authorization rules, and API models to support linking tasks to the task management system.
userImpact: Users can now create a GenAI model scope from an existing task via a new API endpoint.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 to 1.38.11 and cfn-lint to 1.35.0. boto3 adds API support for Path Component Exclusion in EC2 Reachability Analyzer, updates for ImageBuilder, MediaLive, SageMaker, and Synthetics. cfn-lint receives a minor version bump with general improvements.
userImpact: Users gain access to the latest AWS SDK features and CloudFormation linting improvements through dependency updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2452 fix duplicate user creation API response
summary: Fixed a TypeError in duplicate user email error handling that occurred in AWS with real Keycloak instances. The error handling code was comparing a bytes object to a string, causing the duplicate user detection to fail. Error message comparison now correctly handles the bytes-like error response from Keycloak.
userImpact: Users attempting to create duplicate accounts now receive the proper error response instead of a TypeError when using AWS with real Keycloak authentication.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2447: Handle non-iterable in filters gracefully
summary: Fixed a crash in the connector filter logic when encountering non-iterable values during filter processing. The connector_read_filters.py module now gracefully handles these edge cases instead of raising an exception, improving robustness of data plane operations.
userImpact: Users can now read data from connectors without encountering errors when filter values are non-iterable.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2445 create raw data reader role
summary: A new "Raw Data Reader" role has been created to allow Workspace Read All users to also view raw data independently. This is a new role in the authorization system that can be bound to multiple resource types, with corresponding updates to the SpiceDB schema and comprehensive unit tests added to ensure the multi-resource binding works correctly.
userImpact: Admins can now grant users a Raw Data Reader role to independently grant raw data access alongside existing workspace or project-level permissions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2436 fix redis connections in AWS
summary: Redis Python client v6 introduced a breaking change setting `ssl_check_hostname` to `True` by default, which caused Redis connections in AWS to fail with a certificate verification error. This fix disables `ssl_check_hostname` to restore compatibility with AWS deployments.
userImpact: Redis connections in AWS environments now work correctly after the Redis Python v6 upgrade.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency boto3 to v1.38.10
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.38.9 to v1.38.10. This patch release includes improvements to EC2 volume initialization, ServiceCatalog API validation, and Timestream endpoint support.
userImpact: Users gain access to improved EBS Provisioned Rate for Volume Initialization, stricter ServiceCatalog idempotency token validation, and enhanced Timestream dualstack endpoint support.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2383 fix ownership of upsolve execute patches directory in ECS
summary: Fixed ownership of the upsolve execute patches directory in ECS environments. The `/db-patches` directory was created with root permissions instead of being owned by the upsolve user, causing permission denied errors when the application tried to read and write executed patch tracking files.
userImpact: Users running upsolve in ECS will no longer encounter permission errors when processing database patches.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2383 upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded upsolve dependency to include backend and theme fixes, plus read-only support. The upgrade addresses BE stability issues and UI improvements without requiring data re-export due to API-only changes in this release.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved backend reliability, theme rendering fixes, and the ability to use read-only mode with upsolve.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2423: Handle failure to load datasets in metrics calculation jobs
summary: Added graceful handling for dataset load failures in metrics calculation jobs. Previously, when a dataset failed to load, metrics configured to run on that dataset would fail with a generic KeyError, obscuring the root cause. Now the system tracks which datasets failed to load and skips metrics calculation for those datasets, while providing clear error messages to users about why metrics weren't calculated.
userImpact: Users now see clear, actionable error messages when metrics fail due to dataset load failures, instead of generic KeyError exceptions that mask the underlying problem.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2425: Add datetime information to task connection info
summary: Added datetime information to task connection info, including creation and modification timestamps. This change includes a database migration to add time-related columns to the connection info schema and updates to the task API models and repositories to track and manage this temporal data.
userImpact: Users can now view when task connections were created and last modified, enabling better audit trails and troubleshooting of task connection changes.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2427 refresh keycloak token during user invite call
summary: Fixed an issue where the user invite API call would fail when the Keycloak token was expired or not yet initialized, which could occur on server startup. The Keycloak client now refreshes the token before making the user invite call to ensure a valid token is always available.
userImpact: User invite calls will no longer fail due to expired or missing Keycloak tokens on server startup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2426 dashboard edit rbac
summary: Added a new `model_edit_dashboard` permission to enable role-based access control (RBAC) for dashboard editing. This permission is integrated into the authorization schema and managed roles, with comprehensive test coverage to ensure the permission is correctly enforced.
userImpact: Administrators can now control which roles have access to edit dashboards through RBAC policies, providing more granular permission management.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2424: Fix regeneration failures
summary: Fixed a regression in connection secret regeneration that was introduced when adding support for deleting connection info in the control plane. The issue was caused by AWS Secrets Manager's scheduled deletion behavior interfering with secret regeneration logic, even when using ForceDeleteWithoutRecovery.
userImpact: Connection secret regeneration now completes successfully without failures.
author: alex380125

title: Update dependency redis to v6
summary: Updated redis-py from v5 to v6, introducing new hash commands with expiration support (HGETDEL, HGETEX, HSETEX), improved async Sentinel client features, and experimental VectorSet command support. This major version includes several breaking changes: ssl_check_hostname now defaults to True for security, retry configuration changes for cluster clients, and removal of deprecated charset/errors arguments and RedisGears/RedisGraph module support.
userImpact: Users gain access to new hash expiration commands and improved security defaults, but must review breaking changes including mandatory retry configuration for cluster clients and removal of deprecated parameters.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated multiple non-major dependencies including authzed/spicedb (v1.42.1 → v1.43.0), boto3 (v1.38.5 → v1.38.9), psycopg (v3.2.6 → v3.2.7), and redis (v5.2.1 → v5.3.0). These updates bring in bug fixes, API enhancements, and compatibility improvements across AWS SDK, database drivers, and caching libraries.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved AWS API compatibility, database driver stability, and Redis client enhancements without requiring breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2399: Tech design for failing metrics calculation jobs
summary: Added technical design documentation for handling failing metrics calculation jobs. The design outlines approach for detecting and alerting on metrics calculation job failures, including job type dimensions, alert rule configuration, and integration with the alert check job system.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: UP-2402 alert fixes
summary: Added technical design documentation for stabilizing false positive alerts in the alerting system. The document outlines the proposed refactor for the alert rule implementation, providing guidance for future development and reducing alert noise.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: boto3 from 1.38.3 to 1.38.5 and pydantic from 2.11.3 to 2.11.4. These patch updates include AWS service API enhancements and bug fixes but do not introduce breaking changes or user-facing features.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency updates maintain backward compatibility and are recommended for users to adopt latest service capabilities and stability improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2420: Delete validation key on task deletion
summary: When a task is deleted, the associated validation key is now properly removed from the control plane to maintain consistency with shield. This prevents orphaned validation keys and ensures the control plane and data plane states remain synchronized during task deletion operations.
userImpact: Validation keys are now correctly cleaned up when tasks are deleted, preventing orphaned keys from persisting in the system.
author: alex380125

title: NO-REF: Fix bug with data plane config for raw data retrieval
summary: Fixed a bug in the data plane configuration that was causing failures in raw data retrieval jobs. The issue was resolved by correcting the configuration handling in the data plane config module, with corresponding test updates to validate the fix.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval jobs now complete successfully without configuration-related errors.
author: alex380125

title: Up-2383 fix upsolve migration
summary: Version 0.2.40 of the upsolve migration contained a bug that broke when applied on the dev database. Version 0.2.41 fixes this issue by updating the Docker image version in the upsolve Dockerfile.
userImpact: Dev database migrations for upsolve now apply correctly without breaking.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency boto3 to v1.38.3
summary: Updated boto3 dependency from v1.38.2 to v1.38.3. This patch release includes API updates for bedrock-runtime (S3 image/document reference support in InvokeModel and Converse APIs), ECS documentation updates, and AWS Marketplace Deployment Service documentation fixes.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency maintenance update with underlying SDK improvements for AWS service integrations.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] rename engine internal env vars
summary: Renamed internal engine environment variables across the codebase for consistency and clarity. This change affects configuration files, connectors, documentation, and local development scripts.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This is an internal refactoring of environment variable names used by the Shield Control Plane.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2385: Validation key regeneration job
summary: This merge request implements a validation key regeneration job that allows users to rotate their API validation keys for GenAI models. The implementation adds a new job type with proper authorization, database storage, and API endpoints for initiating and managing validation key regeneration. Key management is handled securely with ordered operations to prevent orphaned keys in the system.
userImpact: Users can now regenerate validation keys for their models through a managed job, enabling secure key rotation and remediation of potentially compromised credentials.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2386: Upload validation key in task creation job
summary: Added automatic upload of validation keys to Shield during task creation. The task creation job now generates and uploads validation credentials in Shield, enabling secure task authentication without manual key management. This integrates validation key lifecycle management into the task provisioning workflow.
userImpact: Users can now create tasks with automatically provisioned validation keys in Shield, eliminating the need for manual key upload steps and improving task security configuration.
author: alex380125

title: Bump auth version to 1.0.47 in scope-version-manifest.json
summary: Updated the auth service version to 1.0.47 in the scope-version-manifest.json file. This version bump likely includes bug fixes, security updates, or minor improvements to the authentication service.
userImpact: Users will receive the latest auth service version (1.0.47) with any improvements or fixes included in that release.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [NO-REF]: Update invite user docs
summary: Updated API documentation for the invite user endpoint to include proper status code specification. This ensures the API docs accurately reflect the expected response status rather than defaulting to 200.
userImpact: No direct user impact—API documentation is now more accurate for developers integrating with the invite user endpoint.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] add expel scripts to add schema
summary: Added onboarding scripts for the Expel customer to facilitate schema modifications. These utilities enable adding and removing columns from their dataset during setup.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Statful services ansible playbook
summary: Added Ansible playbook to provision stateful services on on-premise RHEL VMs using Podman containers. The playbook automates deployment of PostgreSQL and TimescaleDB containers with proper volume mounting, permission handling, and multi-registry container image support.
userImpact: Operations teams can now automatically provision stateful database services on RHEL infrastructure using Ansible, reducing manual configuration and improving consistency across deployments.
author: kacperek0

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies including boto3 (1.38.0 → 1.38.2), cfn-lint (1.34.1 → 1.34.2), and h11 (0.14.0 → 0.16.0). These updates include patch fixes and minor version improvements to maintain compatibility with the latest AWS API changes and HTTP/1.1 protocol enhancements.
userImpact: No direct user impact—routine dependency maintenance to keep tools and libraries current.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2834: Add task connection APIs
summary: Adds new task connection APIs and database infrastructure to manage task-level connector validation keys. Introduces task_connection_info table schema, secrets management for task connections, permission models, and router endpoints to support task-specific connector configuration and validation workflows.
userImpact: Users can now create, retrieve, and manage task-level connector connections through new API endpoints, enabling per-task connector configuration and validation key management.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2398 add capabilities to engine resources
summary: Added a typed Capabilities object to data plane resources with initial support for `gen_ai_enabled` flag. Capabilities can now be set during data plane creation (POST) and updates (PATCH), and filtering by `gen_ai_enabled` is available across workspace and project data plane APIs.
userImpact: Users and the frontend can now set and filter data planes by GenAI capability status during installation flows, enabling better management of Shield-enabled resources.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: boto3 from 1.37.38 to 1.38.0 (minor version with new AWS API support) and greenlet from 3.2.0 to 3.2.1 (patch). These updates include new AWS Account Management, Cognito, EC2, and Redshift Serverless API features via botocore.
userImpact: No direct user impact; routine dependency maintenance to receive latest AWS API changes and bug fixes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [no-ref] fix shield e2e test
summary: Fixed Shield e2e test that was failing because the expected task was not visible on the first page of results. The demo instance contains ~40 tasks, requiring pagination logic to be updated in the test case.
userImpact: Shield e2e tests now run reliably without missing expected tasks due to pagination.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: allow timescale stack to create backup role
summary: Updated the Timescale database deployment script to grant the necessary permissions for creating a backup role. This allows the Timescale stack to properly initialize backup functionality during deployment.
userImpact: Database backups can now be properly configured and executed when deploying Timescale stacks.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2379 create internal connectors on data plane project association
summary: Automatically creates internal connectors when a data plane is associated with a project. Adds validation logic to prevent data plane removal if user-facing or dependent internal connectors exist, and updates deletion logic to automatically clean up internal connectors and datasets when safe (no dependent models).
userImpact: Projects now automatically have internal connectors provisioned when data planes are added, with safeguards preventing accidental removal of data planes that have active connector or model dependencies.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2219: Add engine name validation
summary: Added engine name validation to the data planes and users operators to enforce naming constraints at the API level. Validation logic was centralized in the validation tools module, with comprehensive test coverage added for both data plane lifecycle and user validation scenarios. This ensures that engine names meet required standards before being persisted.
userImpact: Users will now receive validation errors when attempting to create or update engines with invalid names, preventing invalid configurations from being stored.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2378 & UP-2380 hide engine internal connectors and datasets
summary: The connectors and datasets APIs now hide internal engine resources by default, preventing them from appearing in user-facing lists. This prevents users from seeing or managing engine-internal connectors and datasets that should be managed through dedicated GenAI model flows instead.
userImpact: Users will no longer see engine-internal connectors and datasets in the APIs, simplifying the interface and directing management of these resources through appropriate GenAI model workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix email settings passing to bootstrap CFT
summary: The bootstrap CloudFormation template was not receiving all email configuration values from environment files, causing authentication email reply-to settings to be missing. This fix ensures all email configuration parameters are properly passed through the deployment script to the CFT, resolving bootstrap job warnings.
userImpact: Email reply-to and other email settings are now correctly configured during bootstrap deployments, eliminating configuration warnings.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore: add timescale daily backups for ec2 images/amis
summary: Added automated daily backups for TimeScale by creating AMI snapshots of EC2 instances. This provides a short-to-medium term backup and restore solution for the postgres data on the root volume.
userImpact: Users can now restore TimeScale database instances from daily AMI backups if data corruption or loss occurs.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2377 internal connector
summary: Added a new `engine_internal` connector type that functions like the Shield connector but retrieves credentials from the data plane's environment variables instead of the platform. The connector is intentionally hidden from the API connector types list to prevent it from appearing in the frontend, as it is used internally for Shield API key management jobs and code template generation.
userImpact: Users can now use the `engine_internal` connector for internal Shield operations that authenticate using data plane environment credentials rather than platform-managed credentials.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2290: Add API to retrieve feature flags
summary: Added a new API endpoint that retrieves all feature flags as a single JSON object. The endpoint abstracts feature flag management to a dedicated operator and router, allowing the frontend to fetch all feature flags in one call without requiring backend changes when new flags are added.
userImpact: Frontend applications can now retrieve all environment feature flags via a single API call, enabling dynamic feature flag consumption without backend modifications.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2315: Update org bootstrap to use immutable org identifier
summary: Updated organization bootstrap logic to use an immutable organization identifier, preventing default organizations from being recreated during bootstrap re-runs. The fix tracks organizations via a stable identifier rather than allowing duplicate creation, with added safeguards and unit tests to validate the behavior.
userImpact: Bootstrap jobs no longer recreate default organizations when run multiple times, avoiding unintended duplication and configuration drift.
author: alex380125

title: XP-1556: Add variable to control fetching raw data
summary: Added an environment variable to control whether raw data fetching is enabled in the data plane. This allows operators to disable raw data access at the engine level by setting a configuration parameter, with support across Docker Compose, CloudFormation, and deployment environments.
userImpact: Users and administrators can now control raw data fetching access on a per-engine basis through environment configuration, enabling stricter access controls in different deployment environments.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: fix: timescale port log in deployment script
summary: Fixed an incorrect log statement in the deployment script that was logging the timescale hostname instead of the timescale port. This correction ensures deployment logs display the correct information during testing and diagnostics.
userImpact: Deployment logs now correctly display the timescale port value instead of the hostname.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2291: Create new user via email
summary: Adds functionality to create and invite new users via email without requiring a password setup flow. Users can be invited with organization, workspace, or project-level role bindings and assigned to multiple groups. The implementation includes database migration for group tracking in user invites, updated authorization schema, and enhanced user invitation operator logic.
userImpact: Users can now invite new team members via email and assign them roles and groups directly, without requiring them to set a password during the invitation process.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2383 update upsolve and new export
summary: Upgraded upsolve to the latest release with new dashboard and export capabilities. Added a new multiclass model dashboard and a new chart component for adding text descriptions to dashboards.
userImpact: Users can now view monitoring dashboards for multiclass models and add text descriptions to dashboard charts.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2066 tech design for task API key management
summary: This MR documents the technical design for managing Shield API connection information in the platform, including support for regenerating API keys for shield tasks. The design proposal outlines the backend, job, and key management architecture to ensure secure API key handling and rotation.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Temporarily support user claims with or without org ID included
summary: Updated the token validator to support user claims with or without organization ID included, enabling a smooth transition when org ID support is enabled in the organization client scope mapper. This change maintains backwards compatibility during a phased rollout where org IDs will be added to claims in subsequent steps.
userImpact: Users' authentication tokens continue to work correctly during the gradual migration to include organization IDs in user claims, with no disruption to token validation.
author: alex380125

title: Hotfix/byo timescale
summary: This MR adds support for bring-your-own (BYO) Timescale parameters across dependent infrastructure stacks (upsolve, bootstrap, and app plane). The changes eliminate cross-stack references to the Timescale stack and enable configuration of external Timescale instances via environment parameters. Deployment logic has been updated to determine and apply Timescale settings (host and instance ID) during deployment.
userImpact: Users can now deploy with external Timescale instances by configuring BYO_TIMESCALE_HOST and BYO_TIMESCALE_INSTANCE_ID parameters, eliminating the dependency on the internal Timescale stack.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-2242 Fix Query Slowness by removing old metric versions
summary: Implemented automatic deletion of old metric versions when new versions are uploaded, resolving a critical performance issue where hundreds of thousands of accumulated metric versions were causing dashboards to load in over a minute. This addresses a post-mortem from Axios Media where query slowness significantly impacted user experience.
userImpact: Dashboard queries now complete significantly faster by automatically removing obsolete metric versions, restoring the performance that users experienced before metric version accumulation became problematic.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2315: Add organization user-friendly display name
summary: Added support for organization user-friendly display names by introducing a display_name field to organizations and refactoring the authentication system to use organization IDs from Keycloak claims instead of mutable identifiers. This includes database migrations, updates to the Keycloak and authentication clients, and corresponding API model changes to expose display names to users and administrators.
userImpact: Users can now see user-friendly organization display names in email invites and throughout the system, while the system uses immutable organization IDs for secure internal lookups and access control.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] update docs
summary: Minor documentation updates across metrics queries and SDK authentication examples. Changes include formatting and code example improvements to aid clarity and consistency.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency types-croniter to v6
summary: Updated the types-croniter type stub package from v5 to v6 (v5.0.1.20250322 → v6.0.0.20250411) across both application and data plane requirements. This is a routine dependency update to keep type hints aligned with the latest croniter library stubs.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated 13 non-major dependencies across the codebase, including patches to boto3, numpy, uvicorn, and minor version bumps to authzed, greenlet, opentelemetry packages, and pydantic_core. All changes are routine dependency maintenance with passing e2e tests.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2328 add allowed functions for new PSI query
summary: Added support for new allowed functions in the PSI query modifier to fix Expel's alert rule continuous query execution. The query modifier now permits additional functions required for the PSI query to function correctly.
userImpact: Expel's alert rule continuous queries using PSI now execute successfully with the newly allowed functions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Automated dependency updates for boto3, cfn-lint, and pytest_httpserver to their latest patch versions. These updates include AWS SDK improvements, CloudFormation linting enhancements, and a critical bug fix for pytest-httpserver server shutdown issues.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2317 fix keycloak email from
summary: Added configuration for the Keycloak email "From Display Name" field to ensure proper email sender identification. This setting was previously only applied manually to dev and prod environments; now it is codified in configuration files for all environments.
userImpact: Keycloak-sent emails now display the correct From name consistently across all environments, and replies route correctly to support ticketing systems.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2183: Support email on password update
summary: Updated Keycloak authentication configuration to support email notifications on password updates. The change enables the JBossLoggingEventListenerProvider in the Keycloak configuration, which logs user events (sign-in, register, password changes) and error events as warnings in the arthur-auth container logs.
userImpact: Users will now receive email notifications when their password is updated, improving account security awareness.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] turn on sign up in prod
summary: The sign-up feature flag has been enabled in the production environment. This deployment makes the sign-up functionality available to all users in prod now that the feature is ready for general availability.
userImpact: Users can now sign up for accounts in the production environment.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix invalid escape sequence string
summary: Fixed invalid escape sequence warning in converters.py by correcting an improperly escaped string literal. The SyntaxWarning for '\.' has been resolved, improving code quality and eliminating runtime warnings during application startup.
userImpact: Application no longer emits SyntaxWarning about invalid escape sequences during startup.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2303 add multiclass use case for expel
summary: Added multiclass classification as a new model problem type with corresponding metrics including multiclass confusion matrix and inference count by class. This expands the platform's capabilities to support multiclass classification use cases alongside the existing binary classification support.
userImpact: Users can now define multiclass classification models and monitor their performance using confusion matrix and inference count by class metrics.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2304: Fix deletion of sub-resources
summary: Fixed incorrect deletion behavior for sub-resources (datasets, models, and connectors) by correcting the cascade logic in the base operator. The fix ensures that when deleting parent resources, dependent resources are properly handled according to the deletion parameters, and error messages now include resource names for better user feedback.
userImpact: Users can now correctly delete workspaces, projects, and data planes with proper handling of their dependent sub-resources, and deletion errors now clearly identify which resources are blocking the operation.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates all non-major dependencies to their latest patch and minor versions, including boto3, cfn-lint, duckdb, pydantic, python-keycloak, and timescaledb. These are routine dependency updates to ensure the project benefits from bug fixes, performance improvements, and new non-breaking features from upstream libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2233 remove model metrics configs for unlinked datasets
summary: When a dataset is unlinked from a model, any metrics configurations that reference that dataset are now automatically removed. Previously, these orphaned metric configs would persist even after the dataset was unlinked, causing inconsistent state.
userImpact: Users can now safely unlink datasets from models without leaving behind stale metric configurations that reference the removed dataset.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2238 add filter to get /project/id/datasets API to allow limiting results by data plane ID
summary: Added a filter parameter to the GET /project/{id}/datasets API endpoint to allow limiting results by data plane ID. This enables the frontend to retrieve only datasets associated with a specific data plane, improving filtering capabilities for multi-plane environments.
userImpact: Users can now filter dataset listings by data plane ID through the GET /project/{id}/datasets API, allowing for more precise dataset queries in environments with multiple data planes.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2237 prevent models and joined datasets from multiple engines
summary: Added validation to prevent models and joined datasets from being backed by different compute engines within a multi-engine workspace. Additionally, denormalized data_plane_id as a column on datasets, available_datasets, and models resources to enable frontend filtering and visibility of engine compatibility.
userImpact: Users can no longer accidentally create cross-engine model associations or joined datasets, preventing runtime failures and data inconsistencies in multi-engine environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3, cfn-lint, and moto to their latest patch versions. These are routine dependency updates that include bug fixes and improvements to AWS mocking and linting capabilities.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2223: Enforce 1:1 model to shield dataset
summary: Enforces a 1:1 relationship between models and shield datasets to ensure proper data isolation and security. This change validates that each model is paired with exactly one corresponding shield dataset, preventing misconfiguration and protecting dataset integrity.
userImpact: Users can now rely on a guaranteed 1:1 model-to-shield-dataset mapping that prevents configuration errors and ensures data protection integrity.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2220 final task apis
summary: Implements task state cache storage in the database as the final API needed for GenAI model management in the UI. Adds a new database migration for task storage caching, extends the model tasks router and repository to support retrieving cached task state, and includes comprehensive integration tests for the new task management functionality.
userImpact: The UI can now retrieve the cached task state from the backend for GenAI model management workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2282: Set up assigning permissions to first user
summary: Implemented the invitation acceptance flow that allows users invited via email to accept invitations and be automatically assigned permissions to their organization. When a user hits the `accept-invite` endpoint, they are now redirected to the create workspace flow, establishing the foundation for first-user permission assignment and organization access control.
userImpact: Users can now accept email invitations to join organizations and automatically receive the appropriate role bindings (e.g., organization super admin) to access their workspace.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated patch versions of boto3, pydantic, and typing_extensions dependencies. This is a routine dependency maintenance update containing only non-major version bumps with minor bug fixes and API changes in upstream libraries.

The updates include security fixes and bug fixes from upstream dependencies.
userImpact: No direct user impact. These are internal dependency updates to maintain compatibility and receive upstream bug fixes and security patches.

title: UP-2221 Part 2 implement task jobs
summary: Implemented task management jobs for shield-based task creation, fetching, and deletion. The implementation uses API models instead of generated client types to properly handle union rule config types, avoiding parsing ambiguities. End-to-end task creation with multiple rule types (KeywordRule, RegexRule, PIIDataRule) now works correctly.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage shield tasks through the task management job API, supporting multiple rule configuration types with proper validation and storage.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Dependencies updated to their latest non-major versions, including spicedb (minor), boto3 (patch), timescaledb (patch), and types-PyYAML (patch). These routine updates maintain compatibility with upstream projects and include incremental improvements to AWS service support and type stubs.

title: UP-2221 Part 1 Create separate job types for API-facing jobs vs internal jobs
summary: This MR introduces separate `PostJobKind` and `PostJobSpecType` types to distinguish between API-facing jobs and internal jobs that should only be submitted through specific service endpoints. Internal jobs like `fetch_data` are now isolated and removed from the public POST jobs API, laying the foundation for additional internal jobs to support task management features.
userImpact: The POST jobs API no longer exposes internal jobs like `fetch_data`, while the GET jobs API continues to show them; the API contract remains backward compatible for existing job submissions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2222 part 2 add task management to shield connector
summary: Adds task management functions to the shield connector, building on previous work to integrate with the Shield API. This includes create, read, update, and delete operations for tasks, with comprehensive unit tests and integration test fixes to support the new functionality.
userImpact: Users can now manage tasks directly through the shield connector using the new task management API methods.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Feat/ssm onprem replacement
summary: Replaced the in-memory secret store with a PostgreSQL-backed implementation and added AWS Secrets Manager support for on-premise deployments. The changes introduce a new database migration, refactor secret manager classes for clarity, and remove the deprecated memory-based secret storage approach in favor of persistent database storage.
userImpact: Users can now securely store secrets in a persistent PostgreSQL database, with optional AWS Secrets Manager integration for on-premise deployments, eliminating reliance on volatile in-memory storage.
author: kacperek0

title: Update all non-major dependencies

title: UP-2222 part 1, convert shield to use generated API client
summary: Converted the Shield connector to use a generated API client instead of manual HTTP implementation. The auto-generated client code (based on OpenAPI specs) is checked in and provides better maintainability. This is part 1 of the work; subsequent PRs will introduce new task-management functions using this client foundation.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved Shield connector stability and maintainability; existing functionality works the same while the underlying implementation is now backed by a generated, typed API client.

title: UP-2220 add onboarding_identifier to model resource and APIs
summary: Added `onboarding_identifier` field to the model resource and related POST/GET APIs. This enables the frontend to label models during the GenAI onboarding flow, making them easily discoverable after the model creation job completes.
userImpact: Users can now tag models with an onboarding identifier during model creation, allowing the GenAI onboarding flow to efficiently track and retrieve models after async job completion.

title: feat: push data plane images to dockerhub
summary: Added automation to push Data Plane images to Docker Hub during release deployments. The pipeline now tags and publishes Data Plane container images with both the release version and latest tags (on production releases only), while removing unnecessary Nexus login steps.
userImpact: Users can now pull Data Plane images directly from Docker Hub using release version tags and latest tags, eliminating the need to pull from internal registries.

title: UP-2220 initial task management API facade
summary: Introduces the initial task management API facade for Shield control plane, enabling task creation, retrieval, and lifecycle management through REST endpoints. The API provides hard-coded responses that unblock frontend development, with full backend integration to follow in subsequent work.
userImpact: Frontend developers can now begin integrating task management APIs using typed clients generated from the openapi.json specification, with support for creating and retrieving tasks within projects.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2258 make metric names more user friendly
summary: Metric names in the UI now display using explicit `display_name` attributes set by metric developers, replacing the previous unfriendly class-based names. This improves readability and makes it easier to search for and discover metrics when adding them to models. No migration required as metrics are loaded from aggregation definitions at runtime using stable IDs.
userImpact: Users now see clear, human-readable metric names in the UI and can more easily search for and discover available metrics when configuring models.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated boto3 from 1.37.20 to 1.37.21 with AWS service API improvements including new features for ARC zonal shifts, Direct Connect tagging, MediaConvert quality settings, and WAF v2 Amplify integration. Updated pydantic-core from 2.32.0 to 2.33.0 with support for extra keys validation in model fields and a revert of the `by_alias` default value change.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest AWS SDK improvements and pydantic validation enhancements without breaking changes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2245 ship data plane latest tag
summary: Updated CI/CD pipeline to automatically tag the latest data plane image deployed to production SaaS as "latest" for easier identification and deployment. This improves the deployment workflow by tracking the most recent production data plane version without requiring manual tagging at release time.
userImpact: Users can now reference and deploy the most recent production data plane using the "latest" tag instead of tracking specific version numbers.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2196: Make schema inspection work with no recent data
summary: Schema inference now works reliably when recent data is sparse or unavailable. The system progressively searches backward through time to find data for schema inspection, starting with the most recent interval and expanding further back if needed. This ensures schema inference can complete even when data is unevenly distributed across time.
userImpact: Users can now successfully run schema inspection jobs on datasets with no recent data; the system will automatically search through historical data to infer the current schema.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2249: Fix org name validation
summary: Fixed organization name validation to properly align with Keycloak's URL-friendly requirements. The validation regex was updated from a restrictive alphanumeric-only pattern to match Keycloak's standard, which prohibits only reserved characters (`:/?#@!$&()*+,;=[]\ \s`) and allows international characters and numbers. This enables organizations to be created with non-English characters and numeric names, consistent with Keycloak's validation rules.
userImpact: Users can now create organizations with names containing international characters and numbers, improving support for global users and SaaS deployments.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies across the project including boto3, cfn-lint, google-cloud-bigquery, types-PyYAML, types-pytz, and typing_extensions. These routine updates include patch versions for stability improvements and minor versions for new features in type stubs and utility libraries.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2241 latest upsolve export
summary: Updated the latest upsolve export to resolve a regression dashboard issue where link filters pop-up was not displaying correctly. The export includes a fix for corrupt filter state that was causing the problem.
userImpact: Users can now view the link filters pop-up on the regression dashboard without encountering display issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2177: User permissions tech design

userImpact: No direct user impact.

title: UP-2229 and UP-2217 add JSON data type
summary: Added support for DuckDB's JSON data type to handle cases where data cannot be mapped to a list or structure, such as multi-typed arrays. This enables successful ingestion of datasets with heterogeneous nested data. Also refactored CI/CD pipeline configuration to run app and data plane tests only when relevant scope_common changes are made, reducing unnecessary pipeline overhead.
userImpact: Users can now successfully onboard datasets with complex multi-typed nested data structures that previously failed during schema inference.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2224 update alert rule query guides
summary: Updates alert rule query guides and documentation that were missed in a previous MR. The changes include corrections to the alert rule query implementation in the axios onboarding demo and updates to the query overview documentation and related assets.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-2201: Include jobs referencing datasets using models in model activity log
summary: Updated the model activity log to include jobs that reference datasets using models. This expands the visibility of model usage by capturing indirect references through dataset-aware jobs, providing a more comprehensive audit trail of model interactions.
userImpact: Users can now see all jobs that use models through datasets in the model activity log, giving better visibility into model usage across their data pipelines.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2167: Update CFTs with email parameters
summary: Updated CloudFormation Templates (CFTs) across multiple deployment environments to support email configuration parameters. Added email-related parameter definitions to dev, prod, axios-sts, and expel-sts platform configuration files, and updated the deployment script to properly handle these new parameters.
userImpact: Email configuration parameters can now be managed dynamically across all deployment environments through CloudFormation, enabling environment-specific email settings without code changes.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2224 convert alert rule required fields
summary: Alert rule validation now requires `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` columns instead of `timestamp` and `value`. This resolves naming conflicts with existing table columns that were causing bugs for users. The change includes a query error sanitizer to handle cases where columns don't exist.
userImpact: Users must update their alert rule configurations to use `metric_timestamp` and `metric_value` column names, eliminating conflicts with reserved table column names.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Deploy multi tenancy feature branch
summary: This merge request deploys the multi-tenancy feature branch, introducing organization-scoped client and user management. The changes refactor user creation and client provisioning to use organization names instead of realms, and automatically create Keycloak organizations with default settings when new clients are provisioned. This enables the platform to support multiple independent organizations with isolated authentication and authorization contexts.
userImpact: Users can now manage multiple independent organizations with isolated authentication contexts, and new clients are automatically provisioned with organization-specific scopes and Keycloak organization configurations.
author: alex380125

title: UP-2225 fix hallucination chart
summary: Fixed a hallucination chart query that was not respecting the time range filter. The fix wraps the date range condition in brackets within the SQL query to properly apply the date filtering to the metrics data.
userImpact: The hallucination chart now correctly displays data filtered by the selected time range instead of showing unfiltered results.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] prevent data plane local dev script from overwriting client secret if already set
summary: The data plane local development startup script was unconditionally overwriting the client secret environment variable, preventing developers from testing against dev environments with pre-configured credentials. This change adds a check to preserve an already-set client secret instead of overwriting it.
userImpact: Developers can now run the local dev script against dev environments without losing their client secret configuration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: update shield API key
summary: Updated the Shield API key used in integration tests. This is a maintenance change to keep test credentials current and ensure integration tests continue to function properly.
userImpact: No direct user impact
