title: UP-1609 update timescale connection type
summary: Updated the connection type identifier for Timescale in the database connections configuration. The correct connection type is now properly reflected in the connections schema.
userImpact: Timescale database connections will now be correctly recognized and configured with the proper connection type.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: Faker (~30.3.0 → ~30.4.0) and uvicorn (~0.31.0 → ~0.32.0). Faker adds support for male/female names and prefix/suffix for the hi_IN locale, while uvicorn adds official Python 3.13 support and a warning when max_request_limit is exceeded.
userImpact: Updated dependencies bring improved locale support for name generation and official Python 3.13 compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1518 - Add graceful termination for ECS tasks
summary: Implements graceful termination for ECS tasks by catching SIGTERM signals and allowing running jobs a grace period (default 15 seconds) to complete before forcefully terminating. When ECS sends the termination signal, the job agent stops accepting new work, allows in-progress jobs to finish, and then marks any remaining jobs as failed before cleanly shutting down.
userImpact: ECS tasks can now shut down gracefully, ensuring running jobs have time to complete and preventing data loss or duplicate job execution when containers are being drained.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1609] upgrade upsolve image
summary: Upgraded the Upsolve Docker image to a newer version and fixed a Timescale connection issue. This update ensures compatibility with current dependencies and resolves connection problems in the Upsolve service.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved Upsolve service stability and corrected Timescale database connectivity.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [no-ref] fix local dev upsolve set up
summary: Fixed a 401 Unauthorized error occurring in local development when attempting to retrieve the Upsolve tenant token on the UI. The fix adjusts the docker-compose configuration to resolve authentication issues with the Upsolve service endpoint.
userImpact: Developers can now successfully retrieve Upsolve tenant tokens in local development environments without encountering authentication errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including authzed/spicedb (v1.37.0 → v1.37.1) and mypy (v1.11.1 → v1.12.0). Changes applied to lint requirements and Docker Compose configuration.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1592: Add dataset locator validations and unit tests
summary: Added comprehensive validations to ensure that created, available, or patched datasets comply with dataset locator schema requirements. Introduced a new datasets_operator module to centralize dataset validation logic, with corresponding unit tests covering various validation scenarios and edge cases.
userImpact: Users will now receive clear validation errors if they attempt to create or update datasets with invalid locator information, preventing downstream failures and data integrity issues.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1601 - Add correction for 0 indexing shield connector pages
summary: Shield connector was using 0-indexed pagination while the rest of the system expects 1-indexed pages. Added a correction in the Shield connector to adjust page indices from Scope's 1-based indexing to Shield's 0-based indexing, ensuring consistent pagination behavior across the platform.
userImpact: Shield connector pagination now works correctly with the system's standard 1-based page indexing.
author: peter496

title: UP-1600 - UP-1654 - UP-1430 - Move SQLAlchemy code to 2.0 style from legacy style, remove issue of ambiguous sorting
summary: Migrated SQLAlchemy code from 1.4 legacy `.query()` style to 2.0 modern style across all repository classes, a prerequisite for future async support. Added automatic default ordering by ID descending to all queries, eliminating the need for redundant sorting and fixing ambiguous sort issues in infinite scroll scenarios.
userImpact: Users benefit from more consistent and predictable query ordering behavior, particularly in infinite scroll pagination, while the codebase gains a maintainable foundation for eventual async database access.
author: peter496

title: UP-1592: Expand dataset locator concept
summary: Extended the dataset locator concept to include locator schema definitions on connectors and moved `data_file_type` into the dataset locator structure. This change enables more flexible dataset identification by allowing connectors to specify which fields are required to locate datasets, with proper validation and type information. Database migration included to restructure dataset locator fields with hardcoded IDs for easier future updates.
userImpact: Frontend contracts are broken by this change; datasets now use expanded locator objects instead of standalone data_file_type fields, and connectors now expose dataset_locator_schema to specify required locator fields.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1590 - Break up job_executor.py to smaller pieces
summary: Breaking up the monolithic job_executor.py file into smaller, focused modules for better maintainability and testability. The refactor separates executor logic into dedicated files (alert_check, connector_test, fetch_data, list_datasets, metrics_calculation, schedule_jobs, and schema_inference executors) with no functional changes to the system.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: UP-1597 webhook alert routing
summary: Implements webhook notification routing based on alert rules' linked webhooks, ensuring alerts are only sent to the webhooks configured for their specific alert rule. Also fixes a security issue where alerts could reference any alert rule ID in the system without validating it belonged to the same model.
userImpact: Webhooks now receive only the alerts associated with their configured alert rules, and cross-model alert rule references are prevented.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1597 alert rule webhook routing
summary: Added API endpoints and state management to link alert rules to webhooks for notification routing. This is part 1 of a 2-part change that establishes the data model and API layer; the next MR will update the alert notification routing to utilize these links.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage links between alert rules and webhooks via the API to configure webhook-based alert notifications.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1041 - Add parallelism to dataplane execution
summary: Adds parallel execution to the dataplane job agent, allowing multiple jobs to run concurrently within memory constraints. Each job runs in its own thread or process, with memory allocation tracked and reclaimed when jobs complete. Also makes MemorySecretsClient thread-safe to support parallel integration testing.
userImpact: Dataplane can now execute multiple jobs in parallel, improving throughput for workloads with multiple concurrent tasks while respecting memory limits.
author: peter496

title: chore: upsolve update to 0.1.2
summary: Updated upsolve container image to version 0.1.2 and applied the corresponding patch file. This includes updating the base image tag in the Dockerfile and replacing the patch configuration.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1435 job queue management design tweaks
summary: Updated job queue management documentation to clarify the "move to front of queue" action with a self-loop transition from queued to queued state, and added a previously missed state to the acceptance criteria documentation.
userImpact: Users have clearer documentation of job queue state transitions and the behavior of queue prioritization actions.
author: ianmcgraw

title: NO-REF: Use new file for tracking current in memory mock Keycloak client
summary: Fixed a race condition in local development where multiple processes competed for the same JSON file tracking the in-memory mock Keycloak client. Changed the implementation to use a separate temporary file for each process, eliminating the need for shared file access and preventing synchronization issues.
userImpact: Local development no longer experiences race conditions when running multiple processes with the mock Keycloak authentication client.
author: peter496

title: Add unify-app hosted in vercel origin to CORS
summary: Added https://unify-frontend-app.vercel.app to the CORS allowed origins list to support Vercel deployments of the frontend application. This enables the frontend served from Vercel to communicate with the backend API without CORS blocking.
userImpact: Users accessing the frontend from the Vercel-hosted deployment can now successfully make API requests to the backend without encountering CORS errors.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-1041 - Initial changes to set up multithreaded job runs
summary: Added foundational support for multithreaded job execution by introducing job priority and memory requirement tracking to the jobs database table, new job state for cancellation, and memory-aware job dequeuing that filters results based on agent capacity. Refactored JobsRepository.submit_job to accept a struct of arguments with sensible defaults, removing responsibility for initialization state from callers.
userImpact: Job agents can now report their available memory capacity and receive only jobs that fit within their constraints, enabling safer parallel job execution.
author: peter496

title: UP-1435 job queue management tech design
summary: Added a technical design document outlining job queue management operations, including job cancellation, retry logic, state transitions, and priority-based queue ordering. The design introduces a priority system (USER_FACING, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) to support operations like moving jobs to the front of the queue and clarifies how job clones are tracked across retries and reruns.
userImpact: Users can now understand and rely on predictable job queue behavior including the ability to prioritize urgent jobs, cancel running jobs with clear state transitions, and track job lineage across retries.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1582 | Remove task_name from allowed shield filters
summary: Removed the `task_name` filter from the allowed filtering fields in the Shield connector to prevent unintended access to other tasks' data. The Shield API combines filters with AND logic, so the `task_name` filter posed a security risk when exposed to users.
userImpact: Users can no longer filter Shield inferences by task_name, ensuring they can only access data from their current task.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1493: Scripts to generate expel sample tabular dataset in s3
summary: Added scripts and utilities to generate a sample tabular dataset for the Expel use case, including a Python script that creates realistic synthetic data with configurable parameters for testing and validation purposes. The dataset generation can now be run locally or uploaded directly to S3, with parameters for controlling the number of days sampled and inferences per day to support various testing scenarios.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: alex380125

title: chore: make cpu and memory configurable as parameters
summary: CPU and memory allocations for ECS services are now configurable parameters across multiple CloudFormation stacks, replacing hardcoded values. This allows operators to right-size resource requests per deployment environment without modifying stack templates.
userImpact: Operators can now adjust CPU and memory reservations for services (auth, spicedb, app plane, data plane, frontend, and grafana) via CloudFormation parameters during stack creation or updates.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Feat redis infra
summary: Introduces Redis stack infrastructure support with serverless caching capabilities for the application plane. Adds CloudFormation templates to provision Amazon ElastiCache Redis clusters, subnet groups, and cache usage limit configurations, along with secrets management for Redis authentication.
userImpact: Applications can now leverage Redis caching infrastructure with serverless cache support and cache usage limits to improve performance and control costs.
author: jdb-arthur

title: NO-REF: Change multiprocessing method from fork to spawn
summary: Changed the multiprocessing start method from `fork` to `spawn` to resolve stability issues in CI tests. The fork method was triggering deprecation warnings in Python 3.12 due to multithreading in the FastAPI/Uvicorn process, and spawn provides more reliable behavior at the cost of slightly longer test execution time.
userImpact: CI tests are now more stable and less prone to deadlocks when using multiprocessing with multithreaded FastAPI servers.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Whitespace changes and python minor version CI change
summary: Updated CI base image to run on a higher Python minor version to resolve a bug in the current version. Whitespace changes made across application files to trigger builds.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: peter496

title: chore(deps): update dependency markupsafe to v3
summary: MarkupSafe dependency updated from v2.1.5 to v3.0.0, which introduces support for Python 3.13's free-threaded build and drops support for Python 3.7 and 3.8. This is a major version bump that modernizes packaging metadata and improves escaping performance by 40%.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a dependency update that improves performance and future Python compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: Faker (30.1.0 → 30.3.0) and black (24.8.0 → 24.10.0). These minor version updates include performance improvements, bug fixes, and Python 3.13 support in black.
userImpact: No direct user impact. These are routine dependency maintenance updates.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1578
summary: Fixed GitLab CI pipeline conditions to ensure the Update-Frontend-Version step runs only on the main branch, preventing unintended MR merges and allowing independent FE image updates without triggering branch-specific job logic.
userImpact: Frontend version updates can now be applied to the main branch without accidentally merging unrelated MRs or running pipeline steps on developer branches.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1578 fix auto merging mrs
summary: Fixed an issue preventing automatic merging of merge requests. A configuration adjustment in the GitLab CI pipeline now allows MRs to be automatically merged as intended.
userImpact: Merge requests that should be automatically merged will now complete successfully without manual intervention.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1379 - Add exclude query param to get_jobs endpoint, remove unused job type, lower retry count for scheduled jobs
summary: Added `exclude_kinds` query parameter to the `get_jobs` endpoint, allowing users to filter out specific job types from results. When both `kinds` and `exclude_kinds` are specified, exclusions take precedence. Additionally, removed an unused duplicate job type and fixed a PostgreSQL compatibility issue by reducing the scheduled job retry count from 2³¹ to 2³¹-1, preventing integer overflow errors in production.
userImpact: Users can now exclude specific job types from their queries using the `exclude_kinds` parameter, and scheduled jobs no longer cause 500 errors in PostgreSQL deployments due to integer overflow.
author: peter496

title: UP-1410: log chart bugfix
summary: Fixed a log chart configuration issue in the CloudFormation monitoring template. This follows up on a previous merge that was accidentally merged and required correction to restore proper chart behavior.
userImpact: Log charts in Arthur Scope monitoring now display correctly on ECS deployments.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1410: add upsolve monitoring support
summary: Added upsolve monitoring support to CloudFormation templates, extending monitoring dashboard and alarms to include upsolve service metrics. Charts automatically updated to display upsolve stats, with manual rate chart and log view configurations added following AWS CloudWatch best practices for ECS Container Insights.
userImpact: Users can now monitor upsolve service health and performance metrics alongside other services in the monitoring dashboard and configured alarms.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1573 | Allow for empty webhook header dict update
summary: Previously, webhook header updates with an empty dictionary would be omitted rather than applied. This fix allows webhooks to be properly updated with empty header dicts, ensuring that header removals are correctly persisted.
userImpact: Users can now successfully clear webhook headers by updating with an empty dictionary instead of the update being silently ignored.
author: ooizig

title: chore(deps): update dependency redis to v5.1.1
summary: Updates the redis-py dependency from v5.1.0 to v5.1.1, a patch release that includes three bug fixes: corrected return types for Redis Set commands, fixed partial Hiredis availability issues, and resolved async pipeline failures with certain cluster commands.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal dependency update with stability improvements for Redis operations.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1491 metrics and query docs
summary: Added comprehensive external-facing product documentation for Arthur's metrics system and query language, including guidance on writing queries for dashboards and alert rules. The documentation covers available metrics, query syntax, filter options, and step-by-step instructions for creating alert rules with accompanying screenshots.
userImpact: Users now have clear documentation to understand metrics, write custom queries for dashboards and alerts, and create alert rules with proper filter configuration.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1570 fix toxicity distribution dashboard
summary: The toxicity distribution dashboard chart was computing a distribution of sketch medians for each 5-minute interval instead of a distribution of all values merged across the interval using sketch_merge(). This caused dashboard counts to be severely undercounted (e.g., 500 counts instead of 43.5k total inferences). The fix properly merges all sketch data and computes the probability mass function to accurately represent the full distribution of toxicity scores.
userImpact: The toxicity distribution dashboard now displays accurate distribution counts that correctly reflect the total number of inferences across the selected time period.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1490 webhooks product guide
summary: Added comprehensive webhooks product guide with practical examples demonstrating integration with Slack and Jira. The documentation includes step-by-step instructions, screenshots, and code examples to help users understand and implement webhook functionality.
userImpact: Users can now reference detailed webhook documentation and examples to set up integrations with external platforms like Slack and Jira.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1231: point app plane to on-prem upsolve
summary: Updated app plane configuration to point to on-premise Upsolve service instead of external endpoint. This change enables bootstrap initialization on AWS and includes updates to CloudFormation templates, Docker Compose configuration, and related operator/helper code.
userImpact: App plane now correctly connects to the on-premise Upsolve service and can successfully bootstrap on AWS deployments.
author: alex380125

title: chore(deps): update dependency authzed to v1
summary: Updated authzed dependency from v0.19.0 to v1.0.0. The authzed library released v1.0.0 with no breaking changes, providing access to latest features and improvements.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest authzed-py library improvements and features with no breaking changes to existing functionality.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update dependency cfn-lint to ~=1.16.0
summary: Updated the cfn-lint dependency from ~=1.15.0 to ~=1.16.0. This is a minor version bump of the AWS CloudFormation linter that ensures the project uses the latest available checks and improvements for CloudFormation template validation.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-846 - Change schedule jobs job to retry (nearly) infinite times
summary: Increased the maximum retry attempts for schedule jobs to nearly infinite (sys.maxsize) to prevent temporary failures from exhausting the retry budget and halting the entire schedule. This addresses a critical issue where a brief outage caused all retries to be consumed, effectively stopping all scheduled jobs.
userImpact: Schedule jobs will no longer fail permanently due to temporary failures consuming the limited retry budget, ensuring scheduled tasks continue to execute reliably.
author: peter496

title: UP-846 - Fix import, reduce test parallelism to reduce flakiness
summary: Fixed an import statement in the aggregation matcher module and reduced test parallelism in CI configuration to improve test reliability. These changes address flakiness issues in the test suite.
userImpact: Tests now run more reliably with reduced flakiness from parallel execution.
author: peter496

title: chore: enable upsolve ecs healthcheck & docker-compose fix
summary: Re-enabled ECS healthcheck for the upsolve service by adding curl to the upsolve Docker image. Also fixed docker-compose configuration and optimized the Dockerfile by combining apt-get commands and cleaning up package cache to reduce image size.
userImpact: The upsolve service now passes ECS healthchecks in production and docker-compose environments without requiring workarounds.
author: alex380125

title: chore: upsolve exec and disable ecs healthchecks
summary: Disabled ECS health checks that were causing service instability while ALB health checks remain active. Enabled ECS exec functionality to allow direct container debugging for troubleshooting service issues.
userImpact: The Upsolve service on ECS will now remain stable without ECS health check failures, and operators can use exec commands to connect to the container for debugging.
author: jdb-arthur

title: UP-1468 | Add default order by id to queries
summary: Added a secondary default sort by ID to alert queries to ensure consistent and predictable pagination when primary sort fields contain non-unique values. This prevents ambiguous ordering and potential inconsistent results across paginated queries when multiple records share the same sort key.
userImpact: Users will now receive consistent and predictable alert results across paginated queries, eliminating potential gaps or duplicates caused by ambiguous ordering.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1528 add default limit and page size for raw data retrieval job
summary: Raw data retrieval jobs previously fetched all available data by default, potentially retrieving hundreds or thousands of inferences. This change adds default limit filters to raw data retrieval jobs, ensuring the Shield connector only loads a subset of data unless the user explicitly requests more.
userImpact: Raw data retrieval jobs now return a manageable default subset of results instead of potentially overwhelming amounts of data.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency faker to ~=30.1.0
summary: Updated the Faker dependency from ~=30.0.0 to ~=30.1.0, which adds PIN Code range and union territories support in the `en_IN` address provider. This is a minor version bump handled via automated dependency management.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1521 fix data retrieval job status on failure
summary: Fixed exception handling in the raw data retrieval job to properly mark the job as failed when data upload fails. Previously, upload errors were not being caught, causing jobs to report success even when data could not be delivered to the platform.
userImpact: Users will now see accurate job status when data retrieval jobs fail due to upload errors or connection issues.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated redis-py from 5.0.8 to 5.1.0 and uvicorn from 0.30.6 to 0.31.0. These minor version updates bring performance improvements, bug fixes, and new features like client-side caching in redis-py, while keeping dependencies current with non-major version constraints.
userImpact: No direct user impact; dependency updates improve system stability and enable access to new features in underlying libraries if adopted.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1519 reuse redis client across threads
summary: Redis client is now reused across threads instead of creating new connections for each thread, improving performance and reducing resource consumption. This addresses connection pooling inefficiencies in multi-threaded environments.
userImpact: Applications using Redis will experience improved performance and lower memory overhead in multi-threaded workloads due to efficient connection reuse.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1519 fix redis connection 2
summary: Fixed a Redis connection error that was preventing the application from connecting to the serverless cache endpoint. The fix updates the connection configuration in settings and dependencies to properly resolve the cache host, resolving the "No such file or directory" error that occurred during previous attempts.
userImpact: Redis connections to the serverless cache now work correctly without connection errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1519 fix redis connections
summary: Fixed SSL redis connections by updating Redis configuration settings and dependencies. Changes were made to the app configuration, dependency setup, and CloudFormation template to properly support secure Redis connections.
userImpact: SSL-secured Redis connections now work correctly, resolving connection failures for deployments using encrypted Redis endpoints.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1516 fix shield connector
summary: The shield connector was incorrectly handling pagination when the `page` filter was not explicitly set, causing it to miss available inferences. This fix ensures the connector properly pages through all available results regardless of filter configuration.
userImpact: Users can now reliably retrieve all inferences from the shield connector without needing to manually set pagination filters.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated minor versions of non-major dependencies including authzed (0.18.2 → 0.19.0) and authzed/spicedb (v1.36.2 → v1.37.0). These are routine dependency updates with no breaking changes.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Dependency versions updated to receive latest minor updates and improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Roll back user creation on failure
summary: Previously, if Keycloak failed to accept a PUT request to update a user's type, the user would be created in a malformed state with missing role bindings. This caused bootstrap operations to appear successful while critical org role bindings remained unassigned. The fix now rolls back user creation if the user type update fails, preventing malformed user definitions.
userImpact: User creation during bootstrap operations will no longer leave users in a broken state with missing role bindings if Keycloak type updates fail.
author: peter496

title: chore: remove old stacks from a bad rebase or something
summary: Removed 8 stale CloudFormation stack files that were accidentally left in the repository from a prior rebase. These files are no longer needed and were causing clutter in the codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: jdb-arthur

title: fix: grafana target group
summary: Fixed an incorrect target group configuration in the Grafana ECS CloudFormation stack. This ensures Grafana instances are properly registered with the correct target group for load balancing.
userImpact: Grafana instances now correctly connect to the proper target group, restoring load balancer routing to Grafana.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: new_stacks
summary: Introduces a comprehensive baseline of CloudFormation stack templates for deploying Arthur infrastructure on AWS. The stacks cover core infrastructure (VPC, IAM, ECS, load balancers), database components (RDS, TimescaleDB), and application services (authentication, SpiceDB, app/data planes, Grafana), organized into modular groups for flexibility and maintainability.
userImpact: Users can now deploy Arthur infrastructure on AWS using structured CloudFormation stacks organized by infrastructure tier and component.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Update dependency numpy to v2
summary: NumPy dependency updated from v1 to v2 (specifically v2.1.1), a major version bump that includes maintenance fixes and improvements. This brings the project to the latest stable NumPy release with bug fixes and enhanced compatibility with Python 3.10-3.13.
userImpact: Users benefit from NumPy 2 improvements including bug fixes, better reference counting, and improved array interface handling.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency Faker to v30
summary: Updated Faker dependency from v29 to v30. This major version includes a change to force slug generation to always use ASCII characters.
userImpact: Slug generation now consistently produces ASCII-only output across all Faker usage.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency datasketches to ~=5.1.0
summary: Updated the datasketches dependency from ~=5.0.2 to ~=5.1.0 across app_plane and data_plane requirements. This minor version update brings the latest improvements and fixes from the Apache DataSketches library.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest Apache DataSketches improvements and bug fixes in the minor version 5.1.0 release.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1495 add better URL parsing to API client
summary: The API client previously failed when configured with a URL ending in a slash. This fix improves URL parsing to extract and use only the scheme and host, eliminating configuration errors from trailing slashes.
userImpact: Users can now configure the API client with URLs that have trailing slashes without encountering failures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-846 - Refactor how dataset schemas are defined, support column aliasing
summary: Refactored dataset schema model to introduce a rigid structure with unique column IDs and column aliasing support. Previously, schemas were stored as serialized data without friendly types, making it difficult to handle column name conflicts during joins and implement schema edits in the UI. The new `DatasetSchema` model includes an alias mask for friendly column names, a list of columns with globally unique IDs, and an alias mapping that propagates correctly through joined datasets, preventing column conflicts upfront.
userImpact: Users can now join datasets with conflicting column names by using column aliases, and the API provides strongly-typed schema models for UI schema editing instead of opaque serialized data.
author: peter496

title: UP-394 proof read API docs
summary: This merge request corrects typos and HTTP response codes throughout the API documentation across all major router endpoints. The changes ensure that the API docs accurately reflect the actual behavior and improve clarity for API consumers.
userImpact: API consumers can now rely on accurate and corrected API documentation, including proper response codes and fixed typos across all endpoints.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-394 fix API docs SSO
summary: The API docs Authorize button was redirecting to an incorrect URL that didn't match the load balancer's `/api` prefix, causing requests to fail and display a not-found page. This fix updates the redirect URL to properly match the `/api` prefix and updates the Keycloak client configuration to allow the corrected redirect URL.
userImpact: Users can now successfully authenticate through the Authorize button in the API documentation without encountering not-found errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Add rate/count of failures for toxicity
summary: Added rate and count metrics for toxicity failures to the MVP Shield dashboard, providing better visibility into failure patterns. The dashboard now includes consistent data point intervals across bar charts for improved consistency and readability.
userImpact: Users can now monitor toxicity failure rates and counts through the dashboard, with improved consistency in chart spacing for easier metric comparison.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-509: enable container insights
summary: Container Insights has been enabled for the ECS cluster to provide enhanced monitoring and observability. This enables collection of container-level metrics that can be utilized for building out CloudWatch dashboards.
userImpact: Users can now leverage Container Insights metrics in CloudWatch dashboards for improved visibility into ECS container performance and health.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1473 database bootstrap commands
summary: Added automated database bootstrap commands to create `upsolve` and `spicedb` databases on startup, with idempotent checks to skip creation if databases already exist. Also upgraded upsolve to version 0.0.7 for x86 architecture support and removed hardcoded Grafana container naming to prevent conflicts with other Grafana instances.
userImpact: Database initialization now happens automatically during container startup without requiring manual SQL scripts, and the system supports both x86 and ARM architectures.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1230-bugfix: Gate upsolve bootstrap step behind flag
summary: The upsolve bootstrap step (db-patches) is now gated behind a configuration flag to prevent unintended execution during initialization. This change adds a feature flag to the platform bootstrap sequence, allowing operators to control whether the database patch step runs.
userImpact: Operators can now control whether the database patch step executes during application bootstrap by setting the feature flag, preventing disruptions from unintended automatic database migrations.
author: alex380125

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies: authzed/spicedb (v1.35.3 → v1.36.2) and cfn-lint (~=1.14.0 → ~=1.15.0). These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep tools and libraries current with their latest compatible versions.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Draft: UP-1231: CFT for upsolve
summary: This merge request adds CloudFormation Templates (CFTs) for deploying Upsolve as an ECS service within the Arthur Scope infrastructure. The changes include new CFT definitions for Upsolve ECS deployment, updated security groups and load balancer configurations, secrets management integration, and build/CI pipeline setup for the Upsolve service.
userImpact: Users can now deploy and manage Upsolve service infrastructure using CloudFormation, enabling automated provisioning and lifecycle management of the Upsolve component within the Scope platform.
author: alex380125

title: [no-ref] fix app start up
summary: Fixed ModuleNotFoundError that prevented the app from starting up during import of the permissions router. The issue was caused by an incorrect import path for the Authorizer module that was introduced in a previous change.
userImpact: The application now starts up successfully without import errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1471: Fix timestamp typo
summary: Fixed a timestamp field typo in the API model that was causing incorrect data retrieval behavior. The typo was corrected in the data retrieval model, router, and corresponding unit tests.
userImpact: Timestamp fields in data retrieval API responses are now correctly named and accessible.
author: peter496

title: UP-1471 - Include status and nulled body on GET retrieved data if not loaded.
summary: GET data retrieval endpoints now include a status field and null body when data hasn't been loaded yet. This change simplifies state management by making it clearer to clients when data is unavailable or expired, enabling better polling and retry logic.
userImpact: Users can now check the status field to determine if retrieved data is ready or still loading, and safely handle null body responses without additional API calls.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Add flags to prevent recursive API models from getting split into input / output
summary: Added configuration flags to prevent FastAPI from automatically splitting recursive API models into separate Input/Output variants in OpenAPI documentation. This resolves an issue where certain model field combinations (nullability, defaults, response_model usage) caused unintended model duplication, improving schema clarity and reducing unnecessary type variants.
userImpact: API schemas no longer generate redundant Input/Output model variants for recursive types, resulting in cleaner OpenAPI documentation and simpler client generation.
author: peter496

title: UP-897 | Add permissions check endpoint
summary: Adds a new `/api/v1/permissions/check` endpoint that allows checking permissions for a given resource and permission names. The endpoint supports bulk permission checking in a single call to SpiceDB and includes validation for request sizes with appropriate upper limits.
userImpact: Users can now query whether specific permissions are allowed for a resource without making multiple individual permission checks.
author: ooizig

title: UP-1314 | Add project level jobs filtering based on model_id
summary: Added project-level jobs filtering based on the model_id field, enabling more granular job queries on polymorphic job specs. The implementation dynamically extracts and filters job spec subclasses that have the model_id field defined, reducing the need for manual configuration as new job types are introduced.
userImpact: Users can now filter jobs at the project level using the model_id field to identify and retrieve model-specific job instances.
author: ooizig

title: Update dependency anyio to ~=4.6.0
summary: Updated anyio dependency from ~=4.5.0 to ~=4.6.0. This release includes critical fixes for asyncio cancel scopes, resolving 100% CPU usage during task group exits and fixing cancel scope propagation issues, along with improved task uncancellation consistency.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved asyncio cancel scope behavior with fixes for CPU spikes and cancel error propagation in edge cases.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1212 UP-1213 | FetchData job execution
summary: Implements FetchData job execution for data retrieval operations, enabling asynchronous job-based data retrieval with proper job tracking and permissions. This MR adds database schema support, job execution endpoints, and extends the data retrieval operator to handle job submission and status polling with enhanced security controls.
userImpact: Users can now submit and track FetchData jobs for data retrieval operations asynchronously, with improved permission isolation ensuring data retrievals cannot be accessed across models.
author: ooizig

title: [no-ref] add call flow diagram for raw data retrieval job
summary: Added a call flow diagram to the Raw Data Retrieval technical documentation to help developers understand how the raw data retrieval job works internally. This improves documentation clarity without changing any functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 fix CFT secret IAM access
summary: Fixed IAM access permissions for CloudFormation template to allow task execution role to retrieve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager. Tasks were failing with AccessDeniedException when attempting to pull newly created secrets due to missing secretsmanager:GetSecretValue permissions in the CFT configuration.
userImpact: Tasks can now successfully retrieve secrets from AWS Secrets Manager without encountering AccessDeniedException errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 move root user credentials to a secret
summary: The previous ECS deployment was failing because the task definition was referencing a Systems Manager parameter (secret) that didn't exist. This fix moves root user credentials into AWS Secrets Manager and updates the CloudFormation templates to reference the secret properly with its ARN.
userImpact: Deployments will now succeed without the invalid Systems Manager parameter error that was blocking ECS task definition creation.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 add configurations for missing bootstrap params
summary: Added missing environment variables for the data plane client secret in the ECS bootstrap task and added missing parameters for root user bootstrapping in the CloudFormation stack configuration. These configurations were required for proper bootstrap initialization but were absent from the template and parameter files.
userImpact: Bootstrap operations now have all required configuration parameters, enabling proper initialization of the data plane and root user without manual configuration workarounds.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-1387 fix ECS entrypoint for latest bootstrap command
summary: Updated the ECS start command in the CloudFormation template to use the latest bootstrap script. The ECS entrypoint was referencing an outdated bootstrap command, causing startup issues with the containerized application.
userImpact: ECS deployments now start correctly with the updated bootstrap command.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Update dependency Faker to v29
summary: Updated Faker dependency from v28.4.1 to v29.0.0. This release includes a fix for `pydecimal` distribution when called with a range across 0.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency anyio to ~=4.5.0
summary: Updated the anyio dependency from ~=4.4.0 to ~=4.5.0. This minor version bump includes significant performance improvements to Lock and Semaphore on asyncio (up to 50%), new Python 3.13 compatibility features for anyio.Path, expanded process management capabilities, and several bug fixes addressing subprocess handling, cancellation detection, and socket stream compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved async lock/semaphore performance, better Python 3.13 support in path operations, more flexible process spawning options, and fixes for subprocess, task group, and socket handling edge cases.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1230: docker-compose set up for upsolve
summary: Added docker-compose configuration to set up the upsolve service with PostgreSQL database initialization scripts and bootstrap job integration. This enables local development environment support for upsolve alongside the existing app-plane and other services.
userImpact: Developers can now run upsolve service locally using docker-compose, with automated database initialization and bootstrap job execution.
author: alex380125

title: UP-1346 - Correctly update latest_run_start update
summary: Fixed a bug where the `latest_run_start` timestamp was not being correctly updated when job state transitioned to running. This internal implementation detail was not exposed in API models, which caused it to be missed in test coverage until now.
userImpact: Job run timestamps are now accurately recorded when jobs transition to running state, ensuring correct tracking of job execution history.
author: peter496

title: UP-1467 - Dashboard updates
summary: Added a new inference count over time visualization to the dashboard to help users monitor inference trends. Improved clarity of sensitive data rule charts with more descriptive titles and corrected axis labels for better data interpretation.
userImpact: Users can now view inference count trends over time and better understand sensitive data rule metrics through clearer chart labels and titles.
author: peter496

title: feat(UP-4181): add "No Data in 7 Days" alert rule template
summary: This MR introduces a new "No Data in 7 Days" alert rule template for governance policies. It includes support for a configurable interval UI for policy alert rules, allowing users to set custom time windows for data availability monitoring. The template is pre-configured with a 7-day interval and integrated into the alert rules creation workflow.
userImpact: Users can now use a new "No Data in 7 Days" alert rule template when creating governance policies, and can customize alert rule intervals through the UI.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4321 Add missing metrics calculation schedule configuration warning
summary: Adds a configuration warning for missing metrics calculation schedule settings in the governance applications overview. The warning message is displayed in the policies section and helps users identify when metrics calculation schedules are not properly configured.
userImpact: Users now see a warning when metrics calculation schedule configuration is missing in their governance policies, helping them identify and resolve incomplete configurations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4329 Adjust policy assignment fetching
summary: Adjusted policy assignment fetching logic in the governance policies section. Modified the API infinite query types and refactored the policy assignments hook to use a new dedicated infinite hook, improving the efficiency and maintainability of fetching large datasets of policy assignments.
userImpact: Policy assignments in the governance interface now load more efficiently with improved pagination handling.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add typecheck scripts
summary: Added typechecking scripts to the monorepo and removed the unused `promptSaveState` utility that was causing type errors. The functionality is duplicated in the `arthur-engine` package, so removing it from shared-components simplifies the codebase and enables typechecking to run without errors.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4048 New Project redirect
summary: Retired the standalone `/projects/:id` landing page in favor of the workspace-scoped applications view. The `/projects/:id` route now redirects to `/workspaces/<wsId>/applications?project=<id>`. Project creation permission is now centrally gated via `WorkspacePermissions.canCreateProject`, with UI entry points (sidebar, modals, empty state) hidden for users lacking permission.
userImpact: Users accessing direct project links are now redirected to the workspace applications view, and users without project creation permission will no longer see create project options in the UI.
author: martin452

title: UP-4338 Make the stepper header sticky in policy creation flow
summary: The stepper header in the policy creation flow is now made sticky, keeping it visible as users scroll through multi-step forms. This improves navigation and context awareness during policy configuration by ensuring the step indicator remains accessible at the top of the viewport.
userImpact: Users can now scroll through policy creation steps while always having the stepper header visible, making it easier to track their current step and navigate between steps.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4334 Ensure properly displaying webhook in edit policy modal
summary: Fixed webhook display in the edit policy modal to ensure it renders correctly. The change modifies the edit-policy-modal component to properly handle webhook presentation when users are editing governance policies.
userImpact: Users will now see webhooks properly displayed when editing policy rules in the governance modal.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4337 Unify status badges
summary: Unified status badge components across the application by consolidating JobStatus and JobStatusBadge components into a shared StatusBadge component in the shared-components package. This refactoring reduces duplication and provides a consistent, reusable badge component for displaying job and policy compliance statuses throughout the UI.
userImpact: Users see a consistent visual treatment for all status badges across jobs and governance features, with improved component reusability for future development.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4327): update current violations widget on policy overview
summary: Updated the current violations widget on the policy overview page with improved UX, including capping the violations table to 5 rows, adding an "All applications" filter option, and refactoring components to use react-router Link and ComplianceStatusBadge for consistency. These changes make the violations overview more scannable and enhance the filtering capabilities for policy compliance monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now view a more focused current violations summary with pagination support, filter by all applications at once, and enjoy improved visual consistency in the policy overview interface.
author: lukasz94

title: fix(UP-4325): improve loading state in check history table
summary: Improved the loading state display in the check history table within the compliance checks tab. This fix enhances the user experience by ensuring the loading indicator is properly shown when check history data is being fetched.
userImpact: Users will now see a more accurate loading state when viewing the check history table in the governance policies compliance checks section.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4324): add sidebar trigger on Agent Discovery page
summary: Added a sidebar trigger component to the Agent Discovery page in the governance view. This change enables users to interact with the sidebar directly from the Agent Discovery page, improving navigation and discoverability of the sidebar functionality.
userImpact: Users can now trigger the sidebar from the Agent Discovery page for easier access to sidebar controls.
author: lukasz94

title: fix(UP-4336): shorten pending compliance status to "Evaluating"
summary: The pending compliance status label has been shortened from a longer phrase to simply "Evaluating" to improve UI clarity. This change affects the governance localization strings and was validated through visual regression testing.
userImpact: Users will now see a shorter, cleaner "Evaluating" status label when viewing pending compliance statuses in the governance interface.
author: lukasz94

title: fix(UP-4328): rename compliance checks filter to "Rule Type"
summary: Renamed the compliance checks filter label from its previous name to "Rule Type" for improved clarity and consistency in the governance policies interface. This change updates both the UI label in the compliance-checks-tab component and the corresponding i18n English localization file.
userImpact: Users will now see "Rule Type" as the filter label when viewing compliance checks, making it clearer what the filter controls.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4240 icon loading bug
summary: Fixed a CORS error that occurred when loading icons by downloading the icon asset locally instead of loading it from an external source. The cloudformation-launch-stack-button.png is now bundled with the application, eliminating cross-origin issues.
userImpact: Users will no longer experience CORS errors when loading icons during the AWS console installation onboarding flow.
author: martin452

title: fix(UP-3445): remove active/inactive engines tabs
summary: Removed the client-side active/inactive engines filtering tabs from the engines management interface. The previous implementation was buggy since filtering was only performed on the client side; server-side filtering will be implemented in a future release as a proper solution.
userImpact: Users will no longer see the active/inactive engine tabs that were providing inconsistent filtering results.
author: lukasz94

title: fix(UP-4268): surface actionable attestation submission errors
summary: Improved error handling in attestation submission by surfacing actionable error messages to users. When attestation submission fails, users now receive clear, specific error messages that explain what went wrong, enabling them to take corrective action.
userImpact: Users now receive specific, actionable error messages when attestation submission fails, making it easier to diagnose and resolve submission issues.
author: lukasz94

title: fix(UP-4267): governance UX blue text, rule link, stale tab
summary: Fixed multiple governance UX issues including blue text styling, rule linking, and stale tab handling in the policy detail and compliance checks sections. The policyId is now properly threaded through the NeedsAttentionSection component to enable correct linking and data association.
userImpact: Users can now correctly view and navigate policy rules with proper styling, and stale compliance check data no longer appears in the governance interface.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4235 Add polling to checks page to properly handle ongoing attestations
summary: Added polling functionality to the checks page to properly handle ongoing attestations. The implementation updates the attestation tracking hook and checks page components to refresh data at appropriate intervals, ensuring users always see the current status of in-progress attestations.
userImpact: Users will now see real-time updates of ongoing attestations on the checks page without needing to manually refresh.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Improve claude md by adding useEffect rules
summary: Added useEffect rules to CLAUDE.md to improve the Claude markdown documentation. This update provides developers with guidance on best practices for using React's useEffect hook, helping to establish consistent patterns for the team.
userImpact: Developers can now reference documented useEffect rules in CLAUDE.md when implementing React components.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4217: Improve compliance jobs widget error/status handling
summary: Improved error and status handling in the compliance jobs widget to provide clearer feedback to users. Enhanced the check-status widget, job-status badge, and compliance context components with better error messaging and status state management.
userImpact: Users now see more informative error messages and clearer status indicators when compliance jobs run, making it easier to understand job outcomes and troubleshoot issues.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat: add filters to policies list page (UP-4244)
summary: Added Status, Owner, and Application filter dropdowns to the Policies list page, following the same pattern as the Checks page. The `usePolicies` hook was extended with filter parameters (owner_group_id, compliance_status, model_id) that integrate with the backend API, and new translation strings were added for the filter UI.
userImpact: Users can now filter policies by compliance status, owner group, and application to quickly find relevant policies alongside the existing search functionality.
author: videetparekh1995

title: [no-ref] update trace table to accept row selection props
summary: Updated the trace table component to accept row selection props, enabling better control over table selection behavior. Changes were made to the useTable hook and TracesTable component to support these new configuration options.
userImpact: Users can now configure row selection behavior in trace tables through additional props.
author: ianmcgraw

title: feat(UP-4247): Add server-side sorting to governance checks grid
summary: Added server-side sorting capability to the governance checks grid. Users can now sort the governance checks data directly on the server, improving performance and user experience when working with large datasets. This change modifies the columns configuration and grid index to support the new sorting functionality.
userImpact: Users can now sort governance checks by different columns server-side, enabling more efficient data management in the governance checks grid.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4248): make the table full size
summary: Modified the governance checks table layout to use the full available width on the page. This improves the visibility and usability of the checks table by maximizing horizontal space utilization in the workspace governance view.
userImpact: The governance checks table now spans the full width of the page, providing better visibility of table content and improved user experience when viewing checks.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4238): refactor the edit attestation form
summary: Refactored the edit attestation form to improve code structure and maintainability. The changes reorganize the form components and types across multiple files, streamlining the implementation without altering user-facing functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact; internal restructuring of form implementation for improved code clarity.
author: lukasz94

title: Fix bg color of policy card rule badges
summary: Fixed the background color styling of rule badges in the policy card component. This visual adjustment ensures the badges display with the correct background color as intended.
userImpact: Policy card rule badges now display with the correct background color.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Make resizable code editor scrollable, fix panel collapsing logic
summary: Fixed the resizable code editor to properly handle scrolling when content overflows, and corrected panel collapsing logic in the CollapsibleConfigSection component. A new useFocusWithin hook was added to support improved focus management within collapsible sections.
userImpact: Users can now properly scroll within resizable code editors, and panel collapsing behavior works as expected without unintended side effects.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4220: Add policy dashboard to policy page
summary: A policy dashboard has been added to the policy page to provide users with analytics and insights about their policies. The dashboard is implemented in the policy overview tab and includes updated UI components and internationalization strings.
userImpact: Users can now view policy analytics and metrics directly on the policy page through the new policy dashboard component.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4236): add new loading state
summary: Added a new loading state to the governance checks view. This improves the UI feedback when data is being fetched, providing users with clearer indication of in-progress operations.
userImpact: Users now see an improved loading state while governance checks data is being retrieved.
author: lukasz94

title: feat: revalidate checks after attestation
summary: Added revalidation of compliance checks after an attestation is created. This ensures that the check status is automatically refreshed following attestation actions, keeping the compliance state current without requiring manual refresh.
userImpact: Users will now see compliance checks automatically revalidate after creating an attestation, ensuring accurate and up-to-date check status without manual intervention.
author: lukasz94

title: fix: app routes last pass
summary: Fixed app routes configuration and updated route references across governance-related views. This addresses routing issues in the application's workspace governance features, including checks, policies, and alerts components.
userImpact: Users will experience corrected navigation and routing behavior within the governance section of the application.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4223 Add previous checks section to compliance checks tab
summary: Added a new "Previous Checks" section to the compliance checks tab in policy governance views, allowing users to view historical compliance check results alongside current violations. This complements the existing current violations section and provides a complete audit trail of compliance checks.
userImpact: Users can now view a history of previous compliance checks in the compliance checks tab, enabling better tracking and review of policy compliance over time.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4225): add hasChanges check
summary: Added a hasChanges check to the SelectApplicationsStep component in the AssignPolicyModal. This validation ensures that policy application assignments are properly tracked for state management before proceeding with modal actions.
userImpact: Users will now see improved form state handling when assigning policies to applications, preventing incomplete submissions.
author: lukasz94

title: feat: refactor applications routing
summary: Refactored the applications routing structure to flatten the URL hierarchy from `/:projectId/:applicationId` to `/:applicationId`. This simplification removes unnecessary project-level routing nesting while updating breadcrumbs and all affected route references throughout the codebase.
userImpact: Application URLs are now simpler and more direct, with the routing structure streamlined to access applications by ID without project context in the URL path.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4216): add checks view
summary: Introduces a new Checks view in the Governance section that displays compliance checks with server-side filtering, search, and infinite scroll capabilities. The view integrates with the workspace compliance API, includes row-level action menus, and provides navigation links to associated policies and rules.
userImpact: Users can now view, search, filter, and interact with compliance checks in the Governance module with seamless navigation to related policies and rules.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4212, UP-4213 Fix loading spinner behavior in compliance checks tab
summary: Fixed loading spinner behavior in the compliance checks tab to properly handle attestation tracking state. The fix ensures loading indicators display correctly during policy compliance verification by introducing proper state management in the current violations section and refactoring related components.
userImpact: Users now see accurate loading spinner behavior when viewing compliance checks, preventing confusing or missing loading states during policy attestation operations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4215): unify header on policy screen
summary: Unified the header styling and layout on the policy list screen to align with design standards across the governance workspace. This change improves visual consistency and ensures a cohesive user interface throughout the policies management interface.
userImpact: Users will see a more consistent and unified header design on the policy list screen that matches the rest of the application interface.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4204 Create the edit rule view for policy level alerts
summary: Added edit and view flows for policy-level alert rules and attestation rules within the governance module. This includes new components for managing alert rule configurations at the policy level, along with a reusable AlertRuleEditForm component to consolidate edit functionality across different rule types.
userImpact: Users can now edit and manage alert rules and attestation rules directly within policy governance views.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4211): rebuild edit policy modal
summary: The edit policy modal has been rebuilt with improved component structure and organization. New dedicated step components for adding alert rules and attestation rules were introduced, with enhanced formatting and validation logic. This refactoring improves maintainability and UI/UX consistency within the governance policies interface.
userImpact: Users can now add alert and attestation rules to governance policies through a cleaner, more organized modal interface.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4209): close and redirect on assignment click
summary: Updated the AssignmentSuccessContent component to close and redirect users after they click on an assignment in the policy assignment modal. This improves the user workflow by automatically advancing to the next step after successful assignment instead of requiring manual navigation.
userImpact: Users are now automatically redirected after successfully assigning a policy, providing a smoother and more streamlined assignment experience.
author: lukasz94

title: Disable collapsibility in non-modal code editor views
summary: Disables collapsibility in non-modal code editor views, including the alert rule editor and configuration step components. This prevents users from unintentionally collapsing panels in these views where the editor panel should remain accessible.
userImpact: Users can no longer collapse the code editor panel in non-modal alert rule views, ensuring the editor remains visible and accessible during configuration.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4210): add unstable name matching for alert and attestation rules
summary: Added unstable name matching functionality for alert and attestation rules in governance policy modals and rule management components. This enhancement allows rules to be matched by name patterns in an unstable manner, providing more flexible rule configuration and selection across the policy editing interface.
userImpact: Users can now match alert and attestation rules using unstable name matching patterns when creating and editing governance policies.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4177 Fix attestation modal stale data
summary: Fixed stale data appearing in the attestation modal and policy detail modal components. The changes ensure that modal data is properly refreshed and synchronized, preventing users from seeing outdated information when interacting with policies and attestation workflows.
userImpact: Users no longer see stale or outdated data when opening attestation and policy detail modals.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4183): add create new group sentinel element
summary: Added a sentinel UI element for creating new groups in the policy governance interface. This enhances the group selection experience in both the edit policy modal and new policy creation flow by providing users with a clear option to add new groups directly from the dropdown.
userImpact: Users can now create new groups directly from the group selection dropdown in the policy governance interface.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4149): add remove policy modal
summary: Added a remove policy modal dialog component for governance policies. Users can now delete policies through a dedicated modal interface, improving the policy management workflow in the governance section.
userImpact: Users can now remove policies via a dedicated modal dialog with improved UI/UX in the governance policies management interface.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4182 Support multiline policy descriptions
summary: Added support for multiline policy descriptions in the governance policies UI. Policy descriptions can now span multiple lines in both the edit policy modal and new policy details step, improving usability when entering longer or formatted policy text.
userImpact: Users can now enter and view policy descriptions across multiple lines instead of being limited to a single line.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4184: Collapse config sections for SQL queries in the editor
summary: SQL query configuration sections in the code editor are now collapsible, improving readability and navigation for complex queries. New collapsible section components were introduced, and existing editor views for alert rules, webhooks, and custom aggregations were updated to use this capability.
userImpact: Users can now collapse and expand SQL query configuration sections in the editor, making it easier to focus on specific parts of complex query configurations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4205): add application rename
summary: Added application rename functionality to allow users to change application names within the governance and applications views. The feature includes UI updates to both the application details page and the governance applications layout, along with new internationalization strings for the rename action.
userImpact: Users can now rename applications directly from the application management interface.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4186): improve the empty state when there are no violations
summary: Improved the empty state UI displayed when there are no policy violations in the governance policies overview tab. The change enhances the user experience by providing a better visual presentation when no violations are present.
userImpact: Users now see an improved empty state message when viewing a policy with no violations, providing better clarity and context.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4187): update UI to match Figma
summary: Updated UI components in the governance applications and policies sections to match Figma design specifications. Changes affect the policy detail modal, compliant rules section, needs attention section, and overview tab components across multiple application views.
userImpact: Users will see updated visual styling and layout in the policy detail modals and policy overview sections that now align with current design specifications.
author: lukasz94

title: feat(UP-4167): change application redirect
summary: Changed the application redirect behavior in the governance policies interface. The MR updates the apps-governed-tab component to redirect applications differently and includes an OpenAPI schema update to reflect this change.
userImpact: Users navigating to governed applications in the policies view will now be redirected to a different location.
author: lukasz94

title: Compliance checks widget refactor
summary: Refactored the compliance checks widget to use a separate endpoint for verifying check completion status, replacing the previous polling approach. This change introduces additional API calls (one per app assigned to a policy) but only when a check is no longer in progress, avoiding repeated requests.
userImpact: No direct user impact. The compliance checks widget now more efficiently tracks check completion status with improved architecture.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Add configurable dev server port via VITE_PORT env var
summary: The dev server port is now configurable via the VITE_PORT environment variable, defaulting to 3023 if not set. This enables multiple frontend stacks to run simultaneously on different ports without hardcoded conflicts.
userImpact: Users can now run multiple frontend instances by setting the VITE_PORT environment variable to different port numbers.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: add schedule_compliance_jobs and compliance_policy_check job kinds
summary: Added two new job kinds to the system: `compliance_policy_check` and `schedule_compliance_jobs`. These new types are now available in the JobKind enum with full internationalization support. `schedule_compliance_jobs` is treated as a scheduler meta-job and is excluded from job table displays and filter dropdowns, similar to existing `schedule_jobs` entries.
userImpact: Users can now submit compliance-related jobs via the PostJobs API and will see `Compliance Policy Check` jobs properly labeled in job tables and activity logs, while scheduler meta-jobs remain hidden from standard UI filters.
author: videetparekh1995

title: feat: fix scroll and add info alert
summary: Fixed scroll behavior and added an informational alert to the governance policies interface. Changes were made to multiple tabs and the management button component to improve user experience when navigating policy details.
userImpact: Users can now scroll more reliably within governance policy views and will see an informational alert to guide them through policy management actions.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4175 Keep previous search results while waiting for response
summary: Fixed an issue in the SelectApplicationsStep component where previous search results were cleared while waiting for a new search response. The application now retains the existing search results in the UI until the new results are available, providing a smoother user experience.
userImpact: Users can now see previous search results while waiting for a new search to complete, eliminating the blank state during search requests.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4172 Add action column to the needs attention section of application level policy modal
summary: Added an action column to the "needs attention" section of the application level policy modal. This new column displays actions available for policies requiring attention, improving visibility and usability of policy management workflows.
userImpact: Users can now see and interact with actions directly in the needs attention section of the policy modal without additional navigation.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: fix(UP-4174): when options dropdown is clicked for the first time it disappears
summary: Fixed an issue where the options dropdown would disappear immediately after being clicked for the first time. The fix refactors row action handlers across multiple governance views (alert rules, attestation rules, and policy apps) to ensure the dropdown state is properly managed and remains visible until the user explicitly closes it or selects an action.
userImpact: Users can now click the options dropdown menu on governance rule and app rows and it will remain open until they select an action or close it manually.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4164, UP-4170: Mutate policies data after app assignment
summary: Fixed an issue where policies data was not being mutated after app assignment in the governance policies view. The fix ensures that policy assignments are properly reflected in the application state immediately after the assignment action completes.
userImpact: Users will now see policy assignments update correctly in the UI immediately after assigning policies to applications.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4165: Simply navigate on view policy button press
summary: Fixed navigation behavior when pressing the "view policy" button in the assignment success modal. The change simplifies the navigation logic by removing an unnecessary condition, ensuring users are properly directed when confirming a policy assignment.
userImpact: Users can now navigate correctly when clicking the "view policy" button after successfully assigning a policy.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4161 Properly store custom rules created by the user
summary: Fixed a bug where custom rules created by users were not being properly stored in the governance policies workflow. The issue was present in both alert rules and attestation rules steps, causing user-created rules to be lost or not persisted correctly.
userImpact: Users can now successfully save and persist custom alert and attestation rules they create in governance policies without data loss.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4166 Consider existing assignments in edit assignment modal
summary: The edit assignment modal now properly considers existing policy assignments when displaying available options. This fix ensures that users see accurate information about which applications already have the policy assigned, preventing confusion and duplicate assignment attempts when editing an assignment.
userImpact: Users can now edit policy assignments with confidence that the modal correctly reflects existing assignments and prevents unintended duplicate assignments.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4103): add edit modal
summary: Added a modal interface for editing governance policies, including the ability to manage alert rules and attestation rules within the policy editor. The modal integrates with existing rule management hooks and provides a streamlined interface for policy modifications.
userImpact: Users can now edit governance policies and their associated alert and attestation rules through a dedicated modal dialog.
author: lukasz94

title: Policies bugfixes
summary: This MR fixes two critical issues in the policies governance interface: wires up missing onClick handlers for buttons and menu items that were non-functional, and improves the compliance checks snackbar behavior to retain successful check information instead of disappearing instantly. These fixes enhance the usability of policy management and compliance monitoring workflows.
userImpact: Users can now interact with previously non-functional policy buttons and menu items, and compliance check results persist in the snackbar notification for better visibility of check outcomes.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Application policies flow
summary: Added a policy details modal to the application details page, allowing users to view comprehensive policy information. Fixed the policies section to render consistently across all application kinds and improved the section's loading state behavior.
userImpact: Users can now click on policies to view detailed information in a modal, with consistent policy section visibility and improved loading experience across all application types.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4143: Policy overview tab
summary: Added a new Policy overview tab to the policy detail view in the Governance module. This tab provides users with a dedicated interface to view policy information at a glance, enhancing the policy management experience.
userImpact: Users can now access a dedicated overview tab when viewing policy details, providing a quick summary of policy information.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat: policies in apps
summary: Adds a dedicated policies section to the application view, enabling users to see and manage policies (alert rules and attestation rules) directly from the application detail page. This includes new modals for adding rules and refactored shared rule templates to reduce code duplication across policy creation workflows.
userImpact: Users can now view and add alert and attestation rules to policies directly from the application governance view, without navigating to a separate policies page.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-4152 Compliance check page
summary: Added a new Compliance Checks tab to the governance policies page, enabling users to view current violations and attest to policy compliance. The tab includes a violations section with detailed columns and an attestation dialog for marking checks as compliant. Also fixed a type error in the apps governed tab and corrected policy flow templates.
userImpact: Users can now view and attest to compliance checks directly within policy pages, with visibility into current violations and attestation status.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4142): add check widget
summary: Adds a new check widget for governance policies with compliance status visualization and job tracking capabilities. The feature includes new components for displaying check status, compliance badges, and management buttons, along with hooks for polling policy state and checking compliance status. This enables users to monitor the compliance checks associated with their policies in real time.
userImpact: Users can now view compliance check status and job progress directly within the policy governance interface through an intuitive check widget.
author: lukasz94
