title: UP-4012, UP-4147: Add attestation rules and alert rules views
summary: Added new governance views for attestation rules and alert rules to the application. These views include dedicated pages with column configurations for displaying and managing attestation and alert rules within workspaces, along with internationalization support for governance labels.
userImpact: Users can now view and manage attestation rules and alert rules through dedicated governance pages in their workspace settings.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Pin all unify and shared components deps
summary: This change pins all dependency versions for the unify app and shared-components packages in package.json files. By locking dependency versions in yarn.lock, this ensures consistent builds and prevents unexpected version updates across development and production environments.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Feat: Policies
summary: Introduces a complete Governance Policies feature enabling users to create, view, and manage policies with multi-step creation flows for policy details, alerts, attestations, and confirmation. Users can assign policies to applications, view policy details with tabs for rules and governed apps, and delete policies or individual rules through an integrated UI with real backend endpoints.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage governance policies, assign them to applications, and view comprehensive policy details including rules and assigned apps.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-4088 alert rules navigation 500 error
summary: Fixed an intermittent 500 error that occurred when navigating back from the Alert Rules Management page on the second visit to an application. The root cause was stale project IDs in the Zustand store when workspace resolution failed—the `modelLoader` and `alertRuleLoader` were clearing the workspace ID but not the project ID on errors, causing malformed URLs when navigating back. Both loaders now clear both IDs consistently, eliminating the store inconsistency.
userImpact: Users can now reliably click "Back to Alerts" on the Alert Rules Management page without encountering a 500 error, even on subsequent visits.
author: martin452

title: UP-4097, UP-4098, UP-4100: Implement policy assignment to app flow
summary: Implement a complete policy assignment flow that allows users to assign governance policies to applications. This includes a multi-step modal with application selection, assignment review, and success confirmation, along with a connected call-to-action in the policy card interface.
userImpact: Users can now assign governance policies to applications through an intuitive multi-step workflow accessible directly from the policy management interface.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: feat(UP-4101): policy list and details
summary: Added a complete policy list and details page with support for viewing policies, their associated applications, and both alert and attestation rules. The implementation includes a policy card component, detailed tabs for rules and governed applications, and custom hooks for policy-related mutations and data fetching.
userImpact: Users can now browse a list of policies, view detailed policy information including assigned applications and configured rules, and manage policy assignments and rules from a dedicated governance interface.
author: lukasz94

title: Policy creation flow feedback
summary: Implemented user feedback improvements to the policy creation flow following a demo session. Updated UI components for alert and attestation rule cards, enhanced validation messaging, and refined the policy details and confirmation steps to improve clarity and usability throughout the creation workflow.
userImpact: Users now experience improved feedback and clearer guidance when creating policies, with better validation messages and refined UI components in the alert rules, attestation rules, and policy details steps.

title: Enable analytic dashboard tabs
summary: Enables tab functionality in the analytics dashboard component, allowing users to navigate between different analytics views. Changes include modifications to AnalyticsDashboard and related dashboard components to activate the previously disabled tab interface.

title: Actually generate code and create script for bumping version

title: bump registry version

userImpact: No direct user impact

title: UP-3787: Enable save button on sql query edits
summary: The save button on SQL query edits was incorrectly disabled, preventing users from saving modifications to custom aggregations. A fix to the custom aggregation form validation logic now correctly enables the save button when edits are made to SQL queries.

title: UP-3795: Allow the user to delete custom metrics in the page
summary: Users can now delete custom metrics directly from the page interface. This enhancement improves the custom metrics management experience by allowing users to remove metrics they no longer need without leaving the page.

title: UP-3793 - Enable sorting in custom metrics table
summary: Enabled sorting functionality in the custom metrics table by adding sort support to the CustomAggregationsTable component. This allows users to organize custom metrics by different columns, improving data discoverability and analysis in the metrics interface.

title: feat: fix analytics sidebar
summary: Refactored the analytics sidebar into a dedicated component and layout structure. The sidebar logic has been extracted from the main layout into a new analytics-specific sidebar component, improving code organization and maintainability for the analytics views.

title: Up 3822 user id not visible

title: Update GenAI UI Components
summary: Updated shared UI components with the latest version from genai-engine and bumped the frontend package version. This includes improvements to trace viewer components, filtering logic, and various UI component refinements across the shared-components library.

title: UP-3802: Add date range selector to project dashboard
summary: Added a date range selector component to the project dashboard, allowing users to filter analytics data by custom date ranges. The implementation includes UI updates to the ProjectDashboard view and corresponding internationalization strings for the new functionality.

title: feat(governance): enhance job logs and scanning functionality
summary: Enhanced governance job logs and agent scanning functionality with new UI components for displaying scan results and logs. Added ScanLogsModal and ScanResultPanel components, along with a custom hook (useScanJobLogs) to handle job log polling and data parsing for discovered agents. These changes improve visibility into governance scanning operations with dedicated modal and result display components.

title: NO_REF-use existing yaml and change to env var

title: UP-3692 support datasets without time columns

The update includes a workspace selector, application cards with alert counts, filtering capabilities, and a paginated load-more experience. Layout components have been refactored for better organization and maintainability.

title: Redirect after register to new model + kick off metrics job
summary: Updated the agent registration flow to redirect users to the newly registered agent model and automatically kick off the metrics job upon successful registration. This improves the user experience by providing immediate feedback and initiating monitoring for the new agent.

title: feat(governance): unregistered agents table UX improvements
summary: Enhanced the unregistered agents table UX by removing the warning icon from the name column, styling the infrastructure pill to match ApplicationCard, and making the Source column clickable. The Source column now opens the GCP console in a new tab for GCP sources, or navigates to engine traces when an engine URL is resolved. Added sourceUrls utility and infrastructure color resolution via governance engine connector.

title: [no-ref] fix Upsolve dashboard crashing
summary: Updated the Upsolve SDK dependency to fix a crash in the dashboard caused by excessive setState calls. This resolves a stability issue that was preventing the Upsolve dashboard from functioning properly.

title: Grouped alerts
summary: Integrated the new alert grouping endpoint into the governance alerts view, replacing the previous implementation with improved grouping logic. This update includes fixes for priority assignment, pagination, and last page calculation to ensure proper display of grouped alerts.
userImpact: Users can now view alerts organized by groups using the updated grouping endpoint, with corrected pagination and priority handling.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3694 enable databricks connector
summary: The Databricks connector is now enabled in the UI, making it available to users. Changes include updating the connector types configuration, adding the Databricks icon rendering, and removing the placeholder connector type entry.

title: Feature/register agent
summary: Added agent metadata to the GCP agent registration flow in the Unify frontend. The registerGCPAgent function now sends structured agent metadata containing GCP provider details (project ID, region, resource ID) when registering agents discovered from GCP Vertex AI, while span-based and task-based registration flows remain unchanged.
userImpact: Users can now register GCP agents with complete provider metadata, enabling better tracking and management of agent resources across GCP Vertex AI integrations.
author: videetparekh1995

title: [no-ref] fix upsolve regression
summary: Fixed a regression in the upsolve integration caused by a recharts library downgrade. The recharts 3.x version was causing stack overflow issues, prompting a downgrade to version 2.x to restore stability.
userImpact: Upsolve charts and visualizations now render correctly without stack overflow errors.
author: ianmcgraw

title: Add scan button
summary: Added a scan button to the Unregistered Agents tab in the Governance module. This includes new hooks for triggering agent discovery and registering agents, along with internationalization support for the new UI elements.
userImpact: Users can now initiate agent scanning directly from the Governance interface to discover and register unregistered agents.

title: [no-ref] enable dashboard filters for Sabic
summary: Enables dashboard-level filters for users to customize their dashboard views based on model segments. This change was specifically requested to support Sabic's dashboard filtering requirements.
userImpact: Users can now create and apply dashboard-level filters to segment their dashboard data and views.

title: NO_REF- Fix package publish script
summary: The package publication pipeline has been failing for six months due to invalid .npmrc authentication configuration. This fix updates the publish script in .gitlab-ci.yml to follow GitLab's official npm registry authentication instructions, resolving the "YN0041: Invalid authentication" error that was preventing package releases.
userImpact: Users can now successfully publish packages to the npm registry as part of the CI/CD pipeline.

title: Update dependency globals to v17
summary: Updated the globals dependency from v16 to v17, which includes support for new JavaScript environments (webpack, rspack, bunBuiltin), additional Jasmine globals, and updated browser globals from Chrome and Firefox sources. This release separates the audioWorklet environment from browser globals for better environment-specific configuration.
userImpact: Users get access to updated global definitions for newer JavaScript environments and bundlers, enabling better linting and development environment configuration.

title: UP-3602 Trace Viewer and Evals Config
summary: Reorganized trace viewer and evals components by separating logic wrappers from presentation components, then migrated all presentation components to the shared-components package. This modular approach improves component reusability across the frontend while maintaining separation of concerns.
userImpact: Developers can now reuse trace viewer and evaluator UI components from shared-components in other parts of the application.

title: chore(deps): update dependency react-i18next to v16
summary: Updated react-i18next from v15.6.1 to v16.5.3, bringing bug fixes and new features. This includes improvements to Trans component handling (named tags with underscores, double-escaping prevention), type safety fixes, and new configuration options like transDefaultProps for setting default props on the Trans component.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved Trans component reliability, better TypeScript type support, and additional configuration options for customizing Trans component behavior.

title: feat: upgrade i18next

title: Move unregistered agents to a tab

summary: Enhanced application cards in the governance view to display real data for alerts and inferences, including counts and status information. The cards now show mocked data for inference counts and improved alert displays with better visual organization. Backend API endpoint for loading inference counts in the last 7 days is still needed to complete this feature.

Components moved include ConfirmationModal, ErrorFallback, SearchBar, Drawer, DatePickerField, MaterialAutocompleteField, and MessageRenderer, with several requiring minor refactoring to reduce dependencies and improve reusability.

title: UP-3542 utility function migrate
summary: Migrated utility functions from the genai engine to the shared-components package, including LLM-related utilities (role accent color, tool definitions, JSON formatting) and general helpers (file download, formatters, wait). These utilities did not have hard dependencies on NestedSpanWithMetricsResponse, making them suitable for shared use across packages.
userImpact: No direct user impact. This refactor enables better code reuse and consolidates utility functions for internal use across packages.

title: Set infrastructure of data planes in onboarding
summary: Updated the data plane onboarding flow to set infrastructure based on the selected installation method, with visual indicators for selected options and prerequisite information displayed upfront. Added support for GCP and Azure installation methods (with limited self-service support) while removing the previously disabled AWS CLI option, and ensured data plane icons now correctly reflect the configured infrastructure.
userImpact: Users can now select from AWS, Docker, GCP, or Azure installation methods during data plane onboarding, with each choice automatically configuring the infrastructure and displaying the appropriate icon, alongside prerequisite requirements and support information for limited self-service options.

summary: Fixed dataset choice logic and UI in the metrics configuration form. The change corrects the behavior and display of dataset selection, ensuring the form properly handles dataset choices when configuring metrics specifications.

author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3497: Adjust custom aggregation cache revalidation
summary: Adjusted the cache revalidation logic for custom aggregations in the MetricsConfiguration hook. This fix ensures that cached metrics are properly refreshed when custom aggregation configurations change, preventing stale data from being displayed.
userImpact: Users will now see up-to-date metrics when custom aggregation configurations are modified, eliminating issues with stale cached data.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP3500: Render custom aggregation UI based on permissions
summary: Added permission-based rendering for the custom aggregations UI, displaying different UI states based on user permissions. A new PermissionDenied component and CustomAggregationPermissions service were introduced to check user access and conditionally render the custom aggregations interface across the workspace.
userImpact: Users without permission to manage custom aggregations will now see a permission denied state instead of the full UI, providing better clarity on access restrictions.

title: [no-ref] upgrade upsolve
summary: Upgraded the upsolve dependency to fix issues with variable filters. This update resolves filtering behavior that was not working correctly in the previous version.

title: UP-3520 Hooks Migration
summary: Migrated useSnackbar and useCopy hooks from the genai-engine frontend into shared packages to enable code reuse across multiple applications. Integrated useCopy into the unify codebase, with useSnackbar integration deferred to a separate ticket due to additional complexity.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved consistency in copy-to-clipboard interactions and snackbar notifications across the Unify application as custom hooks are standardized.
author: martin452

title: Custom metric fixes part 2
summary: This merge request addresses multiple custom metric issues including adding a save button to custom aggregation description input, preventing navigation blocks on form submission, and refactoring SQL parsing logic to support a wider variety of expressions and nesting contexts. An external documentation link has also been added to improve user guidance.
userImpact: Users can now save custom aggregation descriptions, submit forms without navigation interruption, and access external documentation for custom metric configuration.

title: Get the connector for the specified engine

title: bump upsolve SDK
summary: Updated the upsolve SDK dependency to resolve a chart horizontal alignment issue. The bump modifies package.json and updates yarn.lock with the new SDK version.

summary: Fixed the trace viewer to work with the new agentic dataset format from the backend and engine. The trace list, types, and utility functions were updated to properly handle and display the new data structure, as demonstrated in the updated UI.
userImpact: Users can now view traces correctly with the new agent data format, with proper sorting and progress display in the trace viewer.

title: Fix tools/subagent registration for task-based unreg agents
summary: Fixed tools/subagent registration for task-based unregistered agents. The registration flow in the UnregisteredAgentsList component and the useRegisterAgent hook now correctly handles agents that need to register their tools and subagents, addressing a previously broken registration path for this agent type.
userImpact: Users can now successfully register task-based unregistered agents with their associated tools and subagents.

title: Register and show agentic apps w/ spans/tools/sub-agents
summary: Added ability to register and display agentic applications with their associated spans, tools, and sub-agents in the governance interface. Users can now expand the Unregistered Agents table to view application details, register agents from span names, and see tools and agent hierarchies directly in the application details view.
userImpact: Users can now register unregistered agents from the governance UI and view detailed information about agentic applications including their spans, tools, and sub-agents without needing external tools or manual registration.

userImpact: Users can now view and edit evaluations directly from the applications governance view through a dedicated Evals tab with modal editing capabilities.

title: Register an unregistered application workflow
summary: Implements a new workflow for registering unregistered applications in the governance section. The feature introduces new modal and dialog components to guide users through the application registration process, along with a custom hook to manage the registration logic. This enables users to easily onboard applications that were previously in an unregistered state.
userImpact: Users can now register unregistered applications through a guided workflow with a registration modal and progress tracking.

title: Implement v0 of governance + application views
summary: Implements v0 of governance and application views, introducing a new detailed application detail page within the governance section. The changes include new UI components for displaying application information, active agents, tools, and multiple monitoring tabs (Alerts, Evaluations, Guardrails, Metrics), alongside an updated governance landing page with cards for applications, high-priority alerts, and unregistered agents.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to individual applications within the governance section to view detailed information about application status, active agents, tools, and monitoring metrics.

title: Custom metrics fixes
summary: This MR addresses multiple issues in custom metrics creation and editing workflows. Changes include adding a version selector component for metrics, improving form validation and UI responsiveness (scrollable SQL input, proper sketch type display), preventing data loss with unsaved changes warnings, fixing the save button state logic, and ensuring tests run against the most recent configuration values.
userImpact: Users can now safely create and edit custom metrics with better validation feedback, clearer visual states, and protection against accidental data loss when navigating away.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3395 Renamed 'packages/ui' to 'packages/shared-components'
summary: Renamed the 'packages/ui' directory to 'packages/shared-components' to better reflect its purpose as a shared component library. This refactor updates all import paths across the application to reference the renamed package and its configuration.

title: UP-3487, UP-3491: Redesign test UI, retain test state
summary: Redesigned the test UI for custom aggregations with improved layout and component organization. The changes refactor the test interface into dedicated modal and panel components while preserving test execution state across user interactions, making the testing workflow more intuitive and stateful.
userImpact: Users can now test custom aggregations with an improved, more organized interface that retains their test state and results during the workflow.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP3367: Manual sync with sql
summary: Introduced manual sync capability for custom aggregations with SQL. Users can now preview synchronization changes before applying them through a new sync preview modal and calculation utility that determines what data changes will occur during the sync operation.
userImpact: Users can now manually trigger synchronization of custom aggregations and preview the exact changes that will be applied before confirming the sync.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3489: Improve test return data distinction logic for custom aggregations
summary: Improved the test return data distinction logic in custom aggregations utility functions. Changes refine how test data is classified and validated, with additional test coverage to ensure the logic works correctly across different scenarios.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3367, UP-3368, UP-3496, UP-3499: Custom aggregates refactor, bugfixes
summary: Refactored custom aggregates functionality with improved code organization and bug fixes across multiple related issues (UP-3367, UP-3368, UP-3496, UP-3499). The refactor extracts shared form logic and utilities into reusable components, while improving SQL parser robustness with additional test coverage.
userImpact: Custom aggregates creation and editing now work more reliably with improved form handling and SQL parsing.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-2995: Custom Aggregation integration with updated BE
summary: Integrates custom aggregation feature with the updated backend API and introduces a test history drawer for viewing past test results. The implementation adds form validation using React Hook Form and Zod, includes a results table component, and comprehensive test utilities.
userImpact: Users can now view the history of custom aggregation tests in a dedicated drawer and benefit from improved form validation when configuring custom aggregations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-3445] fix: engine cards infinite scroll
summary: Fixed infinite scroll behavior in engine cards view by adjusting the scroll container implementation and removing redundant padding calculations. The fix simplifies the EnginesCardsView component logic while maintaining proper spacing and scroll functionality.
userImpact: Engine cards now load and scroll smoothly without infinite scroll issues, and users can search for engines using the newly added search field.
author: lukasz94

title: Add Governance feature with permission-based access control
summary: Adds a new Governance feature that provides permission-based access control for workspaces. The implementation includes dedicated UI components, routing, internationalization support, and integration with the existing permission system via workspace permissions and role-based access checks.
userImpact: Users can now access and manage governance settings within workspaces, with access controlled by their assigned permissions.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-3422: Render existing model problem type options
summary: Fixed rendering of existing model problem type options in custom aggregations. The edit and new custom aggregation views were not properly displaying available problem type options when working with existing models. Updated UI logic and localization strings to ensure options render correctly.
userImpact: Users can now see all available problem type options when editing or creating custom aggregations with existing models.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3365: Add an example sql query to custom metrics
summary: Added an example SQL query to the custom metrics creation form to help users understand the expected format. Also fixed a bug where initial inferred values were always empty, preventing proper form initialization.
userImpact: Users can now see a helpful SQL example when creating custom metrics, and the form correctly pre-fills inferred values on initial load.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3367: Do not overwrite form values in custom metrics
summary: Fixed form value handling in custom metrics to prevent overwriting existing field values during edits and creation. Changes improve field preservation in both EditCustomAggregation and NewCustomAggregation components, and enhance SQL parsing utilities.
userImpact: Users can now edit custom metrics without their previously entered form values being overwritten unexpectedly.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: UP-3391 & UP-3393: CSP supports popular LLM gateways. Remove wildcard
summary: Content Security Policy (CSP) now explicitly allows popular LLM gateway URLs instead of relying on wildcards. The report-only CSP mode was removed and configuration was moved to vercel.json for deployment-time management. This improves security posture by restricting third-party resource access to only known LLM services.
userImpact: Users can now integrate with popular LLM gateways (such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and other major providers) without CSP policy violations, while the application maintains stricter security controls by eliminating wildcard permissions.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: [UP-3132] fix: update range when date changes
summary: Fixed an issue where the date range was not being updated when the selected date changed in ModelDashboard and TracesList components. This ensures that users viewing filtered data see the correct time range reflected after changing dates.
userImpact: Users can now see accurate date ranges that properly reflect their date selection changes in the dashboard and traces views.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-3133: Adjust several custom metric creation issues
summary: Fixed multiple issues with custom metric creation including removing redundant model problem type configuration, relaxed friendly-name validation to support more characters, deduplicated SQL template placeholders regardless of case/spacing, enforced required parameters for custom aggregations, and ensured custom aggregations are hydrated with their latest version before metric configuration.
userImpact: Users can now create custom metrics more intuitively with better validation, clearer required/optional field indicators, and support for more flexible naming conventions.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: NOREF - New Engines Card UI
summary: The Engines management screen UI has been redesigned to display engines as cards instead of a table view, providing a more modern and visually appealing interface. The underlying functionality remains identical, with a planned follow-up to integrate cloud provider information once the API is available.
userImpact: Users will see engines displayed in a card-based layout on the Engines management screen, improving visual organization and readability.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-3177: Custom metrics bugfixes + perf improvements
summary: Fixed a bug in the inference of reported metrics and significantly improved form performance by reducing unnecessary rerenders during user interactions. These optimizations enhance the stability and responsiveness of custom aggregation forms.
userImpact: Users will experience more responsive form interactions with correct reported metrics inference when creating and editing custom aggregations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-3233] Add CSP wildcard
summary: Updated Content Security Policy configuration to include wildcard sources and Report-Only mode for better debugging. This allows the application to identify CSP violations in a non-blocking manner before enforcing stricter policies.
userImpact: Users can now benefit from improved security posture as the team debugs and refines CSP rules without breaking existing functionality.
author: lukasz94

title: Fix/Show all metrics for custom type datasets
summary: Fixed the metrics configuration to display all available metrics for custom type datasets. Previously, the metric addition logic was filtering out some metrics, preventing users from seeing the complete set of available metrics in the UI.
userImpact: Users can now see and select all available metrics when configuring custom type datasets, including previously hidden metrics like Mean Squared Error and Mean Absolute Error.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [no-ref] upgrade upsolve FE 6.14.1
summary: Upgrades Upsolve frontend dependency from version 6.13.x to 6.14.1, which includes new performance enhancements designed to speed up query execution. This dependency update improves overall application performance without breaking changes.
userImpact: Users will experience faster query performance thanks to improvements in the Upsolve frontend library.
author: ianmcgraw

title: fix: empty segmentation columns
summary: Fixed an issue where empty string (`""`) values were not being handled correctly in the segmentation columns field. The fix includes validation logic in the spec form to properly detect and handle empty segmentation column entries across metrics configuration pages.
userImpact: Users can now properly configure segmentation columns without encountering issues from empty string values.
author: lukasz94

title: chore(deps): update node.js to v24
summary: Updated Node.js runtime from v22.18.0 to v24.13.0 (LTS) across the application engine, Docker images, and development dependencies. This release includes critical security patches for TLS socket handling, file permission APIs, and symlink operations.
userImpact: No direct user impact; infrastructure upgrade to a newer LTS Node.js version with security improvements.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: feat: add input-output fallback and color
summary: Added input-output fallback logic and color support to the Traces span rendering components. This improves the display of LLM message data in the span drawer by providing fallback handling and enhanced visual distinction through color.
userImpact: Users can now see LLM input-output traces with better visual presentation through color coding and improved fallback handling when data is unavailable.
author: lukasz94

title: [NO-REF] feat: add new llm input/output views
summary: Added new LLM input/output views to improve trace visualization and message rendering. Two new components (MessageRenderer and SpanLLMMessagesRenderer) were introduced alongside supporting utilities and schemas to provide dedicated views for LLM message data in trace spans.
userImpact: Users can now view LLM input and output messages with dedicated, specialized renderers in the trace viewer.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-3245] fix: breaking changes after API updates
summary: Fixed breaking changes in the traces utility layer caused by API updates. Added Zod validation to ensure type safety when handling API responses, and updated openInferenceUtils.ts to align with the new API contract.
userImpact: Users can now correctly view and interact with trace data without errors caused by mismatched API response schemas.
author: lukasz94

title: Update dependency eslint-plugin-react-hooks to v7
summary: Updated eslint-plugin-react-hooks from v5.2.0 to v7.0.1, which introduces stricter linting rules for React hooks including disallowing inline useEffectEvent values as JSX props and fixes for Node16 ESM project compatibility. The update includes breaking changes that removed legacy configuration presets and adjusted the recommended configurations.
userImpact: Users will benefit from improved linting rules that catch more React hook misuses and better type support for ESM projects, though projects using legacy eslint configs may need to update their configuration.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: chore(deps): update dependency dotenv-cli to v11
summary: Updated dotenv-cli dependency from v10 to v11, which bumps the underlying dotenv-expand dependency to v12. This is a routine dependency update handled by Renovate.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-3229: fix raw data retriever role not shown in add role assignment dropdowns
summary: Fixed role filtering logic in add role assignment dropdowns to ensure all relevant roles, including the Raw Data Reader role, are properly fetched and displayed. The fix updates role filtering across group and user role binding components and adjusts test fixtures to account for the previously missing role.
userImpact: Users can now see and assign the Raw Data Reader role in add role assignment dropdowns where it was previously missing.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: chore: change env variable
summary: Updated an environment variable in the Vite configuration file. This is a routine maintenance change to the build configuration with no user-facing impact.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: lukasz94

title: feat: update renovate config
summary: Updated the Renovate configuration file with a minor adjustment to better manage automated dependency updates. This is a configuration-only change to the Renovate bot settings.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: lukasz94

title: Run the container as non-root user
summary: The FE container now runs as a non-root user, a security requirement mandated by enterprise customer environments such as JPMC's. Changes include updates to the Dockerfile production configuration and removal of the entrypoint script to support this improved security posture.
userImpact: The container now meets enterprise security compliance requirements by running without root privileges, enabling deployment in restricted environments like JPMC.
author: nori2

title: [no-ref] upgrade upsolve 6.13.33
summary: Upgraded the upsolve dependency to version 6.13.33 by updating package.json and yarn.lock. This is a routine dependency maintenance update with no functional changes to the application.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: Add trusted URLs to style-src-elem
summary: Updated the Content Security Policy configuration in vite.config.ts to add trusted URLs to the style-src-elem directive. This change allows stylesheets from specified trusted sources to load without triggering CSP violations.
userImpact: Users will no longer experience styling issues or CSP warnings when stylesheets are loaded from the newly trusted URLs.
author: martin452

title: changed CSP configs
summary: Added report-to and report-uri directives to the Vite CSP configuration and removed the CSP environment variable from the nginx configuration to prevent automatic CSP header injection. This gives the application more granular control over Content Security Policy reporting and enforcement.
userImpact: Users will now see CSP reports directed to configured endpoints, and nginx will no longer automatically inject CSP headers, allowing the application to manage CSP policies more explicitly.
author: martin452

title: UP-3225: Don't overwrite CSP header on dev
summary: Fixed an issue where the Content Security Policy (CSP) header was being overwritten during development deployments. The fix introduces an entrypoint script that conditionally applies CSP header configuration based on the deployment environment, ensuring dev environments retain their CSP configuration.
userImpact: Developers can now safely deploy to dev environments without their CSP headers being unintentionally overwritten, preventing CSP-related security and functionality issues in development.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: unsafe inline into scope
summary: Updated nginx.conf to scope the unsafe-inline directive more precisely. This change improves the Content Security Policy configuration to be more targeted and secure.
userImpact: Users may experience changes to inline script/style handling behavior depending on how the scoped unsafe-inline directive is applied in the updated nginx configuration.
author: martin452

title: Explicit CSP in nginx header
summary: Added explicit Content Security Policy (CSP) header to nginx configuration. This security fix ensures that CSP is properly declared in the web server configuration, improving compliance with security best practices.
userImpact: Web traffic is now served with an explicit CSP header, strengthening security posture against content injection attacks.
author: martin452

title: [UP-3217] fix: modify style-src-elem and script-src-elem
summary: Modified the Content Security Policy directives `style-src-elem` and `script-src-elem` in the Vite configuration. This change addresses CSP enforcement for inline styles and scripts, ensuring stricter security controls are properly configured.
userImpact: Users now have stricter Content Security Policy protections for inline style and script elements, improving application security.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-3200] feat: pass aggregation_kind to model config
summary: This change passes aggregation_kind to the model configuration during model creation. The aggregation_kind parameter is now threaded through to the model config confirmation step, enabling more complete model configuration management.
userImpact: Users can now create models with the aggregation_kind parameter properly configured.
author: lukasz94

title: feat: add CSP plugin & headers
summary: Added a CSP (Content Security Policy) plugin and headers to enforce security policies across the application. The implementation includes configuration for trusted sources from GitHub, AWS, Amplitude, and Vercel, with SRI (Subresource Integrity) enabled only on production deployments. This improves the application's security posture by controlling which resources can be loaded.
userImpact: Users benefit from enhanced security through Content Security Policy enforcement, which prevents unauthorized script execution and resource loading from untrusted sources.
author: lukasz94

title: fix: metrics bugs
summary: Fixed multiple issues in the metrics configuration UI including form clearing after metric spec submission, removal of the first dataset field from spec display, and rendering of DisplayMetric fields based on schema with proper default value handling. These fixes address form state management and UI consistency in the metrics configuration workflow.
userImpact: Users can now add metric specs without residual form data, and the metrics configuration UI displays and handles fields correctly according to the schema.
author: lukasz94

title: fix: field validation and regressions
summary: Fixed field validation regressions in the metrics configuration form that occurred after adding a dataset field to metrics. The changes correct optional field validation logic and resolve issues reported with metrics config validation.
userImpact: Users can now successfully configure metrics without encountering validation errors that were introduced by recent changes to the metrics schema.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-3082: Implement security headers.
summary: Implemented security headers in the nginx configuration to improve application security posture. This change adds HTTP security headers that help protect against common web vulnerabilities by controlling browser behavior and enforcing secure policies.
userImpact: Users benefit from enhanced security protections through HTTP security headers that mitigate common web-based attacks and enforce secure client-side behavior.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: UP-3083: Hide nginx version
summary: Hide nginx version information in HTTP response headers to improve security posture. This change prevents potential attackers from easily discovering the nginx version deployed in the application, reducing the surface area for version-specific exploits.
userImpact: Users benefit from improved security as nginx version information is no longer exposed in HTTP headers.
author: kacper.krupinski

title: [UP-3078] Support nested column metric arguments
summary: Added support for nested column metric arguments, enabling more complex metric configurations that can reference columns within nested structures. The changes update the metric specification form, column filtering utilities, and related components to properly handle nested column references throughout the metrics configuration system.
userImpact: Users can now define metric arguments using nested column references, allowing for more sophisticated metric configurations.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-2986] Traces filtering
summary: Implemented a new minimal traces filtering UI with support for span-level match rules and free-solo input types. The redesigned filtering system includes a new component-based architecture with dedicated stores, validation rules via Zod, and operators for filtering trace data by various criteria including custom trace IDs.
userImpact: Users can now filter traces using an improved, minimal UI with support for span-level filtering rules and flexible input types including free-solo autocomplete fields.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2393] Adjust the route on back button in dataset addition screen
summary: Corrected the back button navigation route in the dataset addition screen. The back button was pointing to an incorrect route, which has been fixed to ensure users return to the intended previous page when adding a new dataset.
userImpact: Users clicking the back button on the dataset addition screen will now navigate to the correct previous page.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-3062] Fix search in custom aggregations page
summary: Fixed the searchbar functionality in the custom aggregations page. The issue was in the CustomAggregationsTable component where search was not working correctly. Changes ensure that users can properly search and filter custom aggregations.
userImpact: Users can now successfully search and filter custom aggregations on the custom aggregations page.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-2213] Make entire expandable row clickable in inference deep dive table
summary: Updated the inference deep dive table's expandable rows to be fully clickable across the entire row, rather than requiring users to click specifically on the arrow button. This improves usability by providing a larger interactive target area.
userImpact: Users can now expand or collapse rows in the inference deep dive table by clicking anywhere on the row, not just the arrow button.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: fix(deps): update dependency uuid to v13
summary: Updated uuid dependency from v11.1.0 to v13.0.0, which includes breaking changes: removal of CommonJS support, drop of Node 16 support, update to TypeScript 5.2, and changes to browser export defaults. These changes align the project with the uuid library's new ESM-first approach and modernized runtime requirements.
userImpact: Projects must now use ESM imports for uuid, require Node 18+, and use TypeScript 5.2 or later; CommonJS usage is no longer supported.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: [UP-2393] Standardize connector configuration navigation with other config pages
summary: Standardized connector configuration navigation to match other config pages by replacing breadcrumbs with a back button and updating the cancel button redirect behavior. This ensures a consistent navigation experience across the application's configuration interfaces.
userImpact: Users now experience consistent navigation patterns when configuring connectors, with a back button and updated cancel behavior aligned with other configuration pages in the application.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: [UP-2966] Support for Snowflake connector
summary: Adds full Snowflake connector integration into the frontend application. This includes updates to connector type definitions, icon rendering, test fixtures, and removes placeholder connector references.
userImpact: Users can now connect to and use Snowflake as a data source through the unified connector interface.
author: artur-arthur-ai

title: Resolve "[UP-2848] Screen for reading/updating an existing custom aggregation"
summary: Added a confirmation dialog that warns users when saving changes to custom aggregations, improving data safety. Enhanced form UX by restricting dimension column selection to a single choice and providing predefined tag hint options for better guidance during custom metric creation and editing.
userImpact: Users can now safely edit custom aggregations with a warning prompt before saving changes, and benefit from improved form validation and input constraints that guide correct data entry.
author: michalarthurai

title: [UP-3065] feat: bump ai-sdk
summary: This MR updates the ai-sdk dependency to version 5, incorporating the latest features and improvements from the library. Minor adjustments were made to form validation schemas and utilities to maintain compatibility with the new version.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest ai-sdk v5 improvements and features integrated into the application.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-3059] feat: new metrics configuration UI/UX
summary: Introduced a new metrics configuration UI/UX with a complete redesign of the metrics management interface. The changes include new reusable form components, state management via a dedicated store, support for metric creation and removal, and improved form validation with literal input support. Old configuration components were removed and replaced with a modular component structure for better maintainability.
userImpact: Users can now configure metrics through an improved UI with better organization, validation, and workflow for adding and removing metrics in model setup.
author: lukasz94

title: Resolve "[UP-3068] Button add custom metric is not linked to the form"
summary: Fixed the "Add Custom Metric" button in the custom metrics table empty state to properly link to the form navigation. The button was previously not linked, preventing users from accessing the custom metric creation flow.
userImpact: Users can now click the "Add Custom Metric" button in the empty state and successfully navigate to the custom metric form.
author: michalarthurai

title: chore(deps): update dependency jsdom to v27
summary: Updated jsdom dependency from v26 to v27, which raises the minimum Node.js requirement to v20 and includes numerous improvements: new event constructors, CSS selector engine switch from nwsapi to @asamuzakjp/dom-selector (closing 20+ bugs), updated user agent stylesheet from HTML Standard, and improved CSS handling via cssstyle upgrade. This is a routine dependency update with no direct user-facing API changes to the application.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal dependency update to support newer testing infrastructure.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Resolve "[UP-3025] Custom Aggregations: Improve UX for Save functionality"
summary: Improved UX for custom aggregations Save functionality by refactoring the form to use react-hook-form with enhanced validation and error handling. Migrated zod validation schemas from v3 to v4 syntax, introduced a new AutocompleteWithChips component for better input handling, and fixed a database selection side effect that was interfering with form validation.
userImpact: Users can now save custom aggregations with improved form validation, clearer error messages, and a better experience when selecting and entering comma-separated values.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3026] Custom Metrics : Changing the versions will remove the basic info"
summary: Fixed an issue where changing the version of a custom metric would incorrectly modify the basic information section (name and description). The fix prevents unintended propagation of version changes to the basic info fields and ensures the modelProblemType is properly initialized from the selected version.
userImpact: Users can now safely change the version of a custom metric without losing or accidentally modifying the name and description in the basic information section.
author: michalarthurai

title: UP-815 adjustments for idp managed groups
summary: Updated the Group Management UX to support IDP managed groups. Changes include a new group creation form for IDP managed groups, updates to the groups table to display "IDP Managed" status, a warning message on the group member view page for IDP groups, and exclusion of IDP managed groups from user invite and add-to-group selection flows.
userImpact: Users can now create and manage IDP-managed groups, with clear visual indicators and restrictions preventing manual member management of IDP-controlled groups.
author: ianmcgraw

title: chore(deps): update dependency @redocly/cli to v2
summary: Updated @redocly/cli dependency from v1 to v2, a major version upgrade. The update includes new OpenAPI validation rules, per-domain mTLS certificate configuration, proxy settings support, and various bug fixes in the Redocly CLI tooling. This is a dependency upgrade to enable use of the latest Redocly CLI capabilities in the build/development environment.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a development dependency update that enables access to new Redocly CLI features and improvements in the build pipeline.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Resolve "[UP-3036] Custom Metrics : create drop down for dataset options"
summary: Converted the custom metrics dataset parameter input from a text field to a dropdown selection interface. The dropdown is restricted to template variables, improving the user experience for dataset selection in custom aggregations and enforcing proper parameter naming conventions.
userImpact: Users can now select datasets from a dropdown list when configuring custom aggregations, making it easier to correctly specify dataset parameters without manual text entry.
author: michalarthurai

title: Extend Eslint config with the typescript-eslint consistent-type-imports rule
summary: Extended ESLint configuration to enforce the typescript-eslint consistent-type-imports rule across the codebase. This rule ensures that type imports are written using the `import type` syntax, improving code clarity and enabling better tree-shaking. Lint commands have also been separated for better organization.
userImpact: Type imports throughout the codebase now consistently use the `import type` syntax, improving code maintainability and enabling better tree-shaking optimizations.
author: michalarthurai

title: Fix: Re-enable amplitude session replay tracking
summary: Re-enabled Amplitude session replay tracking on the platform by explicitly configuring sessionReplay in the amplitude initialization. Previously, session replay was being globally disabled on the landing page, which unintentionally prevented replay tracking across the entire stack since both landing and platform report to the same Amplitude project.
userImpact: Session replay tracking is now enabled on platform.arthur.ai while remaining disabled on the landing page (arthur.ai), allowing proper user session recording on the platform without consuming excess quota.
author: pranavarthur

title: fix: rollback ca changes
summary: This MR rolls back recent changes to the certificate authority (CA) fetch functionality and associated UI tabs. The rollback reverts additions to the metrics configuration step type and removes CA-related fetch operations, restoring the previous metrics configuration behavior.
userImpact: Users will see the metrics configuration interface restored to its previous state, with CA fetch operations and related UI tabs removed.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-2995, UP-3029: Add custom aggregation testing modal
summary: Added a custom aggregation testing modal that enables users to validate custom metric tests against the backend. The feature integrates with the schema editor and includes column compatibility utilities to ensure proper validation during custom aggregation creation and editing.
userImpact: Users can now test custom aggregations interactively within the editor before saving, ensuring their custom metrics are properly validated against backend constraints.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: Resolve "[UP-3028] Custom Metrics (Former CA) : Update the table to show versions"
summary: Updated the custom aggregations table to display multiple versions of each metric in a collapsable row interface. The table now shows up to 5 latest versions per metric with an overflow indicator, improving visibility into version history without cluttering the main table view.
userImpact: Users can now expand custom metric rows to view and navigate between different versions of each metric, making it easier to compare and manage metric version history.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3015] Custom Aggregation Enhancements : Input CA Name should be editable upon selecting"
summary: Custom aggregation name and description are now editable inline with an optional edit icon for better discoverability. The TextFieldEditable component was enhanced to support an optional edit button, improving the user experience when modifying custom aggregation metadata.
userImpact: Users can now edit custom aggregation names and descriptions directly in place with a visible edit icon, making the editable fields more intuitive to discover and interact with.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3027] Custom Aggregation : change terminology to Custom Metrics"
summary: Updated user-facing terminology throughout the application from "Custom Aggregation" to "Custom Metrics" to align with product naming conventions. This change affects labels, navigation items, and internationalization files across multiple components and views.
userImpact: Users will see "Custom Metrics" instead of "Custom Aggregation" in the UI, providing clearer product terminology.
author: michalarthurai

title: Remove platform from meticulous tracking
summary: Removed the platform parameter from meticulous tracking configuration in the main application entry point. This is a minor cleanup to the tracking setup that simplifies the monitoring instrumentation without affecting user-facing functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact.
author: zach.fry

title: Resolve "[UP-3021] Workspace Dashboard: Fix the Team Member Count"
summary: Fixed incorrect team member count display on the Workspace Dashboard by implementing a dedicated GET workspace statistics API endpoint. The workspace dashboard now retrieves team member statistics from a centralized API rather than computing them locally, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
userImpact: Users now see the correct team member count on their Workspace Dashboard.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3020] WorkSpace Dashboard : enhancements"
summary: Simplified the WorkSpace Dashboard UI and adjusted wording throughout the interface. The changes reduce visual complexity while maintaining functionality, with significant updates to the dashboard component and localization strings.
userImpact: Users see a cleaner, more streamlined WorkSpace Dashboard with improved clarity through simplified wording and UI refinements.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-3019] Workspace Dashboard: Fix the Count of the Running Engine"
summary: Fixed the running engines count display in the Workspace Dashboard by ensuring all engines are fetched from the API (previously only the first page was retrieved) and correcting the engine health check logic. The engine health calculation now uses a proper `isEngineHealthy()` function that validates engines are active within the last 5 minutes, rather than a simple timestamp check. The new health checking logic was extracted to a reusable utility for consistency across the application.
userImpact: Users will now see the correct count of running engines in their Workspace Dashboard, including engines beyond the first page of results and with accurate active status detection.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-2411] Disable users from the list that are already in the group"
summary: Fixed user truncation in the group member search results by removing pagination limits in searchGroupMemberships, ensuring all eligible users are displayed. Also refactored the API_FETCH_ALL_PAGE_SIZE constant to be centralized and added an openapi:preview script for local development.
userImpact: Users can now see all available users in the member search list instead of having results truncated by pagination, enabling proper filtering of users already in the group.
author: michalarthurai

title: Resolve "[UP-2211] Standardize how deep-link are being displayed throughout the app"
summary: Standardized deep-link behavior throughout the app by updating alert deep links to open in the current tab instead of a new tab. Also updated the HeaderItem component to conditionally display the "new tab" icon only when links are configured to open in a new tab, ensuring visual consistency with actual link behavior.
userImpact: Alert deep links now open in the current tab for a consistent navigation experience, and the "new tab" icon only appears when links actually open in a new tab.
author: michalarthurai

title: [UP-2825] Show job log for schema inspection in DatasetPreview
summary: Added job log display for schema inspection in the DatasetPreview component. Users can now view the job log output directly within the dataset preview during schema inspection, improving visibility into the inspection process and making it easier to diagnose any issues.
userImpact: Users can now see the job log for schema inspection directly in the DatasetPreview interface.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [UP-2996] - implementing new workspace dashboard without custom aggregations
summary: Implemented a new workspace dashboard that replaces the previous custom aggregations-based design with a simpler, more direct approach. The update includes new components for displaying workspace summary cards and an empty state for workspaces, along with refreshed translations and minor bug fixes for metrics and UI consistency.
userImpact: Users now see a redesigned workspace dashboard with improved workspace summary information and a clearer empty state experience when no data is available.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2847, UP-2848, UP-2849, UP-2850] workspace dashboard and custom aggregations
summary: Added custom aggregations functionality to the workspace dashboard, including new views for creating and managing custom aggregations. This update includes new modal and table components, metrics configuration enhancements, and additional routing to support the custom aggregations workflow across workspaces and models.
userImpact: Users can now create, edit, and manage custom aggregations within their workspace dashboard, enabling more flexible metric configuration and analysis.
author: jakubdom

title: Traces updates 2
summary: This MR introduces multiple trace dashboard improvements including data refresh functionality, an agentic dashboard, tool relevance score conversion, and enhancements to the PII entity UX. The refresh modal logic has been refactored into a separate component, and metric display has been improved to handle zero values and conditionally show reranker/BERT-F scores based on availability.
userImpact: Users can now refresh traces to fetch the newest data, view an agentic dashboard, see improved metric chips display, and interact with a redesigned PII entity interface in the traces view.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2823] add better error handling in metrics calculation
summary: Improved error handling in the metrics calculation module by passing errors directly to the snackbar notification system. The system now has better built-in logic for checking and handling axios errors, providing clearer feedback to users when metrics calculations encounter issues.
userImpact: Users now receive more informative error messages when metrics calculations fail, with proper error handling for network and validation issues.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2863] Add optional endpoint URL input
summary: Added optional endpoint URL input field to the ChatPlayground model configuration interface, allowing users to specify custom API endpoints. This enhancement includes fixes to custom endpoint handling, translation updates, and refinements to error messaging and UI behavior to ensure proper validation and user guidance when configuring non-standard endpoints.
userImpact: Users can now optionally provide a custom endpoint URL when configuring AI models in the chat playground, enabling integration with alternative API endpoints.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: [UP-2830] add docs link to dashboard
summary: A documentation link has been added to the model dashboard component, allowing users to quickly access relevant help documentation. The change includes updates to the dashboard UI component and the corresponding internationalized text strings in English.
userImpact: Users can now access documentation directly from the model dashboard via a new docs link.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2772] add 'add dataset' button with permissions check
summary: Added an "Add Dataset" button to the datasets view with built-in permissions checking. The button is now available in both the models and projects datasets interfaces, with proper role-based access control to ensure users can only add datasets when they have the required permissions.
userImpact: Users with appropriate permissions can now easily add new datasets directly from the datasets view using a dedicated button.
author: lukasz94

title: UP-2677 - PII default state
summary: Updated the UX of the PII entity metric configuration component to improve the default state presentation. The changes modify the PIIMetricConfig component and add corresponding i18n translations to enhance user experience when configuring PII metrics.
userImpact: Users now see an improved interface for PII entity configuration with better default state handling.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2795] Bug on redirection for org members
summary: Fixed a redirection bug for organization members without workspace creation permissions, ensuring they are properly redirected to the /workspaces page instead of encountering an error. Additionally, added a logout button for users without any workspace to improve the user experience in edge cases.
userImpact: Organization members without workspace creation permissions are now correctly redirected to the workspaces page, and users without any workspace can now access a logout button.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: UP-2751 -  segmentation protections
summary: Added segmentation protections by limiting segmentation column selection to a maximum of 3 columns and removing the "possible_segmentation" tag as an option in the schema editor tag dropdown. This prevents over-segmentation and reduces accidental misuse of protected segmentation features.
userImpact: Users can now select up to 3 segmentation columns and will no longer see the "possible_segmentation" tag as a selectable option in the schema editor.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-2914] Rendering of PermissionType in identity & access page
summary: Fixed i18n namespace in the organization permissions table to correctly render PermissionType values on the identity & access page. This resolves a display issue where permission types were not being properly localized in the table.
userImpact: Permission type labels now display correctly with proper localization in the identity & access page permissions table.
author: lukasz94

title: [UP-2901] Add Quick Deploy Step to Onboarding & Engine Creation
summary: This PR adds a `Quick Deploy` step to the onboarding flow and engine creation flow, streamlining the setup process for users. A new reusable `EngineSetupCard` component was introduced to support this functionality and enable code reuse across deployment contexts.
userImpact: Users can now quickly deploy engines during onboarding and engine creation with a dedicated deployment step that supports multiple deployment methods.
author: lukasz94

title: Minor improvements to Agentic Trace viewer
summary: The Agentic Trace viewer now displays tool metric labels and relevance metric chips, and converts span timestamps to local time for improved readability. These updates make trace analysis more intuitive and user-friendly.
userImpact: Users can now see tool metric labels and relevance metric indicators in trace spans, and timestamps are displayed in their local timezone instead of UTC.
author: zach.fry

title: [UP-2799] Display only the supported models in chat playground

The update changes the configuration format for optimizeDeps to use the new `optimizeDeps.rollupOptions.transform.jsx` pattern instead of the deprecated `optimizeDeps.rollupOptions.jsx`, preparing for compatibility with rolldown-vite.
userImpact: Vite build tooling is improved with compatibility for newer rolldown-vite standards, though this is a major version bump that may require configuration adjustments if custom JSX rollup options are in use.

userImpact: Users now see accurate "last updated" timestamps that correctly reflect local time regardless of the timezone format in the source data.

summary: Upgraded Vite from v6.3.5 to v7.0.0, bringing support for Node.js 20.19+ and 22.12+ with removal of CommonJS builds. This major version bump includes improved build targeting defaults and various SSR and API removals as part of Vite 7's modernization.
userImpact: No direct user impact; this is a build tool dependency update that requires Node.js 20.19 or later to run the development and build processes.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including lucide-react (new wine bottle icon), Node.js runtime to 22.17.0 LTS, and patch updates to code coverage, TypeScript types, and Prettier. These updates bring bug fixes and minor improvements from upstream packages.

title: UP-2262 - Dataset Button Permissions
summary: Implemented permission validation for the "Add Dataset" button on both the model and project view pages using the checkPermissions query. The permission check is now performed on page load and when relevant IDs change, preventing unnecessary API calls through optimization in the useEffect hook.
userImpact: Users without permission to add datasets to a project will no longer see the "Add Dataset" button on model and project pages, ensuring proper access control.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including dotlottie-react (patch), TypeScript ESLint plugin and parser (minor), and OpenAI SDK (minor). These are routine dependency maintenance updates that improve compatibility and add minor enhancements to development tooling.

title: UP-2593 - Chat playground updates
summary: The Chat Playground component received several UX improvements including auto-focus of the input field after sending messages via enter key, a call-to-action for empty chat states, and enhanced error handling with translations. CORS headers were also added to help resolve Safari compatibility issues.
userImpact: Users can now enjoy a more seamless chat experience with improved focus management, better error messages, and a guided empty state, plus improved Safari browser compatibility.

title: chore(deps): update dependency @stylistic/eslint-plugin to v5

title: [UP-2512] Add engine installation methods
summary: Added engine installation methods for AWS Console, Docker, and Kubernetes to the Engine Install Screen. The implementation includes reusable UI components for displaying installation instructions, copying commands, checking connection status, and managing installation workflows across multiple deployment platforms.

title: [NO-REF] Temporarily remove @arthur/ui translations
summary: Temporarily removed translations from @arthur/ui components and reverted CustomRangeShortcut helpers to use optional translations while awaiting an upstream fix. The @arthur/ui package was bumped to version 3.7.0 to resolve translation-related issues affecting multiple components including DeepDiveFeedback, InferenceDeepDiveTable, and MessageValidation.
userImpact: Removes broken translations from UI components, restoring stable functionality while the translation system is fixed upstream.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: chore(deps): update dependency @changesets/cli to ^2.29.5
summary: Updated @changesets/cli from 2.29.4 to 2.29.5. This patch release fixes an issue where `workspace:^` and `workspace:~` dependency ranges were not being semantically treated as caret and tilde ranges, causing dependent packages to be bumped more often than necessary.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Inference translations fallbacks
summary: Added fallback translations for inference deep dive UI components across the interface. This ensures that when primary translation keys are unavailable, the application gracefully falls back to predefined values, improving reliability in internationalized deployments.
userImpact: Users will see consistent, fallback text in the inference deep dive interface when specific translations are not available, preventing missing or broken labels.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [NO-REF] Inference table fixes & @arthur/ui version bump
summary: Restored the optional `onFilterModelChange` prop to Shield Inference Table after it was inadvertently removed, which was causing failures. Also includes a version bump to @arthur/ui to incorporate these fixes.
userImpact: The Shield Inference Table component now works correctly without requiring the `onFilterModelChange` prop, preventing runtime errors for users who do not provide it.
author: jakub.ziemba

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated non-major dependencies including knip (5.61.1 → 5.61.2) and lucide-react (0.517.0 → 0.518.0). The knip patch release includes bug fixes and plugin improvements, while lucide-react 0.518.0 adds a new drone icon, updates existing icons, and includes icon naming refinements.
userImpact: Users can access updated icon sets with new drone icon and refined icon naming conventions in lucide-react, plus improved knip dependency stability.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-2734: Basic support for new image dtype tag
summary: Added basic support for the new image dtype tag in the UI, enabling users to change string type data to image type. The inference deep dive now displays base64-encoded images with fixed width formatting, and can retrieve inferences containing image data. Support requires arthur-common to be bumped in the control plane and engine.
userImpact: Users can now convert string data to image type and view base64-encoded images in the inference deep dive interface.

title: UP-2653 - Cancel Button in Filters
summary: The cancel button and clicking outside the filter modal now reset filters to their previous state instead of clearing them entirely. Previously, the cancel button would set the filter array to empty without persisting changes via API, leading to unexpected behavior.
userImpact: Users can now cancel filter changes and have filters revert to their state when the modal was opened, providing more intuitive undo behavior.

title: Update language to get rid of Shield

summary: Implements a new version of the selected project page with enhanced navigation bars, app settings modal, and improved project home interface. This update includes new UI components for project management, updated routing, and refined layout structure to improve project navigation and accessibility.
userImpact: Users now have a redesigned project page with improved navigation, new settings modal options, and a dedicated home tab for better project management and visibility.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2730] show 50 rows per page by default in InferenceDeepDiveTable
summary: Updated InferenceDeepDiveTable to display 50 rows per page by default instead of the previous default. This improves the data visibility and user experience when viewing large inference datasets by reducing the need for pagination in most common use cases.
userImpact: Users viewing the InferenceDeepDiveTable will now see 50 rows per page by default, reducing excessive pagination when browsing inference results.

title: UP-2405 - Alert updates
summary: Alert rule validation has been moved to server-side processing via a dedicated endpoint, improving security and consistency. The default alert graph lookback period has been extended from the previous default to 4 weeks, and validation copy and test coverage have been updated to reflect the new requirements.
userImpact: Users can now create and edit alert rules with server-side validation ensuring queries are correct before submission, and alert performance graphs display a 4-week historical lookback by default for better trend visibility.

title: [UP-2571] Display BE error messages for engine deletion
summary: The application now properly displays backend error messages when users attempt to delete engines and datasets. Previously, errors returned from the backend were not being shown to users. This fix adds proper error handling and Axios error detection to surface these messages in the UI.
userImpact: Users now see meaningful error messages from the backend when engine or dataset deletion fails, instead of silent failures or generic errors.

title: UP-2632 - add more context for S3 connector
summary: Updated the S3 connector form to display more contextual information by removing the collapsible advanced configuration section. This change simplifies the connector setup experience and makes advanced options more discoverable without requiring users to expand hidden sections.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies

userImpact: Users can now open dataset detail pages in a new tab when browsing datasets during ML model onboarding.
author: jakubdom

title: UP-2649 - Remove Data Refresh & Set Default
summary: Removed the data refresh schedule configuration step from the ML Model onboarding flow and set data refresh to a default schedule of 1x per hour with a 1-hour look-back window. This simplifies the onboarding experience by removing a non-essential configuration step while maintaining consistent refresh behavior.
userImpact: Users no longer need to configure data refresh schedules during ML Model creation; the system now uses a sensible default (hourly refresh with 1-hour look-back) automatically.
author: jakubdom

title: [UP-2648] + [UP-2651] - Add New Alert button, fix RefreshModal buttons
summary: Added a new "Add New Rule" button to the Alerts toolbar and fixed several UI issues in the ModelRefreshModal, including button styling gaps and selection state management. These changes improve usability by providing direct access to rule creation and ensuring buttons respond correctly to user interactions.
userImpact: Users can now quickly add new alert rules from the toolbar, and the refresh modal buttons display and behave correctly when interacting with date ranges.

title: fix(deps): update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR updates multiple non-major dependencies across the project, including patches for Babel, Node types, ESLint, Material-UI, Turbo, and minor updates for CodeMirror SQL, ESLint JS, Lottie React, react-hook-form, and Zod. These updates bring in bug fixes, performance improvements, and new features from upstream libraries while maintaining backward compatibility.
userImpact: Users benefit from the latest bug fixes and minor feature improvements in core dependencies, including enhanced SQL language support, improved form handling, and better Material-UI components.

title: UP-2650 - Run ML engine config only in ML use case
summary: ML engine configuration is now only executed when the ML use case is selected during engine installation. This prevents unnecessary configuration steps from running in non-ML scenarios like GenAI engine installations. The installation flow has been updated to conditionally apply ML-specific settings based on the selected engine type.
userImpact: Users installing the GenAI engine will no longer see or process ML engine configuration steps, streamlining the setup experience for non-ML use cases.

title: UP-2545 - new dataset modal fixes
summary: Fixed several issues in the new dataset modal flow, including corrections to the connector step, dataset step, and dataset configuration components. These changes address visual and functional inconsistencies identified during testing.
userImpact: Users can now create new datasets with improved modal behavior and corrected UI rendering throughout the configuration workflow.

summary: This MR addresses multiple UI/UX improvements across GenAI flow, engine management, and workspaces views. Changes include fixes for workspaces double scroll, engine associations display on extra-wide screens, and a display issue in engine project management, along with enhancements like tooltips for validation commands and improved toxicity label presentation in GenAI metrics.
userImpact: Users will experience improved UI stability in workspaces and engine management views, clearer validation command guidance via tooltips, and better presentation of toxicity metrics in GenAI model configuration.
