title: [UP-1497] [FE] Activity Logs - creation of the filter tab
summary: Introduces a new Activity Logs filter tab with a customizable toolbar for filtering activity log entries. The implementation includes a new ActivityLogToolbar component with support for date filtering via a custom calendar header, filter parsing logic with unit tests, and integration with existing JobsTable and ScheduledJobsTable components. Additional refinements fix column display issues and add necessary translations.
userImpact: Users can now filter activity logs using a dedicated filter toolbar with date range selection and custom filtering capabilities.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1439,1440,1571] - UX Improvements
summary: Multiple UX improvements to the activity log and job management interface, including manual refresh capability for activity logs, improved error handling and display, and optimized table pagination. Changes preserve scroll position during data refetches and enhance the visibility of job status information.
userImpact: Users can now manually refresh activity logs, see full error messages in job logs, and experience better scroll position preservation when the jobs table updates.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: Updated development dependencies to their latest minor and patch versions, including TypeScript ESLint tooling (8.10.0 → 8.11.0) and Turbo build system (2.2.1 → 2.2.3). These are routine dependency maintenance updates to keep development tools current with bug fixes and minor enhancements.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Replace Lottie JSON with dotLottie
summary: Replaced Lottie JSON animation files with a single optimized dotLottie format file for the Arthur cube loader animation. This reduces animation asset file size and simplifies the animation management by consolidating dark and light mode variants into one efficient dotLottie file.
userImpact: Animation loading is now more efficient with smaller file sizes and improved performance due to the dotLottie format optimization.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Update @testing-library
summary: Updated @testing-library dependency to a newer version. This is a routine dependency maintenance update that brings the testing library package to its latest release, including any performance improvements or bug fixes included in the new version.
userImpact: Tests continue to work as expected with the updated testing library version, ensuring compatibility with latest improvements and bug fixes.
author: sergai

title: UP-1365: Performs multiple permissions check
summary: Implemented bulk permission checks across multiple UI components to ensure front-end and back-end permissions remain in sync. Created centralized permission service modules for Groups, Organizations, Projects, Users, and Workspaces that encapsulate permission logic, replacing scattered individual permission calls throughout the codebase.
userImpact: Users now see/access only workflows and UI elements matching their assigned role and permissions, with consistent permission enforcement across the front-end application.
author: sergai

title: Update dependency zustand to v5
summary: Zustand has been upgraded from v4 to v5, a major version that drops default exports, removes deprecated features, and increases minimum version requirements for React 18 and TypeScript 4.5. While no new features are introduced, the migration requires code changes to use named imports instead of default exports.
userImpact: Applications using Zustand must update their imports to use named exports instead of default exports and review the v5 migration guide to ensure compatibility.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: NO-REF: Fix CodeMirror package extensions
summary: Fixed CodeMirror package extension configuration in the yarn/npm setup. The change removes outdated extension configuration from .yarnrc.yml and updates yarn.lock to reflect the corrected package dependencies.
userImpact: CodeMirror package extensions are now correctly configured, resolving any build or runtime issues related to missing or misconfigured extensions.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: ESLint fixes
summary: ESLint configuration fixes applied across three component files in the UI layer. Changes resolve linting rule violations in alertRules configuration and user group/role assignment components without altering runtime behavior.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is a code quality improvement to resolve linting violations.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Dynamically import Upsolve global css
summary: Changed Upsolve global CSS to be dynamically imported instead of statically loaded at application startup. This reduces initial bundle size and improves load time by deferring CSS imports until they are actually needed by the UpsolveDashboard component.
userImpact: Users will experience faster application startup time as Upsolve CSS is now loaded on-demand when the dashboard is accessed rather than upfront.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Yarn package xtensions
summary: Updated Yarn configuration and package dependencies, including adjustments to Node.js memory settings via max-old-space-size, ESLint plugin updates, and removal of the Vercel style guide dependency. These changes optimize the build environment and align the project's linting infrastructure.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: ESLint: Migrate to 9.x
summary: Updated ESLint configuration from version 8.x to 9.x, including migration from the legacy .eslintrc.js format to the new flat config format (eslint.config.mjs). This change aligns with ESLint's recommended configuration approach and improves tooling consistency across the codebase.
userImpact: Developers benefit from ESLint 9.x improvements and better standards compliance, with updated linting configurations automatically applied during development and CI/CD workflows.
author: sergai

title: UP-1609 turn off tenant editing permissions for now
summary: Disabled tenant editing permissions in the dashboard to resolve bugs caused by the dashboard not using the latest versions. This change was made as a temporary measure to stabilize the system while the underlying issue is addressed.
userImpact: Users can no longer edit tenant settings in the dashboard, but this resolves dashboard display bugs related to version synchronization.
author: ianmcgraw

title: test: stabilize project access denied tests
summary: Stabilized flaky project access denied tests in the Cypress test suite by improving test reliability and reducing intermittent failures. Changes made to the project access control test logic to ensure consistent test behavior across runs.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Renovate bot only assigns a single person
summary: Updated Renovate bot configuration to assign pull requests to a single person instead of multiple assignees. This adjustment simplifies the review assignment workflow for dependency update PRs.
userImpact: Renovate dependency update pull requests will now be assigned to only one reviewer instead of multiple people.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-1613: Update all non-major dependencies (part 1)
summary: Updated all non-major dependencies across the monorepo, including Babel, Vitest, and various package dependencies. This is part 1 of a larger dependency modernization effort, with corresponding updates to build configuration files (vite.config.ts, vitest.config.ts) and resolution of resulting conflicts.
userImpact: Users benefit from updated dependencies that include bug fixes, performance improvements, and security patches from newer minor and patch versions of their development tools and libraries.
author: sergai

title: UP-1603 - Fix model refresh data modal for models with no run metrics
summary: Fixed a crash in the model refresh data modal when handling models with no run metrics or models still running their first job. The modal was incorrectly defaulting the `from` timestamp to the end of the latest metrics version, which caused errors when that version didn't exist.
userImpact: Users can now open the refresh data modal for models without prior metrics or models with only in-progress jobs without encountering a page crash.
author: peter496

title: Upsolve sdk update
summary: Updated Upsolve SDK to the latest version with improved performance optimizations. The Upsolve dashboard component is now lazy-loaded and code-split separately, reducing initial bundle size and improving page load performance. Fixed related issues including header z-index stacking and dashboard remounting behavior.
userImpact: Users will experience faster page load times as the Upsolve dashboard is now dynamically loaded only when needed, reducing the initial JavaScript bundle size.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [no-ref] fix upsolve dashboard ids
summary: Corrected dashboard IDs in development and production environment configurations. The env configs were pointing to incorrect dashboard IDs for the Upsolve integration, preventing proper dashboard initialization in different environments.
userImpact: Dashboard integrations now load with the correct IDs in development and production environments.
author: ianmcgraw

title: [UP-1497] UI add time zone information to time selector and anywhere timestamps are shown
summary: Added timezone information display to the time selector and all timestamp locations throughout the application. Introduced a new custom PickerActionBar component that displays the current timezone, and updated all timestamp formats across the UI to include timezone information for clarity.
userImpact: Users can now see timezone information in the time picker and alongside all timestamps displayed in the application, eliminating ambiguity about which timezone a timestamp represents.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: Always test connection before navigating to second onboarding step
summary: The onboarding flow now validates connector connectivity before allowing users to proceed to the second step. The connector is marked as permanent only after a successful connection test, preventing incomplete configurations from being saved.
userImpact: Users can no longer advance past the connection step without confirming the connector works, ensuring only valid connections proceed to data source configuration.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1125] [FE] Fix e2e tests for permissions
summary: Fixed failing e2e tests in the permissions test suite, particularly addressing issues with the empty table test case. The permission tests were updated to ensure proper validation of permissions across various scenarios.
userImpact: No direct user impact; ensures the permissions e2e tests pass reliably to catch permission-related regressions in CI/CD.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1381] [FE] deleting a schedule doesn't update the Scheduled Jobs table state, old jobs are still shown until page refresh
summary: Fixed an issue where deleting a schedule did not update the Scheduled Jobs table state, causing old jobs to remain visible until the page was manually refreshed. The fix updates the table data immediately upon scheduled job removal by properly mutating the data after deletion.
userImpact: Users can now see scheduled jobs update in real-time when a schedule is deleted, without needing to refresh the page.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1526: Jobs sorting
summary: Implemented sorting functionality for the Jobs table to improve user experience when reviewing job lists. The changes refactor the JobsTableWrapper component and update test fixtures to support the new sorting behavior, with e2e test fixes to ensure stability.
userImpact: Users can now sort jobs by different columns in the Jobs table for better navigation and data organization.
author: sergai

title: UP-1527: Group roles links
summary: Refactored group roles column definitions by consolidating role column logic into the GroupProjectsManagementTableWrapper and GroupWorkspacesManagementTableWrapper components. Removed the unused RolesByEntity columns module that was no longer needed after the consolidation.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal refactoring to improve code organization and remove unused modules.
author: sergai

title: UP-1598 add alert rule webhook notification state management
summary: Adds webhook notification state management to alert rules through multi-select dropdowns with type-ahead functionality. Users can now assign notification webhooks when creating new alert rules, editing existing ones, and view configured webhooks on alert rule detail and summary pages. Webhook names are clickable links that navigate to the webhook detail pages.
userImpact: Users can now select and manage notification webhooks for alert rules, with visual confirmation of assigned webhooks across creation, editing, and viewing workflows.
author: ianmcgraw

title: UP-898: ReBAC context
summary: Implemented ReBAC (Relationship-Based Access Control) context layer for the frontend application. Added new services and hooks including RebacContext, useHasPermissions hook, and a Can component to enable fine-grained permission checks throughout the UI. Updated main layout and workspace components to integrate the new permission system.
userImpact: Users benefit from more granular permission controls and role-based access that can restrict functionality at the component level based on user permissions.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: i18n fix
summary: Fixed internationalization (i18n) issue in the CustomRangeShortcut component. This addresses a translation-related bug that was preventing proper localization support in the UI package.
userImpact: Users will now see correctly localized text in the CustomRangeShortcut component across different languages.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Fix parser for activity logs
summary: Fixed parser logic for activity logs in the parseFilters utility. The change updates how activity log filters are parsed to correct a previously broken behavior. This ensures activity log filtering works as intended in the application.
userImpact: Users can now correctly filter and parse activity logs without encountering parser errors.
author: sergai

title: Add vercel.json to allow it to use SPAs
summary: Added vercel.json configuration file to enable proper Single Page Application (SPA) support on Vercel. This configuration ensures that client-side routing is correctly handled by redirecting all non-file requests to the index.html entry point, following Vercel's recommended setup for Vite-based SPAs.
userImpact: Deployments to Vercel will now correctly serve the SPA with proper client-side routing support, preventing 404 errors on non-root routes.
author: zach.fry

title: UP-1587: UI i18n fix
summary: Fixed internationalization (i18n) issues in the UI components. The changes update translation strings in the components.json file and correct helper functions in ModelRefreshModal and CustomRangeShortcut to properly reference and format i18n keys.
userImpact: Users will now see correctly translated UI text in all supported languages across the affected components.
author: sergai

title: UP-1327: Mutate key
summary: Updated the infinite query mutation key resolution logic to improve how infinite queries are identified and mutated. Changes include updates to the findInfiniteMutateKey utility function, expanded test coverage, and adjustments to multiple schedule-related modal wrappers to properly reference mutated keys.
userImpact: Users will experience more reliable cache invalidation and refetching behavior for infinite-scrolling lists and paginated data in scheduling workflows.
author: sergai

title: UP-1474 - Low lift UX changes
summary: This merge request delivers several low-lift UX improvements across the application. Changes include enabling navigation out of the onboarding flow, adding organization-level binding to the RolesByEntity view, fixing the copy password button text, and fixing a bug where entity names weren't updating in the UI after modification. Additionally, a "Modified By" header was added to improve transparency.
userImpact: Users can now navigate away from onboarding, see accurate entity names and modification details in the UI, and benefit from improved text labeling on the copy password button.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1572: Raw data retrieval e2e tests
summary: Added end-to-end tests for raw data retrieval functionality, including test fixtures for completed and running data retrieval jobs. Test coverage has been expanded with new Cypress fixtures and test cases to validate the data retrieval workflow.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1572: Filter state and pagination fix
summary: Fixed filter state and pagination behavior in the Inference Deep Dive Table component. The fix ensures that filter selections are properly preserved and pagination resets correctly when filters are applied or cleared.
userImpact: Users can now reliably apply filters and paginate through inference results without losing filter state or experiencing pagination conflicts.
author: sergai

title: UP-1572: raw data retrieval cleaning
summary: Removed unused raw data retrieval hook and cleaned up related code in the inference deep dive table component. This change eliminates technical debt from the raw data retrieval feature and simplifies the codebase.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Internal cleanup of unused code to improve maintainability.
author: sergai

title: Fix lint
summary: Fixed a linting error in the main entry point file (app/src/main.tsx). This is a minor cleanup change that ensures the codebase meets linting standards.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: zach.fry

title: [UP-1137] - replace graphana dashboard with upsolve
summary: Replaced Grafana dashboard integration with Upsolve dashboard component. The implementation now fetches authentication tokens from the backend endpoint instead of directly from Upsolve, and passes the necessary API host override and dashboard ID configuration to the Upsolve component. This provides a more secure and maintainable dashboard solution with proper token management.
userImpact: Users now see model insights and metrics displayed through the Upsolve dashboard instead of Grafana, with improved security through backend-managed token authentication.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1327: Mutate for infinite keys
summary: Added a new utility function `findInfiniteMutateKey` that enables mutation of infinite query caches by matching partial arguments. This allows developers to revalidate infinite-scrolling data without passing all original parameters, reducing boilerplate when triggering updates from other pages or components.
userImpact: Developers can now efficiently mutate infinite API queries by matching on a subset of parameters, simplifying cache invalidation for paginated data across the application.
author: sergai

title: Filter duplicate data entries and sort by time in AlertPerformanceGraph
summary: AlertPerformanceGraph now filters duplicate data entries and sorts results by time. This ensures the performance graph displays clean, chronologically ordered data without redundant entries.
userImpact: Users will see AlertPerformanceGraph charts with deduplicated and properly time-ordered data points, eliminating confusing duplicate entries in the visualization.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-993] - logs whitespaces
summary: Whitespaces are now visible in the JobLogs component output. This improves log readability by making formatting and indentation explicit, helping users identify spacing-related issues in logs more easily.
userImpact: Users can now see whitespaces rendered in job logs, making it easier to debug formatting and indentation issues.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1206: Model dashboard accepts time filters from URL query string
summary: The model dashboard now accepts time filters from URL query parameters, allowing users to share dashboard views with pre-configured date/time ranges. This implementation initializes React state from URL query parameters on component load, enabling deeplinks to specific time periods without requiring manual selection.
userImpact: Users can now share links to the model dashboard with pre-set time filters by passing date/time parameters in the URL query string.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1353, UP-760] - onboarding listing datasets changes
summary: Improved the onboarding dataset listing flow by implementing SWR polling for job state subscriptions instead of promise-based wrapping, and added automatic job initiation on first availableDatasets fetch with improved error handling. The changes also include a new `useListDatasetsV2` hook that manages the dataset listing workflow more reliably across the onboarding process.
userImpact: Users will experience a more reliable dataset listing experience during onboarding, with automatic job initialization when datasets are first fetched and better error handling throughout the workflow.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1214] [FE] As an Arthur UI user, I want to see raw data in the inference deep dive tab
summary: Added raw data viewing capability to the inference deep dive tab, allowing users to inspect detailed inference records with filtering and column visibility options. Refactored shared components (NoRowsOverlay and NoRows icon) into the shared package to support this new feature and improve code reusability across the application.
userImpact: Users can now view and filter raw inference data in the deep dive tab with customizable column visibility, enabling deeper inspection of model inference results.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1433: Deleting a project, UP-1434: Deleting a model
summary: Implemented deletion functionality for both projects and models. Added UI components including a new IconSelect component for user confirmation, along with internationalization support for deletion messages in English. Includes comprehensive test coverage for both deletion workflows.
userImpact: Users can now delete projects and models through the UI with a confirmation dialog before permanent removal.
author: sergai

title: Add meticulous and record interactions w/ scope.dev.arthur.ai
summary: Integrated Meticulous session recording library to capture user interactions on the development instance (scope.dev.arthur.ai). The integration includes conditional initialization that only activates on the specified development hostname, allowing the team to record and analyze user behavior for quality assurance and debugging purposes.
userImpact: User interactions are now recorded on the development instance, enabling the team to replay sessions and identify issues during testing.
author: zach.fry

title: NO-REF: QA fixes (UP-1389, UP-1366, UP-1371, UP-1457)
summary: Fixed QA issues across management table wrappers and components including corrected failed job text color in JobStatus and alignment improvements in role-based management tables. These changes address multiple related QA tickets (UP-1389, UP-1366, UP-1371, UP-1457) targeting display and styling inconsistencies in the UI.
userImpact: Users will see correct visual styling for failed job statuses and consistent table layouts across organization, project, workspace, and role management interfaces.
author: sergai

title: UP-1458: Hide nav bar for org level
summary: The navigation bar is now hidden when viewing organization-level pages. Changes were made to the layout components and drawer content to conditionally hide the nav bar based on the current context, with corresponding test updates to verify the new behavior.
userImpact: Users navigating to organization-level views will no longer see the navigation bar, providing a cleaner interface for org-level operations.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1496] - Chart changes
summary: Improved chart initialization by making it start at the earliest available timestamp from either query results or selected timerange. Fixed a boundary display issue when items don't cross the threshold, and updated the default time range to start 2 weeks back and end 4 hours ahead (rounded to hour start).
userImpact: Charts now display more intelligently aligned to data availability, boundary thresholds render correctly in all cases, and the default timespan better captures recent performance trends.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1300] [FE] Date/ time selectors behaviour not behaving as expected - QA FIX
summary: Fixed the default `from` value in the date/time selector modal to correctly default to the last updated timestamp. Also corrected the `publish-ui` CI job trigger to properly run when changes are made to the packages/ui directory.
userImpact: Date/time selectors in the models view now initialize with the correct default value, resolving unexpected behavior during QA testing.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1445: Workpace loader for webhooks routes
summary: Added workspace loader for webhook routes to improve route initialization and data availability. The change refactors AppRoutes.tsx to implement proper workspace loading during webhook route navigation and expands test coverage across multiple webhook and workspace-related E2E tests.
userImpact: Users will experience more reliable webhook route navigation with proper workspace context loading, ensuring data is available when accessing webhook-related features.
author: sergai

title: Release readiness QA tweaks
summary: Improved error messaging and UI labels across group/role assignment workflows, job logs, and webhooks. Updated localization strings for onboarding confirmations and error handling to provide clearer feedback when assigning groups or roles after resource creation, and refined variable breadcrumbs in job logs for better navigation.
userImpact: Users receive clearer error messages and improved UI labels when assigning groups or roles to newly created resources, and job logs now display variable breadcrumbs for easier navigation.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1315] - Model specific activity logs
summary: Added activity logs as a dedicated tab in the model view, allowing users to track model-specific operations and job history. The implementation includes updates to the model view component, activity log table wrapper, and internationalization strings to support the new tab interface.
userImpact: Users can now view activity logs directly within a model's details page, providing visibility into model-specific operations and job history.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1407: Create workspace button
summary: Added a "Create workspace" button to the application interface, implementing workspace creation functionality in the newWorkspaceModal component. This change enables users to initiate the workspace creation flow directly from the UI.
userImpact: Users can now create new workspaces using the dedicated button in the application interface.
author: sergai

title: UP-1414: Disable workspace creating button
summary: The workspace creation button has been disabled in the UI to prevent users from creating new workspaces. This change removes the ability to initiate workspace creation through the subheader and workspace selector components.
userImpact: Users can no longer create new workspaces through the workspace creation button.
author: sergai

title: UP-1420: Data source: hide sensitve keys on confirmation screen
summary: Sensitive API keys and credentials are now masked on data source confirmation screens during the onboarding flow. This prevents accidental exposure of sensitive information in screenshots or on-screen visibility, improving the security posture of the setup process.
userImpact: Users can now safely share screenshots or screen-record the data source onboarding flow without risk of exposing sensitive API keys or authentication credentials.
author: sergai

title: UP-1426: Workspace overview with dropdown menu
summary: Added a workspace dropdown menu to the layout, allowing users to quickly switch between workspaces from the drawer. A new workspacesMenu component was created and integrated into the drawer content, with the Menu component enhanced to support this new interaction pattern.
userImpact: Users can now view and switch between workspaces using a dropdown menu in the drawer navigation.
author: sergai

title: fix: do not copy Dockerfiles into image so dockerfile changes do not trigger a...
summary: This MR improves Docker build efficiency and nginx configuration validation by excluding Dockerfiles from the image build context, adding an nginx config validation check at build time, and configuring nginx to listen on two ports (80 and 3023) to support both AWS and JD environments. These changes prevent unnecessary rebuilds when Dockerfile changes are made during development and catch nginx configuration errors early in the build process.
userImpact: Developers experience faster local builds when modifying Dockerfiles, and invalid nginx configurations are caught at image build time rather than at runtime.
author: jdb-arthur

title: feat: add a listener on 3023 to match aws sg configuration and allow...
summary: Added an nginx listener on port 3023 to align with AWS CloudFront security group configuration and enable health checks from the load balancer. This change updates both the Dockerfile.prod and nginx.conf to support the new listener port.
userImpact: Load balancer health checks can now successfully reach the application through the new port 3023, ensuring proper load balancer integration with AWS CloudFront security group rules.
author: jdb-arthur

title: Release readiness 2
summary: This release includes three critical fixes: resolving infinite loading states when refocusing on paginated tables, improving error handling in new user creation flows, and enhancing webhook test error messaging with better user-facing feedback. These changes address usability issues and provide clearer error feedback to users encountering common operations.
userImpact: Users will no longer experience infinite loading when returning to alert tables, receive better error messages when creating new users or testing webhooks, and see clearer guidance when operations fail.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: NO-REF: Add node options var bumping heap space
summary: Builds were failing with JavaScript heap out of memory errors during the unify-app build process. Added NODE_OPTIONS environment variable configuration to increase heap space allocation in the CI pipeline, preventing memory exhaustion during build compilation.
userImpact: Build pipeline now completes successfully without heap memory errors.
author: peter496

title: Release readiness part 1
summary: This release readiness PR includes UI refinements, bug fixes, and component refactoring across the onboarding flow, data sources, and job logging. Key changes include replacing datasets translation with tasks, sorting job logs, adding character limits to user/group creation fields, and fixing visual issues with button states and modal scaling on smaller devices.
userImpact: Users experience improved UI consistency, better handling of long text inputs with character limits, corrected button states in onboarding flows, and more appropriate field labels in data source configurations.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1379 - Activity log UX improvements
summary: Improved activity log UI with several refinements: job type filters now map pretty strings to enum values and support multiselect, jobs table columns have been reorganized to hide contextually unnecessary fields, and Scheduled Jobs job types are now hidden from all tables. The scheduled jobs view was also updated to filter specifically for metrics type jobs with ready_after=now() to prevent internal implementation details from appearing to users.
userImpact: Users see a cleaner activity log interface with more intuitive job type filtering, hidden Scheduled Jobs implementation details, and better-organized table columns that reduce visual clutter.
author: peter496

title: Up 1344 alerts updates
summary: Comprehensive updates to alerts functionality including improved table loading with infinite scroll via the useApiInfinite hook, enhanced AlertDetails page with trigger point visualization, and finishing the alert rule edit view. Also includes refinements to webhook validation, performance graph rendering, and code editor improvements for better user experience.
userImpact: Users can now navigate large alert datasets more efficiently with infinite scroll, view alert trigger points in detail, edit alert rules with a complete interface, and benefit from improved performance graphs and webhook validation.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1425] [FE] Create Shared Package for Components
summary: Created a new shared UI package within the monorepo to enable component reuse across applications. The package includes a manual publishing process, shared TypeScript configuration, and unit testing capabilities for components. The implementation also separates reusable dependencies like types and typescript-config into independent packages.
userImpact: Developers can now create and reuse UI components, types, and configurations across multiple applications within the monorepo, enabling faster development and consistency.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1300] [FE] Date/ time selectors behaviour not behaving as expected
summary: Fixed data refresh modal inputs becoming nullable after submission and corrected Last Updated At timestamp not being updated when the modal reopens. The modal now rerenders on open to retrieve fresh date values and provides default values to ensure inputs remain non-null.
userImpact: Users can now rely on the Last Updated At field updating correctly and the date/time selector inputs remaining populated after submitting the data refresh modal.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1413] [FE] Handle password policy errors on frontend
summary: Added a new utility function to extract password policy error descriptions from backend error strings on the frontend. This enables proper error messaging in the user creation flow when password policy violations occur, with plans to refactor to use a structured object format once backend changes are complete.
userImpact: Users now receive clear, extracted error messages when password policy requirements are not met during user creation.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: Up 1344 alerts updates
summary: Comprehensive refactoring and enhancement of the alerts system including reorganization of alert rule components and pages, addition of webhooks to the navigation, improvements to the AlertDetails page with trigger point marking, and enhancements to the performance graph with better x-axis time range reflection. The changes also include moving alert rules pages under the models/:id hierarchy and enabling revalidation for the alerts table.
userImpact: Users can now see trigger points marked in AlertDetails, access webhooks from the navbar, benefit from improved alert table performance with revalidation, and navigate alert rules under their respective models with an enhanced performance graph visualization.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1384: Nav bar tests
summary: Added comprehensive Cypress e2e tests for the navigation bar drawer component, including tests for menu selectors, submenu text, and drawer open/close interactions. Minor adjustments were made to appBar, layout, and Menu components to support test infrastructure.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1319 update time options
summary: Updated time range shortcuts in the Model Refresh Modal to match the options specified in UP-1319. The changes include modifications to the helper functions that generate time options and corresponding UI label updates.
userImpact: Users now have access to updated time range options when refreshing models in the modal.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1164 Pin actions column
summary: The actions column in the Group Table is now pinned to remain visible when scrolling horizontally and has been made non-resizable. This improves usability by keeping critical action controls always accessible without horizontal scrolling.
userImpact: Users can now keep the actions column visible while scrolling through group table data horizontally, and cannot accidentally resize it.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1378 Errors when creating users
summary: Fixed error handling during user creation to properly surface backend error messages to users. The system now displays a specific message when a user already exists (409 error) and exposes validation error messages when password requirements are not met (400 error).
userImpact: Users creating new accounts now receive clear, actionable error messages when creation fails due to duplicate users or password validation issues.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1252 single select for tasks table
summary: Updated the tasks table to enforce single-row selection mode. Users can now select only one row at a time in the tasks/datasets table, improving clarity and preventing multi-select confusion.
userImpact: Users can now select only one row in the tasks/datasets table at a time, providing a clearer and more constrained selection experience.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1242 design changes
summary: Improved the alert performance chart visualization by adding a missing axis label and formatting axis ticks for better readability. These design changes enhance the clarity and professionalism of the alert performance reporting interface.
userImpact: Users can now see a properly labeled and formatted alert performance chart with clearer axis information.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1127, 1129, 1130, 1336] - Webhooks table and edit states, webhook creation bugfixes
summary: Added webhooks table view with the ability to create, edit, and delete webhooks. Implemented webhook editing functionality including name editing and loading states, fixed navigation issues in webhook creation flow, and replaced useAPI with useApiInfinite hook for improved data handling.
userImpact: Users can now view all webhooks in a table, create new webhooks, edit webhook names and configuration, and manage webhooks through a dedicated interface.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Update jitesoft/node-yarn Docker tag to v21
summary: Updated the jitesoft/node-yarn Docker base image from v20 to v21, bringing the latest Node.js and Yarn tooling to the build environment. This is a major version bump that ensures the application uses current stable versions of Node.js and package management tools.
userImpact: Build environment now uses Node.js v21 with corresponding Yarn version for development and deployment.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update dependency react-i18next to v15
summary: Updated react-i18next from v14 to v15, bringing support for React 19 and modern JavaScript features. This major version upgrade includes fixes for Trans component warnings in React 19, improved Trans handling with alwaysFormat, and support for optional chaining and nullish coalescing operators. The update requires i18next > v19.4.5.
userImpact: Users will benefit from React 19 compatibility and reduced console warnings when using the Trans component, along with improved internationalization handling.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1257 Tasks table
summary: Enhanced the tasks/available datasets table with server-side pagination, filtering by name, and sorting capabilities. These features improve usability when browsing large lists of available datasets in the onboarding workflow.
userImpact: Users can now navigate large dataset lists more efficiently with pagination controls, search by dataset name, and sort results alphabetically.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: NO-REF: Remove overwrite of docker credentials
summary: Removed duplicate docker-start step execution that was overwriting ECR credentials. The step was being run both in docker-build-and-publish-main and docker-build-and-publish-template, causing the credentials generated in the first run to be lost.
userImpact: Docker builds and ECR publishing now work correctly without credential loss between pipeline stages.
author: peter496

title: NO-REF: Add docker auth to docker steps
summary: Added Docker authentication credentials to Docker build steps in the GitLab CI configuration. This ensures Docker commands can properly authenticate when pulling or pushing images from private registries during the CI/CD pipeline.
userImpact: CI/CD pipelines can now successfully authenticate with Docker registries, preventing authentication failures during image operations.
author: peter496

title: UP-1269: Slide in drawer content
summary: Implemented a slide-in drawer component for displaying drawer content in the application layout. The drawer is now rendered as a separate mounted component with proper overflow handling, and the main layout has been refactored to support this new UI pattern.
userImpact: Users can now interact with a slide-in drawer interface for navigation and menu items, improving the space efficiency and UX of the application layout.
author: sergai

title: Update dependency jsdom to v25
summary: Updated jsdom from v24 to v25. The major version change updates the prototype chain of EventTarget to point to jsdom's internal Object.prototype instead of Node.js Object.prototype, which only impacts code using non-default runScripts settings.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Development environments using jsdom for testing may see changes in instanceof behavior when using dangerously or outside-only runScripts modes.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update cypress/base Docker tag to v22
summary: Updates the cypress/base Docker image from version 20.18.0 to 22.7.0 in the GitLab CI configuration. This major version upgrade brings the latest Node.js runtime and dependencies to the CI/CD pipeline, improving test environment compatibility and performance.
userImpact: CI/CD pipeline now runs Cypress tests against Node.js v22 base environment, ensuring compatibility with newer runtime features and dependencies.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: Update all non-major dependencies
summary: This MR applies routine non-major dependency updates across the project, including minor and patch updates to core libraries such as @emotion/react, @mui components, @codemirror, ESLint tooling, and various font packages. The updates maintain compatibility while bringing in the latest improvements and security patches from upstream dependencies.
userImpact: No direct user impact. Developers benefit from improved tooling and latest compatible dependency versions.
author: group_3720083_bot_bf89c4e378f22b6cb8b2f93b06d47dae

title: UP-1081 renovate bot changes
summary: Updated renovate bot configuration to group minor and patch dependency updates into single MRs, enable auto-merging for the renovate bot user, assign FE team members for awareness, and remove rate limits to ensure all dependencies are updated regularly. This improves the dependency update workflow by reducing merge conflicts and streamlining the review process.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: ianmcgraw

title: [UP-1338] [FE] Create a seconds to proper unit parser for scheduled jobs
summary: Added a seconds-to-proper-unit parser for scheduled jobs in form editing state. This enhancement ensures that time values are displayed in the most readable unit (e.g., minutes, hours) rather than raw seconds when editing data refresh and existing schedule forms.
userImpact: Users now see scheduled job intervals displayed in appropriate time units instead of raw seconds, making form values more readable and intuitive.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1268 Multi Drop down menu
summary: Implemented a new multi-level dropdown menu component for the navigation bar, allowing users to navigate through nested menu hierarchies. This includes a reusable Menu component with support for submenu icons, tooltips, and dynamic styling, integrated into the project and workspace selectors.
userImpact: Users can now navigate through multi-level dropdown menus in the new navigation bar with improved organization of projects and workspaces.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1332] [FE] Update onboarding flow to create model before confirmation step so that we can reuse it's details for parts of the onboarding
summary: The onboarding flow has been restructured to create the model during the tasks step rather than the confirmation step. This enables the data refresh step to reuse model details during the onboarding process, improving the overall user experience and reducing redundant operations.
userImpact: Users can now see model details populated throughout the onboarding flow, and the data refresh configuration can be completed with full context from the newly created model.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1140] [FE] Add job log attempt # to API call
summary: The job page now supports multiple job attempts, automatically selecting the first attempt by default and allowing users to switch between them via the API. This change enables better job tracking and debugging when jobs have multiple execution attempts.
userImpact: Users can now view and navigate between different attempts of a job directly in the job log page.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1242 Alert Rule Metric Name
summary: Added the ability to specify a custom metric name when creating an alert rule. The custom metric name is now displayed in both the alert rule details page and alert details page, providing clearer context about which metric the alert is monitoring.
userImpact: Users can now assign custom metric names to alert rules, making it easier to identify and understand which specific metrics are being monitored across alert rules and alert instances.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1254 New Navigation bar
summary: A new navigation bar replaces the previous sidebar, featuring a slide-in drawer content, multi-level dropdown menus, and an updated theme. This consolidates approved changes from three feature branches into a cohesive navigation system with improved UX for project and workspace selection.
userImpact: Users now have a new navigation bar with enhanced menu interactions, slide-in drawer content, and reorganized project navigation patterns.
author: sergai

title: UP-1267 New theme
summary: Introduces a new theme and layout to support the upcoming navigation bar redesign. This MR replaces the previous sidebar and accordion-based navigation with new app bar and sub-header components, updates spacing and styling throughout the UI, and modifies related utility functions and translations. The changes are being merged to an integration branch for comprehensive testing before final integration into the main branch.
userImpact: Users will experience a redesigned navigation interface with a new app bar, updated layout structure, and improved visual spacing as part of the upcoming navigation bar rollout.
author: sergai

title: UP-1266 date format issues
summary: Fixed date format display to use 12-hour format with AM/PM instead of 24-hour format, and added missing translation string for "updated at" label. This improves date readability for users in regions that prefer 12-hour time notation.
userImpact: Users now see dates and times displayed in 12-hour format with proper AM/PM indicators, and the "updated at" label is correctly translated in the UI.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: NO-REF: granulate workspaces tests
summary: Test improvements for workspace functionality. Modified cypress e2e tests for workspaces to ensure better test coverage and reliability.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: [UP-1106, UP-1255, UP-1259, UP-1260] - Alert rules testing functionality, QA fixes
summary: Fixed multiple issues in alert rule testing functionality including table height and revalidation problems, removed client-side sorting to rely on API ordering, and fixed threshold value persistence. Added line number display to CodeEditor and a performance graph visualization to the metrics modal.
userImpact: Users can now properly test alert rules with correctly formatted tables, accurate threshold values, and improved visibility through the new alert performance graph in the metrics modal.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1119: useSWRInfinite
summary: Introduces useApiInfinite hook for handling paginated API requests using SWR's useSWRInfinite. The hook automatically manages pagination state, flattens response data, and provides configuration defaults (revalidateFirstPage=false) optimized for table-based data that doesn't change frequently. Includes unit tests, TypeScript typing, and integration with existing components like AlertsTableWrapper and OrgPermissionsTableWrapper.
userImpact: Users can now implement infinite pagination and lazy-loaded tables using the useApiInfinite hook, which simplifies managing large datasets with automatic page management and request batching.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1128] - Add a new webhook flow
summary: Added a complete new webhook creation flow with multi-step setup, including name configuration, webhook client integration, header management, and review capabilities. The implementation includes new reusable components for secret fields, webhook headers display/editing, and localization support for the webhook workflow.
userImpact: Users can now create and configure webhooks through a guided multi-step interface, with support for custom headers and secure secret field handling.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: NO-REF: Route not authorized tests
summary: Added test coverage for route authorization scenarios across project, workspace, and model E2E tests. These tests validate that unauthorized access to routes is properly blocked, improving test reliability and coverage for authentication flows.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1190: Name field editable
summary: Implemented editable name fields across alert rules, models, groups, and projects using a new reusable TextFieldEditable component. Users can now double-click name fields to enter edit mode and modify the names directly in the UI.
userImpact: Users can now edit name fields by double-clicking them on alert rules, models, organization groups, and projects.
author: sergai

title: [UP-832] [FE] Adjust all CRON Inputs once we will have ready backend implementation and connect form to BE
summary: Implemented CRON-based scheduling UI for data refresh jobs across model pages and activity logs. Created reusable wrapper components and forms to manage job schedules (create, edit, remove), with support for converting loopback intervals to CRON expressions and displaying scheduled jobs. Updated form validation, translations, and e2e tests to support the full scheduling workflow.
userImpact: Users can now create, manage, and remove scheduled data refresh jobs using CRON expressions on model pages and activity logs, with real-time job status visibility.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: NO-REF: Stabilizing route not authorized tests
summary: Stabilized route authorization tests in the E2E test suite by fixing flaky test assertions across project, workspace, and model test files. These changes improve test reliability and reduce false negatives in the authorization verification tests.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1203 Connector step
summary: Fixed the Connect to Arthur Shield flow to automatically advance to the next step upon successful connection instead of remaining on the connection step. This eliminates the unnecessary requirement for users to manually trigger the next step by clicking the button again.
userImpact: Users now automatically progress to the next onboarding step after successfully connecting to Arthur Shield, eliminating duplicate button clicks.
author: sergai

title: UP-1202: Modal container align
summary: Fixed alignment issues in modal containers across the application by adjusting ModalContainer and updating related modal components. Changes standardize modal positioning and layout consistency across multiple dialog implementations including workspace, role binding, project, group, and user modals.
userImpact: Modals throughout the application now display with consistent and correct alignment.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1109] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - default
summary: Updated translation label in the Individual Alert Rule Page from "Updated" to "Last Modified" for improved clarity. This change applies to both the i18n translation file and the alertRule component view.
userImpact: Users will now see "Last Modified" instead of "Updated" when viewing alert rule details, providing clearer terminology for when rules were last changed.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1246: Fixes for JobsV1 endpoint types incompatibility
summary: Fixed JobsV1 endpoint type incompatibility caused by the removal of `total_records` field from the get job API. Updated JobsTable and related wrapper components to handle pagination and data display without reliance on the removed field, and removed the now-incompatible useJobs hook.
userImpact: Jobs table now displays correctly and pagination works as expected following the API changes in UP-1035.
author: sergai

title: UP-1098: Alert performance graph
summary: Refactored and improved the alert performance graph component with enhanced chart filtering, configuration, and data visualization. The changes include a new AlertPerformanceGraph wrapper component, improved tooltip formatting with x-axis tick formatting, and new UI elements like HeaderItem and ValidateCheck icon components to better display alert performance metrics.
userImpact: Users can now view alert performance metrics with improved graph filtering, better formatted data visualization, and a more polished UI for alert performance analysis.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF - Move jobs to EKS runners
summary: Frontend build jobs are now configured to run on internal EKS runners instead of shared GitLab runners. This change reduces GitLab compute minute consumption while maintaining build performance on internal infrastructure.
userImpact: Developers will see frontend builds run on internal EKS runners, reducing consumption of shared GitLab compute minutes.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1142] Use swr instead of a local hook for fetching jobs
summary: Replaced a local hook with SWR for fetching jobs to improve reliability and eliminate bugs. Updated the OpenAPI specification and modified the JobsTableWrapper component to use the new fetching mechanism with a new filter for activity logs.
userImpact: Users benefit from more reliable job fetching with fewer bugs, and a new filter option is now available in the activity logs table.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1112] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - delete alert rule
summary: Added the ability to delete alert rules from the Individual Alert Rule Page. The implementation includes a delete modal confirmation, user-friendly snackbar notifications, and comprehensive test coverage for the deletion workflow.
userImpact: Users can now delete alert rules directly from the alert rule detail page with confirmation and feedback messages.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1099: Alert dimensions
summary: Implemented display of alert dimensions object in the alert details view. A new AlertDimensionsTableWrapper component renders alert dimensions in a table format with proper internationalization support.
userImpact: Users can now view the dimensions associated with each alert in the alert details panel.
author: sergai

title: UP-991 Add Dataset
summary: Added functionality to create new datasets directly from an existing connector via a modal dialog. The modal enables users to both add the dataset and create its corresponding model in a single workflow, streamlining the data onboarding process.
userImpact: Users can now add new datasets to existing connectors and automatically create associated models through a dedicated modal interface.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: Shadcn shared package
summary: Extracted shadcn UI components and shared configuration into a dedicated package within the monorepo. This involved moving components (card, chart), utilities, styles, and build configurations (tailwind, postcss, tsconfig) into `packages/shadcn`, while updating the main app to reference the new shared package instead of duplicating configuration.
userImpact: Component and configuration code is now centralized in a shared package, reducing duplication and making it easier to maintain consistent UI components and styling across the application.
author: sergai

title: fix: [UP-947] - design inconsistencies
summary: Fixed multiple UI design inconsistencies in the user management flow: standardized the heading "Add a User into Organization" capitalization, applied consistent IBM Plex Mono font family to form inputs and text, and added a back navigation button matching the design system used elsewhere in Identity & Access.
userImpact: Users now see consistent typography and capitalization in the organization user management interface, with improved navigation to return to the previous screen.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1101, UP-1102] - Manage alert rules table, design tweaks, alert testing mocked
summary: Added a new alert rules management table with full CRUD capabilities, design refinements across components, and a metrics modal with mocked test data. This includes a new AlertRulesTableWrapper component with toolbar actions, theme shadow enhancements, and new alert rules views at both model and global levels.
userImpact: Users can now view, manage, and configure alert rules in a dedicated table interface with sorting, filtering, and a metrics testing modal with mocked alert data.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-495 maintain state in onboarding flow
summary: Fixed an issue where navigating back to previous steps in the onboarding flow would lose all previously entered data. The form inputs and selections are now properly persisted across step navigation, allowing users to review and edit their entries when returning to earlier steps.
userImpact: Users can now navigate backwards through the onboarding flow and see their previously entered data intact, making it possible to review and edit information without losing progress.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1195 - Fix job filter change
summary: Fixed job filter query parameter from `kind` to `kinds` in the JobsTable columns component. The API endpoint for retrieving jobs now expects the query parameter name `kinds` instead of `kind`, and this change ensures the frontend correctly sends the filter parameter to match the backend contract.
userImpact: Job filtering now works correctly when users apply kind filters in the jobs table.
author: peter496

title: [UP-1102] - New alert rule flow
summary: Implemented new alert rule creation flow at `/models/{modelId}/alert-rules/new` with a multi-step wizard. The flow includes an information step for basic details and a configure step with SQL query editing via CodeMirror and AST-based column validation. This unblocks dependent tasks and establishes the foundation for alert rule testing and creation UI.
userImpact: Users can now create new alert rules through a structured wizard interface, selecting models and writing SQL queries with built-in validation for referenced columns.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: Downgrade swagger-typescript-api
summary: Fixed a build-time error "TypeError: e.commands is not iterable" in swagger-typescript-api version 13.0.19 by downgrading the dependency to 13.0.16. This restores the ability to successfully generate TypeScript types from Swagger definitions during the build process.
userImpact: Users can now successfully build and generate TypeScript types from Swagger API definitions without encountering the commands iteration error.
author: sergai

title: [UP-1109] [FE] Setup of Individual Alert Rule Page - default
summary: Added a new Individual Alert Rule detail page allowing users to view alert rule configurations, history, and details. The page includes an alert rule form component, alerts history table with filtering toolbar, and alert details display with proper date formatting and routing.
userImpact: Users can now view individual alert rule details at a dedicated page with full alert history, configuration information, and filtering capabilities.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-963] [FE] Setup of Identity & Access Page : Org Access Default Page Delete - QA Fixes
summary: Fixed translation issues found during QA testing for the Identity & Access Page organization access default page functionality. Updates to English translation strings and component wrapper localization ensure consistent user-facing text.
userImpact: Users will now see correctly translated and consistent text throughout the Identity & Access Page organization access interface.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1137 - Migrate job creation endpoint to batch equivalent
summary: Migrated job creation endpoint from individual job requests to use the batch-equivalent API. Updated all job creation hooks (useAddJob, useJob, useListDatasets, useOnboardModel, useTestConnector) and their corresponding test fixtures to align with the new batch endpoint pattern. This change improves efficiency by reducing redundant API calls.
userImpact: Job creation operations now use a more efficient batch API endpoint, with no visible change to user workflows.
author: peter496

title: UP-1122 Inference Deep Dive Table
summary: Added a new "Inference Deep Dive" tab to the model page with a detailed inference table, row expansion, side panel details, and result visualization components. The implementation is ported from the shield repository and currently uses mocked data as the backend API is not yet ready; the tab is disabled pending full backend integration.
userImpact: Users will gain visibility into detailed inference data with filtering, sorting, and expandable row details when the Inference Deep Dive tab is enabled and the backend API is implemented.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1012] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Individual Role View
summary: Fixed QA testing issues in the Organization Management Identity & Access Individual Role View feature. Changes include minor fixes to the CustomTabPanel and RolesTable components to resolve identified defects discovered during testing.
userImpact: Users will experience improved stability and correctness in the Organization Management role view interface.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-955] [FE] Workspace Management :  redirect to specific workspace role
summary: Users can now click on a role in workspace and user management interfaces to navigate to a dedicated single role view. This change enhances navigation across all user management and organization management screens by making roles directly clickable.
userImpact: Users can now click on workspace roles in management tables to view detailed information for a single role.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1082, UP-1097: Alert details overview + beautiful charts example usage
summary: Implemented alert details overview page with integrated Beautiful charts library components. This MR adds a new dedicated view for individual alerts, establishes a reusable shadcn-ui package for chart components, and introduces the necessary routing and loader infrastructure to support detailed alert inspection.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to and view detailed information about individual alerts, including support for rich chart visualizations powered by the Beautiful charts library.
author: sergai

title: [UP-963] [FE] Setup of Identity & Access Page : Org Access Default Page Delete
summary: Refactored Identity & Access page to consolidate role binding management logic and improve error handling. Created a shared utility function for handling permission errors when removing role bindings, reducing code duplication across org, project, and workspace user management components. Added comprehensive test coverage for missing permission scenarios.
userImpact: Users now see consistent, properly localized error messages when they lack permissions to remove role bindings across all access management levels.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1062: Using StyledGrid for groups selection
summary: Refactored the GroupsSelectionTable component to use StyledGrid for improved consistency and maintainability. This change simplifies the component implementation by leveraging the shared StyledGrid component rather than custom styling.
userImpact: No direct user impact. The groups selection UI maintains the same visual appearance and functionality with cleaner, more maintainable code.
author: sergai

title: UP-1009 keep users selected
summary: Added a prop to UsersSelectionTable to preserve user selections across pagination. This prevents the loss of selected users when navigating between pages in the user selection interface.
userImpact: Users can now maintain their selections when navigating between pages in the user selection table without having selections cleared.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1050] - New workspace flow
summary: Introduced a new workspace creation flow with a modal dialog that allows users to create workspaces directly from the workspace selector. The feature includes a new modal component, updated workspace selector UI, internationalization strings, and comprehensive e2e tests.
userImpact: Users can now create new workspaces using a dedicated modal interface accessible from the workspace selector.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: UP-1086: User roles per organization
summary: Implemented the ability to manage user roles on a per-organization basis. Users can now be assigned different roles within individual organizations through a new role binding modal and wrapper component that extends the existing role management system.
userImpact: Users can now assign and manage organization-specific roles for individual team members within their organizations.
author: sergai

title: UP-1131: Keep previous data for paginated table
summary: Implemented keepPreviousData flag across all paginated table queries to retain previously fetched data while new data is being loaded. This prevents the display from clearing when users navigate between pages, providing a smoother pagination experience without the flash of undefined data.
userImpact: Users navigating between paginated tables no longer experience a blank state while the next page loads, improving perceived performance and user experience.
author: sergai

title: NO-REF: Stabilize home tests
summary: This MR stabilizes flaky end-to-end tests in the home and workspace test suites by adjusting test conditions and timing. The changes address intermittent test failures to improve CI reliability and test consistency.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: UP-1071 navigation items
summary: Refactored the ListItem component and consolidated navigation item logic by removing the duplicate istItem component. Fixed an issue where selected items were not displaying correctly, and standardized sizing across workspace and project drawers (14px outer, 12px inner).
userImpact: Navigation items in the sidebar now display correctly when selected, with consistent sizing across different drawer contexts.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: [UP-1012] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Individual Role View
summary: Added a new individual role view page in the organization management interface, allowing users to inspect role details including permissions and basic information. The implementation includes two new table components (BasicRolesTable and RolePermissionsTable) and corresponding API endpoints, along with comprehensive end-to-end test coverage.
userImpact: Users can now navigate to a dedicated role detail page to view role permissions and properties within the organization management section.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1092 Hide privacy toggle
summary: The privacy toggle in the "create project" modal has been hidden from the UI until its underlying functionality is fully implemented. The code has been commented out rather than deleted to avoid rewriting it during future development.
userImpact: Users will no longer see the privacy toggle option in the create project modal, reducing UI confusion around incomplete features.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1094 Alert Check Job Name
summary: Added "alert_check" job name to the English translation file. This ensures the alert check job type is properly localized and displayed to users in the UI.
userImpact: Users will now see the correct display name for alert check jobs in the application interface.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-1059: Alerts history page
summary: Added a new alerts history page with filtering and time range selection capabilities. Users can now view alerts with customizable date-time range filters and clear applied filters. The implementation includes a new toolbar with time range selector, enhanced parsing of filter parameters, and improved infinite loading behavior.
userImpact: Users can now access a dedicated alerts history page where they can filter alerts by custom date-time ranges and manage filter states.
author: sergai

title: UP-1093: Sidebar navigation
summary: Fixed navigation sidebar behavior in the layout component. Introduced a new useSideBar hook to manage sidebar state and updated the sidebar and layout components to use the new hook. E2E tests were added to ensure the sidebar navigation works correctly across the project, model, and workspace sections.
userImpact: Users can now reliably navigate using the sidebar without encountering previous navigation issues.
author: sergai

title: [Up-950][FE] - Create user summary
summary: Added user creation flow with summary and IAM assignment capabilities, including new RowSelectTable component for group and role selection, user information summary step, and user creation logic with snackbar feedback. The implementation includes role preview drawer with permissions display and requires newly created users to be assigned to at least one group or role.
userImpact: Users can now create new users in the organization management interface, assign them to groups and roles with visual previews, and receive feedback on the creation status.
author: marcin.szczepaniak

title: [UP-1013] [FE] Org Mgmt Identity & Access:  Default Permissions tab
summary: Added a new Permissions tab to the Organization Management Identity & Access module with infinite scroll table support. The implementation includes a new PermissionsTable component, table wrapper with custom columns, and comprehensive e2e tests and fixtures for permissions data fetching and filtering.
userImpact: Users can now view and manage organization permissions through a new Permissions tab in the IAM settings, with infinite scroll support for large permission datasets.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: OpenAPI update
summary: Updated the OpenAPI specification file and fixed related end-to-end tests across multiple Cypress test suites. The changes ensure that tests remain aligned with the latest API schema definitions.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: sergai

title: Rename JobStatusSnackbar to StyledSnackbar
summary: Renamed the JobStatusSnackbar component to StyledSnackbar to better reflect its general-purpose styling nature rather than job-specific functionality. This refactor updated all imports and usages across the codebase including multiple wrapper components and views.
userImpact: No direct user impact — this is an internal refactoring that improves code clarity without changing functionality.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP 975 model page link
summary: Added a link from job detail pages back to the associated model page for jobs that are linked to a specific model. This improvement enhances navigation and allows users to quickly access the model details from a job context.
userImpact: Users can now navigate directly from a job page to its associated model page via a new link.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-760: Data sources views refactor
summary: Refactored data sources views to replace legacy hooks with the new `useAPI` hook, eliminating redundant re-renders and duplicate API requests. The refactor splits components into smaller, more focused units and updates the data loading logic throughout the data sources feature.
userImpact: Users will experience improved performance with faster page loads and reduced API traffic when viewing and managing data sources.
author: sergai

title: Replace data grid with prop version
summary: Replaced the standard `DataGrid` component with the more feature-rich `DataGridPro` variant across the application. This consolidation removes the separate `StyledDataGridPro` component by merging its functionality into the main `StyledDataGrid` wrapper, simplifying the codebase while gaining access to advanced data grid capabilities.
userImpact: Users now have access to enhanced data grid features such as advanced filtering, column customization, and improved performance across all table views in the application.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-990 Update Job View
summary: Updated the Job View page layout and styling to prevent error messages from being cut off. Adjusted component positioning, widths, and error log display styles across JobData, MetricsJobErrors, and activity log views.
userImpact: Error messages on the job page are now fully visible and no longer truncated.
author: vivienmarcolin

title: UP-942: Keycloak initialization
summary: Keycloak initialization now sources backend URL and Keycloak URL dynamically from the backend's OIDC metadata API in production builds, eliminating hardcoded environment configuration. Local development continues to use `.env` + `.env.development` configs, improving deployment flexibility and reducing configuration friction.
userImpact: Production deployments can now automatically discover Keycloak and backend URLs from the environment, enabling easier multi-environment deployment without manual configuration changes.
author: sergai

title: Bring back lint
summary: Re-enabled the lint step in the CI/CD pipeline that had been previously disabled. This ensures code quality checks are enforced again during the build process.
userImpact: No direct user impact, but developers will now have linting validation enforced in CI/CD again.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: Project Cleanup
summary: Reorganized project structure with modules directory cleanup, converting file naming to camelCase and consolidating module layouts. Removed deprecated onboarding, bench, chat, and scope module pages while updating routing configuration to reflect new structure.
userImpact: No direct user impact — internal project structure reorganization to improve code maintainability.
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: [UP-1015] [FE] Project User Management : Default Page Add Assignment
summary: Added comprehensive end-to-end tests for the Project User Management feature, covering user and group role binding operations. Test fixtures were added to mock authorization endpoints and role configurations, enabling automated validation of the default assignment page functionality.
userImpact: No direct user impact
author: kapotyrala-arthur

title: UP-1009 Adding users from multiple pages to a group
summary: Added the "keepNonExistentRowsSelected" prop to the DataGrid component in the GroupsSelectionTable to persist user selections across pagination. This leverages built-in Material-UI functionality to improve the user experience when adding multiple users from different pages to a group.
userImpact: Users can now select users from multiple pages and their selections will persist when navigating between pages, making it easier to add multiple users to a group.
author: vivienmarcolin
